Zh: “你帮我拿来的场外拷贝很有帮助，不过……”她话到中途就停了下来。“你知道吗，如果你不要说话，让我继续工作的话，那就太好了。”

En: *Great* job!
Zh: 干得*漂亮*！

En: She's beaming -- you can feel it in your heart.
Zh: 她喜气洋洋的——你能打心底感觉到。

En: Time for a celebratory drink perhaps...? Just a thought!
Zh: 也许是时候来一杯庆功酒了……？只是有个想法！

En: Great -- after we've done this, we can finally get to the island, the lieutenant says, satisfied.
Zh: “太棒了——等这边完工之后，我们总算是可以上岛了，”警督满意地说到。

En: Great -- after you've done this, you can finally get to the island. Let's wrap this up!
Zh: 太棒了——等你这边完工之后，总算是可以上岛了。快点结束它吧！

En: Death *is* scary. You can't just start over again. Or can you?
Zh: 死亡*是*很恐怖。你不能再从头来过。又或者，你可以呢？

En: Great -- you *do know* that we can just abandon this mission and go to the island? Let's close this case!
Zh: 很好——你*知道*我们可以放弃这个任务，直接上岛的吧？让我们了结这个案子吧！

En: Great -- and here I thought we were finally going to the island... the lieutenant murmurs to himself.
Zh: “太棒了——我还以为我们总算是可以上岛了……”警督自言自语到。

En: 'But I *am* interested,' you read from his face.
Zh: 但是我*很感兴趣*，你能从他的脸上看出来。

En: Cuno must be already on the island, wondering where the hell you are... or maybe he doesn't care. Yeah, he probably doesn't care.
Zh: 坤诺肯定已经在岛上了，正好奇你到底死哪去了……或者他可能并不在乎。是啊，他可能并不在乎。

En: Brilliant negotiating there, detective -- as always!
Zh: 这次谈判相当成功，警探——就像往常一样！

En: That's a bummer. A *big* bummer.
Zh: 真倒霉。倒*大*霉了。

En: Those ravers won't be bothering you anymore -- I told them to leave because they were planning a drug trade.
Zh: “那些派对狂再也不会打扰你了——因为他们计划贩毒，我让他们离开了。”

En: And here's my Kvalsund multitool. You might need it to hack loose some ice. It opens everything. If you get me the off-site copy, then you can keep the Kvalsund.
Zh: “这是我的库瓦桑德多用工具。你可能需要用它凿冰。它什么都能打开。如果你能把场外拷贝带回来，这个库瓦桑德就送给你了。”

En: The *legendary* Kvalsund multitool! This is prybar version 2.0 -- for *professionals*, like you.
Zh: *传奇的*库瓦桑德多用工具！这就是2.0版本的撬棍——给*专业人士*用的，比如说你。

En: This glorious multitool makes a regular prybar completely OBSOLETE.
Zh: 这件光辉耀眼的多用工具让一根普通的撬棍完全变成了废物。

En: There's a solution, but she doesn't want to hand it over to you yet. It's a *thing*, something she holds dear.
Zh: 有一种解决办法，但她还不想把它交给你。它是一件*东西*，她很珍视的东西。

En: It hurts a bit for her to say this -- she's not too happy to be parting with the Kvalsund.
Zh: 说出这句话让她有些痛心——她不太愿意跟自己的库瓦桑德分开。

En: If you'll please carry that amplifier, officer. She gestures to the lieutenant. "And if you'll take that spool of cable, we should be able to move it all in one trip..."
Zh: “请你把扩音器拿上，警官。”她向警督做了个手势。“如果你还能把那团电线拿上，我们应该就能一次搬完了……”

En: If you'll please carry that amplifier and cables, officer. We have quite a lot to carry...
Zh: “请你把扩音器和电线拿上，警官。我们有很多东西要搬……”

En: Okay. She lets out a relieved sigh. "I'll go make a phone call outside and then return for my things. You can tell the ravers that they can move in."
Zh: “好吧。”她松了一口气。“我去外面打个电话，然后回来取我的东西。你可以告诉派对狂，他们可以搬进来了。”

En: Okay, I'm going. You can tell the ravers that they can move in.
Zh: “好吧，我走了。你可以告诉派对狂，他们可以搬进来了。”

En: In any event, just leave that equipment here. I'll put it away later. If you'd like, you can take one of the extra headsets. I don't need so many spares.
Zh: “把设备留在这儿就行了。我一会把它收起来。你要是愿意，可以多带一对耳机。我不需要多的。”

En: Well, this was an *interesting collaboration*.
Zh: “这是一次*有趣的合作*。”

En: We make a pretty good team, Soona.
Zh: “我们真是个不错的团队，苏娜。”

En: What do you think? Could I make it as a radio engineer?
Zh: “你觉得我能成为一名无线电工程师吗？”

En: Perhaps. You're certainly resourceful. That is a good trait in an engineer...
Zh: “也许吧。你很有想法，对工程师来说，这是十分优秀的素质……”

En: You decapitated our antenna. No, I do not think it's a good idea for you...
Zh: “你弄坏了我们的天线。我觉得你不太适合当工程师……”

En: Now you'll excuse me. I have to get back to my own work.
Zh: “失陪了，我得回去继续我的工作了。”

En: Perhaps. You're more reliable than the writers I used to work with, though that is not a difficult bar to clear...
Zh: “也许吧。你比以前和我共事的作家要可靠，虽然这种事情不算难……”

En: Really? I do not think we were all that successful. But maybe the RCM has a different definition of success...
Zh: “真的？我觉得我们也没那么成功。不过，也许是RCM对成功的定义不太一样吧……”

En: That is why we don't use it, then.
Zh: “所以我们才没用它。”

En: Where am I supposed to find a radio transceiver?
Zh: “我该上哪儿去找一台无线电收发器？”

En: Alright, I guess I need to find one of these transceivers then. (Conclude.)
Zh: “好的，看来我得去找一台收发器。”（总结。）

En: In the meantime, I will gather the rest of the materials. We will meet back here when you have it.
Zh: “那我就去收集剩余的材料。等你找到，我们就在这儿汇合。”

En: And before you ask: No, you can't have the radio from my Kineema.
Zh: “话说在前面，你可别想从我的锐影上拿。”

En: That sounds like a perfectly adequate radio. She nods.
Zh: 她点点头。“感觉是台很合适的无线电。”

En: There is no need to go out of your way to find a *top-shelf* radio for this purpose. As long as it's functional, that is what counts.
Zh: “你没必要去找一台*顶级*无线电。只要功能齐全就够用了。”

En: That will be fine. As long as the radio is *functional*, that is what counts.
Zh: “可以的。只要无线电*功能齐全*就够用了。”

En: You know what, Kim? I'll find an even *better* radio on my own.
Zh: “你知道吗，金？我要自己去找一台*更好的*无线电。”

En: Certainly, though it requires a fair amount of technical finesse to remove them. Frankly, it's not worth the bother. Just find a regular radio.
Zh: “当然了，光是拆除就需要很高的技术含量。说实话，这么做不值得。去找一个普通的无线电就行了。”

En: I highly doubt that. When I crank the volume on my DynaWave, it's like you're in the front row at the National Concert Hall.
Zh: “我不信。每次我把动力波的音量调到最大时，那种感觉就像是坐在国家音乐厅的前排一样。”

En: Wasn't there a *highly functional* radio in that abandoned lorry by the roundabout?
Zh: 在环岛附近的废弃货車里不是有一台*功能齐全*的无线电吗？

En: I didn't know *Speedfreaks FM* had a concert series.
Zh: “我不知道*狂飙怪人.FM*还会转播音乐会。”

En: Alright, Kim, challenge accepted.
Zh: “好了，金，我接受挑战。”

En: On second thought, this is childish. I'm just going to look for a regular radio.
Zh: “细想一下，这种行为太幼稚了。我还是去找一台普通无线电好了。”

En: I think I saw a radio in that office near the harbour...
Zh: “我好像在港口附近的办公室里见到过一台无线电……”

En: Possibly. It's worth a look, at the least.
Zh: “有可能，至少你可以去看看。”

En: Do radiocomputers have transceivers in them?
Zh: “无线电脑里面有收发器吗？”

En: Joke all you want, detective. Whatever radio you manage to scrounge up won't lay a *finger* on my set-up.
Zh: “随你怎么想吧，警探。不管你要弄一台什么样的无线电，反正别*碰*我的东西。”

En: A radio transceiver. Cool. I've got this. I've seen *plenty* of those lying around.
Zh: “无线电收发器啊，真不错。我知道在哪儿去找，这东西我见到过*好多*。”

En: Do you think the pawnshop will have one?
Zh: “你觉得当铺会有卖的吗？”

En: Of course you have. They aren't exactly rare.
Zh: “你当然见过。那东西很常见的。”

En: I cannot answer that for you, but I don't expect it to be *that* difficult. There's no shortage of radios around here.
Zh: “我没法回答你的问题，可是应该没*那么*难。这儿附近有许多无线电……”

En: If you have reál to spare, you might even be able to *buy* one. But it doesn't really matter where it comes from, so long as it works.
Zh: “如果你钱多，甚至可以去*买*一台。反正不管你从哪儿弄，只要能用就行……”

En: Can't we just use your Rehm Prefect?
Zh: “我们不能直接用你的雷姆行政官吗？”

En: Can't we just use your radiocomputer?
Zh: “我们不能直接用你的无线电脑吗？”

En: Absolutely not. My Rehm Prefect is my livelihood. If something were to go wrong, it would be a disaster.
Zh: “当然不可以。我的雷姆行政官就是我的饭碗。如果出了什么茬子，那问题可就大了。”

En: The wind pushes a piece of tare across the cracked tiles. From the north comes the malicious laughter of two children...
Zh: 一阵风吹来，回收品的碎片在破裂的瓷砖上移动。北边传来两个小孩不怀好意的笑声……

En: From behind you, a low, spectral voice whispers: 'Nay.'
Zh: 你的身后传来一个低沉如幽灵般的声音：‘不。’

En: The programmer gives you an impatient look before returning to her radiocomputer.
Zh: 程序员不耐烦地看了你一眼，然后回到她的无线电脑前。

En: I wasn't kidding earlier about contacting Coalition Warship Archer. Can you help me?
Zh: “我先前说想要和联盟国战舰弓箭手号取得联络，这可不是在开玩笑。你能帮我吗？”

En: Now that the ravers are settled in, can you help me contact Coalition Warship Archer?
Zh: “派对狂那边已经安排好了，现在你可以帮我联络联盟国战舰弓箭手号了吗？”

En: It's true, the drug fiends have made themselves quite comfortable. But *I* still have a great deal of work to do...
Zh: “没错，那帮瘾君子过得逍遥自在，可*我*还有一大把工作要做……”

En: This project is immensely important to her. She's not going to help you until it's finished.
Zh: 这个项目对她很重要。在完成之前，她是不会帮助你的。

En: If you're desperate for collaborators now, I suggest you ask your new friends. They seem to have plenty of time for side projects.
Zh: “如果你着急寻找伙伴，我建议去问问你的新朋友。他们好像有大把时间去处理编外项目。”

En: You really should look into this totalled MC when you have a chance. Seems important...
Zh: 如果你有时间，真该好好看看这辆車。看起来很重要……

En: He's seen *you*.
Zh: “他看见*你*了。”

En: And?
Zh: “所以呢？”

En: And? *The crab-man* has seen you!
Zh: “所以？那个*蟹男*看见你了！”

En: I don't care, I don't care about crab men. She barely looks up, now tinkering with the machine's printer.
Zh: “我不在乎，我不在乎什么蟹男。”她几乎没怎么向上看，埋头开始修理机器的打印机了。

En: Exactly, what *does* it mean? There's something frantic about her as she locks her gaze with you, eyes shining like pearls. "Up to now it has been impossible to say what it is, because it's impossible to measure *nothing*."
Zh: “没错，这到底是*什么*意思？”她把视线锁定在你身上的时候，给人一种狂乱的感觉，她的眼睛就像珍珠一样闪亮。“目前为止，还不可能说它是什么，因为要测量*无*是不可能的。”

En: What do *you* think it is? What qualities does *nothing* have? How do you measure something that does not exist? She's suddenly absorbed in the conversation, waiting for your answer.
Zh: “*你*觉得这是什么？*无*有什么特性？不存在的东西又该怎么测量？”她突然被谈话吸引了，正等待着你的答案。

En: Easy, you measure it by the world around it.
Zh: 简单，测量它周围的世界。

En: What? She squints her eyes. "I hate it."
Zh: “什么？”她眯起眼睛。“我恨它。”

En: A youth centre would be nice.
Zh: “青少年中心应该挺不错的。”

En: A petting zoo, a place for animals!
Zh: “宠物动物园，留给动物的地方！”

En: Maybe some community space to help the elderly?
Zh: “也许可以当成帮助老人的社区空间？”

En: I'm still convinced they want to establish a nightclub for anodic dance music -- they said it's their *dream*.
Zh: “我还是很确信他们想把这里建成一个阳极舞曲的夜店——他们说这是他们的*梦想*。”

En: But honestly, it's not progressing very well. She grows silent, staring at her circle of basins -- it looks like some ancient ritual.
Zh: “但是说实在的，进行的不是很顺利。”她盯着自己的水盆圆环，陷入沉默——它看起来就像某种古老的仪式。

En: It sounds like you're not worried about him at all.
Zh: “听起来你一点也不担心他。”

En: No, you're right, I'm not... Her voice trails off, as she bends over to tinker with the machine's printer.
Zh: “不，你说得对，我不在乎……”她弯腰摆弄着机器的打印机，声音也变小了。

En: I don't know, I'm not here for some science, I just want to solve a murder so I can go home.
Zh: “我不知道，我来这里不是为了科学研究，我只想破获这起凶杀案，这样才能回家。”

En: You measure it by collecting data on its surroundings, on that which exists.
Zh: “可以收集它周围的数据来测量，就是那些存在的东西。”

En: Exactly, she nods, "very true! That's what I've been aiming for, that's why I have those basins. I've tried using hydrotransducers to record the silence -- to find out where it *begins*."
Zh: “没错，”她点点头。“非常正确！这就是我一直以来的目标，所以我才会弄来那些水盆。我尝试用水电传感器记录这种寂静——找出它*开始*的地方。”

En: Isn't your Rehm Prefect radiocomputer made exclusively for the government-use?
Zh: “你的雷姆行政官无线电脑好像是政府专用版啊？”

En: So you do know *something* about computers. She looks up, almost proud of you. "You're right, Prefect *is* used mostly by the government peeps."
Zh: “所以你确实懂得*一些*电脑的事。”她抬起头，几乎有些为你感到骄傲。“你说得对，行政官基本上*都是*政府拿来偷窥用的。”

En: I can't even understand how we're talking about something that doesn't exist, let alone measure it.
Zh: “我甚至都不明白，我们要怎么讨论一种不存在的东西，更不用说测量了。”

En: You measure it by its surroundings -- by that which does exist, she replies, "which is what I've been trying to do. I've tried using hydrotransducers to record the silence -- to find out where it *begins*."
Zh: “你可以通过周围的环境来测量它——它就存在于此，”她回复到，“这就是我一直尝试的事情。我尝试用水电传感器记录这种寂静——找出它*开始*的地方。”

En: A small satisfied smile breaks free on her face, infuriatingly triumphant.
Zh: 她的脸上显露出一丝满意的笑容，得意洋洋的样子让人有些窝火。

En: I'm protected by the Wayfarer Act.
Zh: “我受到旅人法案的保护。”

En: What's the Wayfarer Act?
Zh: “旅人法案是什么？”

En: (Cross your arms.) "There is no Wayfarer Act."
Zh: （抱起双臂。）“没有什么旅人法案。”

En: The Wayfarer Act states that citizens have the right to gather in public spaces unless they're disrupting the public order.
Zh: “旅人法案声明：公民有权在公共空间聚集，除非他们扰乱了公共秩序。”

En: Ah, but there *is* and it *protects* me!
Zh: “啊，但是它*就在*那里，而且*保护*着我！”

En: Did she say *over 16 years of experience*? She must have started programming when she was still a teenager.
Zh: 她是不是说了*超过16年的工作经历*？她肯定是从青少年时期就开始编程了。

En: Look at how pleased she is with herself, playing with her 'important science machines'. Academics!!
Zh: 看看她对自己有多满意，玩弄着她那些‘重要的科学仪器’。学者，哼！！

En: Wait, I have my... I have my... (Take out your gun.)
Zh: “等等，我有……我有……”（把枪掏出来。）

En: You're right. The Wayfarer Act is the foundation of our legal order. I can't cross it on a whim. You're welcome to stay.
Zh: “你是对的。旅人法案是法律秩序的基础。我不能一时兴起就违反它。欢迎你留在这里。”

En: Have some dignity, the woman sighs, turning back to her radiocomputer.
Zh: “有点尊严吧，”女人叹了口气，然后转回到她的无线电脑前。

En: You're leaving this church... (Take out your gun.) "...one way or another."
Zh: “你会离开这座教堂……”（把枪掏出来。）“……不管以什么方式。”

En: But you don't take out your gun -- instead you fumble with the holster, desperately trying to free the weapon from its pouch... It looks pathetic.
Zh: 但是你没能掏出枪——而是笨手笨脚地摆弄着枪套，绝望地想把武器从里面取出来……你的样子看起来实在是太可悲了。

En: God... The girl immediately steps back and raises her hands. "Easy now, I'll go, it's not an issue! Can I pack my things first?"
Zh: “天啦……”女孩立马退后一步，举起双手。“放松点，我会走的，这不是问题！我能先打包下自己的东西吗？”

En: (Nod once.) "Just make sure you're gone by the time I get back."
Zh: （点一下头。）“只要保证我回来的时候你已经走了就行。”

En: You better start going if you don't want to be frogmarched to the jail. (Roll up your sleeves.)
Zh: “你最好马上走，如果不想被押进监狱的话。”（卷起你的袖子。）

En: God... She immediately steps back and raises her hands, sensing the aggression. "Easy now, I'll go. It's not an issue. Can I pack my things first?"
Zh: “天啦……”女孩立马退后一步，举起双手。“放松点，我会走的，这不是问题！我能先打包下自己的东西吗？”

En: She turns off her computer and leaves.
Zh: 她关掉电脑，然后离开了。

En: Miss, I need you to be gone from this church when I come back. (Put away your gun.)
Zh: “小姐，等我回来的时候，我需要你已经离开这座教堂了。”（把枪收起来。）

En: Detective...
Put the gun down, detective. The lieutenant's voice is low and firm.
Zh: “把枪放下，警探。”警督的声音低沉而坚定。

En: I'm not putting down my gun unless she promises to be gone when I come back.
Zh: “我不会放下枪的，除非她答应等我回来的时候她就不在了。”

En: I promise, I promise! she exclaims, words echoing below the ceiling rafters.
Zh: “我保证，我保证！”她大喊着，声音在天花板椽木下回荡。

En: Good. (Put away your gun.)
Zh: “很好。”（把枪收起来。）

En: Please just go, will you? Please... Pretty please, I don't want to fight. Fighting is scary, and gives me breathing difficulties.
Zh: “请直接离开，好吗？麻烦你……真的麻烦你，我不想动武。动武太吓人了，会让我呼吸困难。”

En: Of course I can stay. It wasn't even a question.
Zh: “我当然可以留下来。这根本都不算是个问题。”

En: I don't want to make anything work, she replies, her expression unchanged.
Zh: “我不想跟任何人和平共处,”她回答到，脸上的表情毫无变化。

En: Put on the headphones.
Zh: 戴上耳机。

En: Examined the ravers' speakers.
Zh: 检查派对狂的扬声器。

En: Col Do Ma Ma Daqua.
Zh: 哈库多玛达塔。

En: Helping Egg Head with his jam.
Zh: 帮助爱凡客完成音乐。

En: Found a melody for Egg Head.
Zh: 帮爱凡客找一段旋律。

En: And then the pressure changes.
Zh: 然后压力改变了。

En: All you hear is your own breathing, heavy and hoarse from all the nights spent drinking... It's the breath of an old man.
Zh: 你唯一能听到的只有自己的呼吸，因为夜夜饮酒变得沉重而嘶哑……这是一个老人的呼吸。

En: A chance to cook some speed, you mean? she asks sourly, crossing her arms.
Zh: “你是说，做兴奋剂吗？”她抱紧双臂，坏坏地问到。

En: (Lie.) "They *promised* they'll do it clean."
Zh: （撒谎。）“他们*保证*会干干净净地开店。”

En: They *promised* they'll do it clean.
Zh: “他们*保证*会干干净净地开店。”

En: Uh, *did they*? she asks, clicking her tongue. A moment passes.
Zh: “啊，他们*这么说*吗？”她砸吧着舌头，问到。一刻过去了。

En: Alright, this is all very good and sad, but I have my research to finish. Trust me, this is far more important than the fate of some local speedfreaks and their dance music.
Zh: “好吧，这一切都很好，也很悲伤，不过我还要完成自己的研究。相信我，这比本地派对狂的命运和他们的舞曲要重要得多。”

En: What if I helped you *finish* your project? You said it's not going very well. You could leave when it's finished -- and they could come in.
Zh: “如果我帮你*完成*这个工程呢？你说过进行的不是很顺利。完成之后你就可以离开——然后他们就能搬进来。”

En: What if I helped you *finish* your project? Then you could leave -- and they could come in.
Zh: “如果我帮你*完成*这个工程呢？然后你可以离开——然后他们可以搬进来。”

En: Well? Soona asks as soon as you take off the headphones. "Did you hear anything?"
Zh: “怎么样？”你一取下耳机，苏娜就开口问到。“你听见什么了吗？”

En: No, nothing. It's just silence.
Zh: “没有，什么也没有。只有寂静。”

En: Yeah... Her voice is bitter with disappointment.
Zh: “是啊……”她的声音苦涩中带着失望。

En: Why can't you go and get the filament yourself?
Zh: “为什么你不能自己去拿纤维内存呢？”

En: Where is your old workspace?
Zh: “你的旧办公地址在哪？”

En: Hold on... If it's called an off-site copy, then why is it still *on*-site?
Zh: 等等……如果叫场外拷贝的话，那为什么还在*现场*状态呢？

En: If it's called an off-site copy, then why is it still on-site?
Zh: “等等……如果叫场外拷贝的话，那为什么还在*现场*状态呢？”

En: Oh god, not *this* again... She takes a deep breath, before letting it all out: "It is not *on-site*, it is *in the basement*, perfectly safe and not connected to the front *at all*."
Zh: “天啦，别又来*这个*……”她深吸一口气，然后吐出来说到：“它不在*现场*，是在*地下室*里，非常安全，*完全没有*跟任何前端连接。”

En: And where exactly is the off-site copy?
Zh: “那场外拷贝到底是什么？”

En: There's this giant ice bear fridge in the building's cellar -- it's there.
Zh: “那栋建筑的地下室里有一个巨大的冰熊冰箱——它就在那里。”

En: I found a note from the ice bear fridge. It said the off-site copy had been moved to 'a safer place'.
Zh: “我在冰熊冰箱里发现一张纸条。上面说场外拷贝已经被转移到一个‘更安全的地方’了。”

En: Zawisza, of course... She relaxes. "Our project lead Sulisław Zawisza. God, he was always so hell-bent on keeping the copy somewhere 'safe'. And feature creep..." she mutters, "and the Valley of the Heads..."
Zh: “扎维沙，当然是他……”她放松了一些。“我们项目的主管苏里罗·扎维沙。天啦，他总是这么不顾一切地要把拷贝放在‘安全’的地方。还有特征蠕动……”她嘟囔着，“还有人头谷……”

En: Is this the filament you're looking for? (Show her the production schedule.)
Zh: “这就是你要找的纤维内存吗？”（把生产计划拿给她看。）

En: Wait, no! That's dangerous, she shouldn't do it!
Zh: 等等，不行！这样太危险了，她不应该这么做！

En: Don't waste your time printing it out, there's nothing but ink there. It's broken.
Zh: “别浪费时间打印了，上面除了墨水什么也没有。它坏了。”

En: What an interesting proposition! Truly a task for the intellectuals -- solving the puzzle of water bowls.
Zh: 真是个有趣的命题！真正为知识分子准备的任务——解决水盆之谜。

En: Yes, but you like moving things around. Moving things around is calming.
Zh: 是的，但是你喜欢移动东西。这样能让你平静。

En: I have moved the bowls. It was mindnumbingly easy. What's next?
Zh: “我把水盆放好了。真是简单地让人思想都麻木了。接下来呢？”

En: I moved the water bowls to the right position, just like you asked. What's next?
Zh: “我按照你要求的，把水盆移到正确的位置了。接下来呢？”

En: Because it reminds us of death. And we humans tend to think that death is pretty scary.
Zh: “因为它让我想起死亡。而且我们人类倾向于觉得死亡是很可怕的。”

En: It's scary, but we just have to face it.
Zh: “是有点吓人，不过我们必须去面对。”

En: Despite her fear she was hoping for something extraordinary to take place.
Zh: 尽管她有些害怕，却还是希望能发生一些离奇的事情。

En: MAXIMUM! shouts Egg Head, a great smile still adorning his face, larger than a red dwarf star.
Zh: “调到最大！”爱凡客大喊到，他的脸上仍然挂着一个大大的笑容，比红矮星还要大。

En: Why do you need an antenna? (Point to it.)
Zh: “你要天线做什么？”（指向它。）

En: What about those bowls of water over there?
Zh: “那边那些水盆呢？”

En: What are you doing with your radiocomputer?
Zh: “你在用无线电脑做什么？”

En: Why are there so many machines in this place?
Zh: “为什么这里有这么多机器？”

En: I brought them here. These are my machines. Please don't touch anything.
Zh: “我把它们弄过来的。这些都是我的机器。请不要碰任何东西。”

En: What are you doing in an abandoned church?
Zh: “你在这座废弃的教堂做什么？”

En: Do you have any idea where this hole might be located?
Zh: “你知不知道这个洞可能在什么位置？”

En: A hole in the world... What does that mean exactly?
Zh: “世界上的一个洞……这到底是什么意思？”

En: Okay. Wow. That was quick. Why do you hate it?
Zh: “好吧，哇哦，还真够快的。你为什么恨它？”

En: Maybe I'd have to be *on drugs* to get it, but to a sober mind it just sounds like uninspired rug whipping. No idea what it has to do with either dancing or music.
Zh: “也许我必须*嗑药*才能做到，不过在一个清醒的大脑看来，它听起来不过像是寻常的拍打地毯的声音。不知道这跟舞蹈或者音乐有什么关系。”

En: Right, right... but how do you feel about a *club* for anodic dance music?
Zh: “好吧，好吧……那你觉得阳极舞曲的*夜店*怎么样？”

En: This is about those speedfreaks in the tent, isn't it? She looks up, shaking her head. "I've got some news for you, it's not a *nightclub* they want to build here..."
Zh: “是关于帐篷里那些派对狂的，对吧？”她抬起眼，摇了摇头。“我有消息要告诉你，他们想在这里建的不是一家*夜店*……”

En: What do they want to build then?
Zh: “那他们想建什么？”

En: Take a guess, why don't you?
Zh: “你为什么不猜猜看呢？”

En: I heard they're also planning on building a drug lab.
Zh: “我听说他们打算在这里建一个制毒实验室。”

En: Bingo. She snaps her fingers. "That's exactly what they're up to."
Zh: “答对了。”她打了个响指。“他们就是打算干这个。”

En: Wonderful, the lieutenant whispers, admiring the embellished pillars and the spectacular architecture in the church.
Zh: “太美妙了，”警督轻声低语，赞赏着教堂里的装饰柱和壮观的建筑风格。”

En: Good.
Zh: “很好。”

En: Really?
Zh: “真的吗？”

En: No, of course not. She turns her tired, sardonic eyes to face you. "I feel even worse about drugs than I feel about nightclubs."
Zh: “不，当然不是。”她把那双疲惫又嘲弄的眼睛转向你。“我对毒品的感觉甚至比夜店还要糟。”

En: I feel that's illegal.
Zh: “我感觉这是不合法的。”

En: I see you've talked to them, good. Drugs are illegal, and I'm not leaving this church -- especially because of some corrupt drug lab idea you've cooked up in that tent.
Zh: “我看见你跟他们谈话了，很好。毒品是违法的，而且我不会离开这座教堂——尤其是因为你在那顶帐篷里编造的那些堕落的制毒实验室的主意。”

En: Right, isn't that what makes it fun?
Zh: “好吧，不过这样不是比较有意思吗？”

En: Your feeling is right, manufacturing drugs is a little illegal.
Zh: “你的感觉是对的，制毒是有些违法。”

En: No, certainly not. I've got a citizen's investigation to run and I want nothing to do with local vices.
Zh: “不，当然不是。我还要进行公民调查研究，而且完全不想跟当地恶势力扯上任何关系。”

En: Yes, I know that.
Zh: “是的，我知道。”

En: They're just encouraging an active *youth culture* here, nothing wrong with that.
Zh: “他们只是在鼓励活跃的*青年文化*，这没什么错啊。”

En: Mind-altering substances are a vital part of anodic club scene, it helps to bring the community together.
Zh: “致幻物质是阳极舞曲俱乐部的关键所在，它能帮助整个社区的团结。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: So that's settled, great. She supports her chin on her hand, visibly pleased: "No nightclubs in the church then."
Zh: “所以就这么定了，很好。”她用手托着下巴，明摆着很高兴：“那教堂里就不建夜店了。”

En: Yeah, what I said before... Not possible to appreciate that kind of music without being high as a kite.
Zh: “是啊，我之前说过了……如果不嗑的像风筝一样飞得那么高，根本就不可能欣赏得了那种音乐。”

En: I'm just encouraging an active *youth culture*, nothing wrong with that.
Zh: “我只是想鼓励一种活跃的*青年文化*，这没什么错啊。”

En: Wonderful, the lieutenant whispers. "Now this place is also going to be a drug lab. Always something fascinating happening in tents."
Zh: “太棒了，”警督低语到。“现在这个地方要变成一个制毒实验室了。帐篷里总是会发生一些让人神魂颠倒的事呢。”

En: Mind-altering substances are a vital part of anodic club scene. It helps to bring the community together.
Zh: “致幻物质是阳极舞曲俱乐部的关键所在，它能帮助整个社区的团结。”

En: Say nothing.
Zh: 什么也不说。

En: No, don't mind me, go on with your little project. I'm sure you can spin it as an under-cover op. Many cops do.
Zh: “不，别在意我，继续你的小工程吧。我肯定你能把它编造成一出秘密行动。很多警察都可以。”

En: It is, yes. It's against the law. We're cops -- the law is *important* to us.
Zh: “是的，没错。这是违法的。我们是警察——法律对于我们来说很*重要*。”

En: Gotcha. It's almost party time!
Zh: “明白。差不多就快到派对时间了！”

En: How could you? This is a completely separate issue!
Zh: “你怎么可以这样？这是一件完全不相干的事情！”

En: (Ignore him and address the lead programmer) "I want you to take your belongings and leave within an hour."
Zh: （无视他，跟主程序员说话）“我想让你带上自己的行李，在一小时内离开。”

En: Interesting, a police officer trying to evict a citizen from a public space despite the Wayfarer Act.
Zh: “有意思，一个警官居然想违反旅人法案，把一个市民驱逐出公共空间。”

En: Apologies, I was just thinking out loud. Go on.
Zh: “不好意思，我只是在自言自语。你继续。”

En: No, please, not the Wayfarer Act again... (Close your eyes.)
Zh: “不，请不要再提旅人法案了……”（闭上眼睛。）

En: Yes, thank you. Now, if you'd only let me work...
Zh: “是的，谢谢你。现在，如果你能让我继续工作的话……”

En: And before you go on, I am *not* disrupting any order here, I'm a circuit bender and no one has anything against circuit benders.
Zh: “在你继续之前，我*不会*扰乱任何秩序，我是一个电路创意改装师，而且没有人会反对一个电路创意改装师。”

En: I'm a circuit bender and no one ever has anything against circuit benders.
Zh: “我只是一个电路创意改装师，而且没有人会反对一个电路创意改装师。”

En: I don't *care* about the Wayfarer Act! Get out of here! (Stomp your feet.)
Zh: “我不*在乎*旅人法案！给我出去！”（用力跺脚。）

En: I can't do it. I don't *feel* scary.
Zh: 我做不到。我没有*感觉*到恐惧。

En: No, but you see, it doesn't matter whether you care about it or not. It's the law, she explains to you without even sparing a look, "I'm not going anywhere."
Zh: “不，不过你看，你在不在乎不重要。这是法律，”她向你解释着，不过甚至连看都没看你一眼。“我哪里都不去。”

En: She's still typing behind the radiocomputer, her sweater-clad frame hunched over the machine.
Zh: 她还在无线电脑背后敲打着键盘，穿着毛衣的身影俯在机器前面。

En: Hold on, you don't want to make *anything* work?
Zh: “等等，你不想跟*任何人*和平共处？”

En: Yes, *anything*. I don't want to make anything work.
Zh: “是的，*任何人*。我不想跟*任何人*和平共处。”

En: It's not the anodic dance music that's made her bitter... it's the failure of Fortress Accident.
Zh: 让她痛苦的不是阳极音乐……而是要塞事故的失败。

En: Are you bitter because your radio game project failed?
Zh: “你痛苦是因为自己的无线游戏项目失败了吗？”

En: That's right. Something strange shines in her eyes. "If we couldn't get our *welkins* to happen, I don't want anything to happen. Ever again."
Zh: “是的。”某种奇怪的东西在她的眼睛里闪烁。“如果我们不能让*精灵*成功，那我也不想让任何事情成功。再也不想了。”

En: There's not a trace of irony in her voice. She means it.
Zh: 她的声音里完全没有讽刺的意味。她是认真的。

En: Don't bring it up.
Zh: 别提这件事。

En: This is Martinaise, what can you expect? They're the product of their surroundings.
Zh: “这里是马丁内斯，你还能期待什么？他们是周围环境的产物。”

En: Very cool, but I don't want any part of those 'surroundings' myself, good or bad.
Zh: “很酷，不过我不想要自己周围那些‘环境’，不论好坏。”

En: On Rue de Saint-Ghislaine in the East Delta Commerce Centre. She wipes some hair off her face and sighs.
Zh: “在东德尔塔商业中心的圣吉莱纳路。”她拨下脸上的几缕发丝，叹了口气。

En: The bookstore lady hates me -- says I'm part of the *curse*, whatever that means.
Zh: “书店女主人很恨我——说我是*诅咒*的一部分，虽然我不知道她是什么意思。”

En: Wait -- bookstore lady -- you mean Plaisance?
Zh: “等等——书店女主人——你是说普莱桑斯？”

En: That's her name, I believe.
Zh: “我想应该是这个名字没错。”

En: Why does she think you're part of the curse?
Zh: “她为什么觉得你是诅咒的一部分？”

En: Because she's from Martinaise and people from Martinaise have never ever seen a radiocomputer. She thinks it emits elemental *evil*.
Zh: “因为她来自马丁内斯，马丁内斯人从没见过无线电脑。她觉得它会放射*邪恶*元素。”

En: By your old workspace do you mean the studio of Fortress Accident in that office building on plaza?
Zh: “你说的旧办公地址，是不是广场那栋办公大厦的要塞事故工作室？”

En: By your old workspace do you mean the studio of Fortress Accident in the Doomed Commercial Area?
Zh: “你说的旧办公地址，是不是诅咒商业区的要塞事故工作室？”

En: Yeah, that's the one. You can get in through the bookshop. You just have to do some explaining to the bookstore lady.
Zh: “没错，就是那里。你可以从书店里面进去。只要能想办法跟书店女店主解释就行。”

En: About the off-site copy you asked me to bring...
Zh: “关于你让我找的那个场外拷贝……”

En: Yes?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: She gets back to work.
Zh: 她回头工作了。

En: And no, taking it outside the building *wouldn't* have protected it from the data loss. There's nothing wrong with keeping the backup in the basement. What happened was a freak accident that has nothing to do with how the backup was stored.
Zh: “不是，把它带出那栋建筑*不能*保证不出现数据丢失。把备份放在地下室里保存是没有问题的。发生的特殊事故跟备份如何保存是没有关系的。”

En: This is clearly a painful topic for her. She must have had to explain herself numerous times.
Zh: 对她来说，这显然是个痛苦的话题。她肯定已经解释过无数遍了。

En: No, that's the production schedule you stole and accessed without authorization. She's tapping the table in a badly concealed impatience. "I don't need it."
Zh: ”不，你偷的是生产计划，没有授权是无法读取的。”她拍打着桌子，完全掩饰不住自己的不耐烦。“我不需要它。”

En: By the way, we put a dead body in that fridge.
Zh: “话说，我们把一具尸体放进那台冰箱了。”

En: You put it there... She leans back, massaging her eyelids. "You put a dead body inside the ice bear fridge..."
Zh: “你把它放在那里……”她向后靠了靠，按摩着眼睑。“你把一具尸体放在冰熊冰箱里面……”

En: I found the ice cream maker, but couldn't get it open -- it's completely frozen.
Zh: “我找到一台冰淇淋制造机，但是打不开它——它完全冻住了。”

En: I brought you the filament. (Give her the off-site copy.)
Zh: “我把纤维内存给你拿来了。”（把场外拷贝交给她。）

En: No, that's the production schedule, I don't need it. The right filament memory has 'OFF-SITE COPY' written on silver tape.
Zh: “不，那个是生产计划，我用不着。正确的纤维内存上有一条银色胶带，上面写着‘场外拷贝’。”

En: Wait! Don't put it in yet, it might be dangerous!
Zh: “等等！先别放进去，可能很危险！”

En: I know it's just ink, she replies, as the filament clicks into place, "but Lintel was somehow able to divine the location of the anomaly from this broken copy. I want to repeat their calculation, only this time with better equipment."
Zh: “我知道只是墨水，”她回复着，纤维内存正好也嵌入进去，“不过林内尔还是想办法从这个坏掉的拷贝里预测出异常地点。我想重复它们的计算，只是这次会用更好的设备。”

En: Don't waste your time printing it out, there's nothing but a speck of white in a sea of ink. It's broken.
Zh: “别浪费时间打印了，上面除了一片墨海中的一个白色斑点之外，什么也没有。它坏了。”

En: A speck of white? She sounds excited, as the filament clicks into place. "That's exactly what I'm hoping to find."
Zh: “一个白色斑点？”她回复着，纤维内存正好也嵌入进去。“那就是我希望找到的东西。”

En: What are you even hoping to find?
Zh: “你到底希望找到什么呢？”

En: I have a theory, she says, as the filament clicks into place. "Lintel was able to divine the location of the anomaly from this broken copy. I want to repeat their calculation, only this time with better equipment."
Zh: “我有一个理论，”她回复着，纤维内存正好也嵌入进去。“林内尔能够从这个坏掉的拷贝里预测出异常地点。我想重复它们的计算，只是这次会用更好的设备。”

En: Watch, she says and presses PRINT on the machine's keyboard, "what an intricate display of failure..."
Zh: “看着，”她一边说，一边按下机器键盘上的打印按钮，“多么复杂的显示故障啊……”

En: Lintel was able to divine the location of the anomaly from this broken copy. I want to repeat their calculation, only this time with better equipment.
Zh: “林内尔能够从这个坏掉的拷贝里预测出异常地点。我想重复它们的计算，只是这次会用更好的设备。”

En: (Play it cool.) "Now what?"
Zh: （不露声色。）“现在怎么样？”

En: Something's very wrong with that filament memory.
Zh: “那个纤维内存真是出大问题了。”

En: This is wrong, machines shouldn't behave like that.
Zh: 不对啊，机器不应该有这种反应的。

En: It's the abyss staring back at you.
Zh: 这是深渊在回望着你。

En: (Step back.) "Please just... stop. Stop playing with this thing!"
Zh: （退后。）“麻烦你……停下来。别再播那玩意了！”

En: Cold sweat trickles down your spine... This is spooky as hell.
Zh: 冷汗顺着你的脊椎向下流窜……简直像地狱一样让人毛骨悚然。

En: Huh? She looks up, puzzled.
Zh: “哈？”她不解地抬起眼。

En: You okay?
Zh: “你还好吧？”

En: I'm okay, just please be careful. Trying to locate a *hole in the world* might be dangerous.
Zh: “我还好，只是要小心点。试图定位*世界上的洞*可能很危险。”

En: Nothing, khm, please... continue.
Zh: “没什么，唔，请……继续。”

En: I *am* being careful. Treat it as a mere technical anomaly, makes it easier to bear. She runs her hands over the printout, searching for something...
Zh: “我*很*小心了。就把它当成是一个技术异常吧，这样容易承受一些。”她用手抚摸着打印文件，寻找着什么……

En: Soona doesn't reply, her hands running over the printout. She's looking for something -- for her morning star -- eyes scouring the millimetres.
Zh: 苏娜没有回复，用手抚摸着打印文件。她在找什么——找她的晨星——眼神毫米不差地彻底搜索着。

En: Here, she suddenly says, rising her ink-stained fingertip, "I found it."
Zh: “这里，”她突然开口，举起沾染墨水的手指，“我找到它了。”

En: Right, I'll go figure it out, I'm good at intellectual puzzles.
Zh: “没错，我会查清楚的，我很擅长智力题。”

En: Figure it out? She shakes her head, grinning. "No, I don't need you to figure anything out, I've got a computer for that." She pats the mainframe.
Zh: “查清楚？”她摇摇头，咧嘴笑了。“不，我不需要你查清楚，我有一台电脑就是干这个的。”她拍了拍主机。

En: What? She only wants you to follow instructions, nothing *intellectually stimulating* in this task... A child could do it!
Zh: 什么？她只想让你遵循指示，这个任务没有什么*智力模拟*……一个小孩都能做到！

En: Just walk over to the circle and follow my instructions: Move the third bowl two centimetres to the left and the fourth bowl five centimetres to the right. This should do the trick.
Zh: “只要走到那边的圆圈里，然后听从我的指示：把第三个水盆向左移动2厘米，第四个水盆向右移动5厘米。这样应该就可以了。”

En: You like moving things around. Moving things around is calming.
Zh: 你喜欢移动东西。这样能让你平静。

En: What, you don't trust my intellectual capabilities to solve this problem on my own?
Zh: “什么，难道你不相信以我的聪明才智可以独立解决这个问题吗？”

En: Sure, no problem.
Zh: “当然，没问题。”

En: Thanks. She smiles.
Zh: “谢谢。”她笑了。

En: Eh? She frowns. "Come on, it's not about your brain -- even I couldn't figure it out on my own."
Zh: “呃？”她皱起眉头。“拜托，并不是说你的脑子怎么了——就连我自己一个人也搞不清楚的。”

En: Wait, why did you have your headphones on mute in the first place?
Zh: “等等，你一开始为什么要把耳机静音呢？”

En: Isn't it going to be just silence and nothing else?
Zh: “这样不是除了寂静之外什么也没有了吗？”

En: Honestly... She's avoiding your gaze. "Honestly, I'm a little scared."
Zh: “老实说……”她回避了你的目光。“老实说，我有点害怕。”

En: I don't know. She stares at the heart of her computer. "That's what I'm scared of: I don't know. It could be *anything*."
Zh: “我不知道。”她盯着电脑的中心。“这就是我害怕的：我不知道。它可能是*任何东西*。”

En: I don't know, I'm just scared. Maybe it's going to be something terrifying. Maybe it's going to tear the world apart.
Zh: “我不知道，我只是有点害怕。也许是很可怕的东西。也许它会把这个世界撕裂。”

En: And then nothing.
Zh: 然后什么都没有。

En: Like that evil ink that filled the printout, erasing coherence and meaning.
Zh: 打印文件上满是邪恶的墨水，清除掉了连贯性和含义。

En: The lieutenant takes a step back...
Zh: 警督退后一步……

En: But there's something else...
Zh: 但是有什么别的东西……

En: There *has* to be. Something big, something unexpected... something *new*, yet to be discovered.
Zh: *一定*有的。某种大的，意料之外的东西……某些*新的*、尚未被发现的东西。

En: When was the last time this world had anything new to say?
Zh: 这个世界最后一次出现新东西是什么时候？

En: What does it mean?
Zh: 这是什么意思？

En: Yes, but can I *hear* anything?
Zh: 是的，但是我能*听到*什么吗？

En: It feels like flying on an aerostatic, or when your ears pop, or like a subtle difference in the atmosphere, a weather change hanging in the air...
Zh: 感觉就像是乘坐在静风舰上，或者你耳膜爆裂，或者就像大气中存在着一些细微的差异，天气在发生变化……

En: What if the sound you're looking for is too low for you to hear it?
Zh: 如果你要找的声音太低，结果听不见呢？

En: Her lower lip is quivering.
Zh: 她的下唇在颤抖。

En: There's caution in his voice.
Zh: 他的声音里有一些警告的意味。

En: Finally, compliance.
Zh: 终于，顺从了。

En: Like it would have made a difference -- the off-site copy was perfectly safe when the data loss happened. That data loss was *anomalous*. She crosses her arms defiantly.
Zh: “说的好像就能有什么区别一样——数据丢失发生的时候，这份场外拷贝可以说是处于完美的安全状态。这次数据丢失是*异常的*。”她抱紧胳膊以示对抗。

En: From your neighbours, of course.
Zh: “当然，是从你的邻居那里。”

En: Suddenly a rhythmic beat permeates the walls, causing a small patch of decorative stucco to crumble onto the wooden floor.
Zh: 突然间，一阵韵律节拍在墙壁上弥漫，把一小块装饰粉刷震碎到木地板上。

En: You mean the *speedfreaks*? She closes her eyes, as more dance music invades the holy silence of the sanctuary.
Zh: “你的意思是*瘾君子*吗？”她闭上双眼，更多舞曲侵犯着这个圣殿神圣的寂静。

En: Sure, if you agree to let them build their drug lab.
Zh: “当然，如果你同意让他们建制毒实验室的话。”

En: They *would* if you wouldn't mind them moving in with you.
Zh: “如果你不介意他们搬进来跟你一起的话，他们*会的*。”

En: No, officer, I don't *agree* to anything.
Zh: “不，警官，我什么也没*同意*。”

En: But most of the time I'm fully absorbed in my work with the radiocomputer, she then goes on, "and there are still parts of the church I haven't had time to explore, so..."
Zh: “但是大多数时间我都要全神贯注地在无线电脑上工作，”她继续说到，“教堂里还有些部分我还没时间探索，所以……”

En: Great, I'll go talk to them then.
Zh: “很好，那我去跟他们聊聊。”

En: Sure, let me know how it goes. She lingers for a moment, before adding: "Thanks, officer."
Zh: “当然，有什么情况记得告诉我。”她踌躇了一会儿，然后补了一句：“谢谢你，警官。”

En: I guess I could live through a week or two of peaceful coexistence.
Zh: “我猜我可以跟他们和平共处一到两个星期吧。”

En: She pauses, looking you in the eye.
Zh: 她停顿下来，看着你的眼睛。

En: They should really allocate some renovation funds to this place... murmurs the lieutenant, inspecting the damage done to the arabesques.
Zh: “他们真的应该给这个地方分配一些翻新资金……”警督低语着，检查着藤蔓花纹上的损伤。

En: I know, I know it is, but... could you *please* turn it down just this instance? Just *this* one time -- maximum is *not* the only way, okay?
Zh: “我知道，我知道是这样没错，但是……能不能*请*你稍微调小一点点？只要*这一次*——开到最大*不是*唯一的方式，好吗？”

En: MAXIMUM IS THE ONLY WAY!
Zh: “开到最大就是唯一的方式！”

En: Pump it to the brick, pump it to the hard master! There is no other way. Glue-style.
Zh: “左边跟我一起画个龙，右边画一道硬核的彩虹！没有其他的办法。胶胶漆漆的风格。”

En: I think what Egg Head is trying to say here is that the volume button is *stuck* on maximum.
Zh: “我想爱凡客想说的是，声音按钮*卡在*最大量上了。”

En: See? She turns to face you, ready to give up. "He *pumps it to the hard master*. It's hopeless..."
Zh: “看见没？”她面向你，准备放弃。“他要*右边画一道硬核的彩虹*。没希望的……”

En: YEAGHHH! PERMANENT ENLIGHTENMENT. RAY OF SOUND.
Zh: “耶！！！！！永恒的开悟！声音射线。”

En: Of course it is. She shrugs, consigned to her fate.
Zh: “当然是了。”她耸耸肩，决定听天由命。

En: Never mind then, let's get on with our project... I am going to unmute the speakers on a count of five. Everyone ready? She looks around the church.
Zh: “那不要紧，我们继续工程……我打算倒数5个数之后把扬声器解除静音。大家准备好了吗？”她环顾着教堂的四周。

En: Okay, but... She seems hesitant, her eyes still fixed on Egg Head. "Think you can ask him to turn the volume down a bit, just in case?"
Zh: “好的，但是……”她好像有点犹豫，目光依然固定在爱凡客身上。“为了以防万一，你能不能让他把声音调小一点？”

En: Okay. Ready. (Proceed.)
Zh: “好的，准备好了。”（继续。）

En: Uh, hold on for a moment...
Zh: “呃，等一下……”

En: What?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: Why do you want to turn down the volume?
Zh: “你为什么想调小音量？”

En: Because I'm afraid that something might happen. It's an unknown phenomenon... She turns to Egg Head. "We can always turn it back up if there's a need."
Zh: “因为我担心会发生什么事情。这是一个未知的现象……”她转向爱凡客。“如果有需要的话，我们总是可以再把它调大的。”

En: CAN'T TURN DOWN THE HARD CORE!
Zh: “硬核不能调小声！”

En: No, we should definitely do this. I *am* going to do this. Ready? She pauses, looking around the church -- everyone stares at her hand on the keyboard.
Zh: “不，我们必须这么做。我*要*这么做。”她停顿了一下，看了看教堂周围——所有人都盯着键盘上她的手。“准备好了吗？”

En: There's tightness in your chest; anxiety.
Zh: 你感觉胸口一阵发紧；焦虑。

En: Never mind, let's do this. I'm ready. Whooh... (Proceed.)
Zh: “没关系，我们开始吧。我准备好了。呜呼……”（继续。）

En: In the silence, a low hum starts creeping up your spine. It's a song inside you -- not in the speakers, not in the room. A great bass sigh in the basement of your mind. Slowly it builds, until the air around you starts to vibrate.
Zh: 一片寂静中，一阵低沉的嗡嗡声爬上你的脊椎。那是你心底的一首歌——不在扬声器里，不在这个房间。你的心底深处发出一声低沉的叹息声。慢慢变大，直到你周围的空气都开始震颤。

En: Guys, what's going on? There's alarm in the man's voice, as he steps back to scan the surroundings. A slight rattle like crystal clattering in the cupboard fills the air, joining the chorus.
Zh: “兄弟们，出什么事了？”男人的声音里有点警戒的意味，他退后一步，扫视着周围。一阵轻微的咔哒声弥漫在空气中，就像水晶在橱柜里碰撞着，加入了这个合唱。

En: HOSIANNAH! MOTHER OF MEGA! You hear Egg Head yell -- then something else, but his voice is growing faint...
Zh: “和撒那！百万之母！”你听见爱凡客在大喊——然后是别的什么东西，但是他的声音越来越微弱……

En: COME DOWN TO US! LOVE!
Zh: “降临到我们头上吧！爱情！”

En: Everybody, don't panic, it's beautiful!
Zh: “大家都别慌，这真是太美了！”

En: Yeah, we should stop. Soona, mute it!
Zh: “是啊，我们应该停下。苏娜，快静音！”

En: BEAUTIFUL CORE, BEAUTIFUL LIFE!
Zh: “美丽的核心，美丽的生活！”

En: Shit, it doesn't stop! The woman is furiously pressing down on her keyboard -- but the sound doesn't stop. "Acele, have you...?"
Zh: “该死，它停不下来了！”女人猛烈地敲打着键盘——但是声音仍然没有停止。“阿西尔，你有没有……”

En: Fuck... Andre frantically smashes buttons. "I can't shut it up, the signal's passed... It's not *in* here! It's..."
Zh: “见鬼……”安德烈疯狂地砸向按钮。“我没办法把它关上，信号正在传输……它不在*这里*！它……”

En: Yes, I've turned it off! She yells, holding the contact mic in her hand.
Zh: “是的，我已经关掉了！”她大喊着，手里紧紧握着接触式麦克风。

En: No, what they *really* should do is shut down the discomen for disturbing neighbourhood peace.
Zh: “不对，他们*真正*应该做的，是关掉扰乱邻居安宁的迪斯科舞曲。”

En: Yes, but they could help with the speakers...
Zh: “是的，但是他们可以帮忙弄扬声器……”

En: It's getting louder... says Noid, his eyes riveted on the strange circle of water basins.
Zh: “越来越响了……”诺伊德说到。他的眼睛紧紧盯着水盆上的怪圈。

En: Hey, uh, what's that weird rattling sound...?
Zh: “喂，这诡异的咔哒声是什么……？”

En: I've never heard anything this hard core in my life!
Zh: “我这辈子从没听过这么硬核的东西！”

En: The window is going to come down. NO, THE ROOF... She looks up. A screech fills the air, a scream of wood and nails -- the pillars of the church twist and creak above and around you.
Zh: “窗户要掉下来了。不，屋顶……”她抬起头。空气中充斥着一阵尖利的声音，那是木头和钉子在尖叫——教堂头顶和周围的柱子扭曲着，发出咯吱咯吱的声响。

En: The BEAUTY AND THE BEAT! The future of dance, PLANETARY!
Zh: “美丽和节拍！未来舞曲，行星级的！”

En: In the corner of your eye, the lieutenant steps aside cautiously, his eyes searching for a possible evacuation route.
Zh: 你用眼角的余光看到警督谨慎地躲开了，眼睛在搜寻可能的逃生路线。

En: No, Egg! It's the window. The glass shards around Dolores Dei's vacant heart appear to be vibrating from the sound. It almost looks as if she's alive.
Zh: “不，凡客！是窗户。”德洛莉丝·黛那颗空虚心脏周围的玻璃碎片像是在随着声音震动。看起来她仿佛活过来了一样。

En: Below it all the bass grows, like the jaws of a giant compressor gnawing on metal and wood. It does not sound benevolent *at all*.
Zh: 在那下方，所有低音都在增长，就像一台巨大压缩机的下颚在啮咬着金属和木头。听起来*一点也不*亲切。

En: A great PULSE arises in your flesh...
Zh: 你的肉体产生了一次巨大的脉动……

En: In his mind, a tidal wave approaching from afar, swallowing entire coastlines on its way... salvation.
Zh: 在他的脑海里，一阵浪潮从远处掀了过来，沿途吞没了整条海岸线……救命。

En: OH, I WANT TO DANCE WITH SOMEBODY! (Start tapping your foot.)
Zh: “哦，我想跟人一起跳舞！”（开始跺脚。）

En: OH, I WANT TO FEEL THE HEAT WITH SOMEBODY!
Zh: “哦，我想和某人一起感受这份热度！”

En: And then it stops. Totally and utterly -- as if there never was a sound. Only your ears still ring from the shock. Everybody is staring at Egg Head -- holding a dangling cable in his hand. A black three-pin connector.
Zh: 然后它停止了。完完全全，彻彻底底——就好像声音从来没有出现过。只有你的耳朵还在因为刚才的震颤嗡嗡作响。所有人都看向爱凡客的方向——他的手中握着一根缠绕电线。一个黑色的三销接头。

En: Egg...
I pulled the plug, he says, calmly. "It was getting *too* hard core."
Zh: “我把插头拔了，”他平静地说到。“它变得有点*太*硬核了。”

En: You did good, Egg... Andre breathes a sigh of relief and inspects the window.  "Most of the place seems to be intact. Fucking L... Programmer-lady, tell me you were recording that!"
Zh: “你做的很好，凡客……”安德烈松了一口气，查看着窗户。“大部分地方好像都还完好无损。该死的……程序员小姐，别说你就是要录这玩意！”

En: Right, I'll try not to touch anything. Next question...
Zh: “好的，我尽量不去碰任何东西。下一个问题……”

En: Great. She dwells back into the glowing terminal.
Zh: “很好。”她又把头埋到那台发光的终端后面。

En: I use the AR-1 as my Rehm Prefect's processing unit.
Zh: “我用AR-1做雷姆行政官的处理单元。”

En: Somewhere underneath those roof beams, I assume. She looks up, eyes trying to pierce the pitch-black heights above, but without much success.
Zh: “我猜应该在天花板下面的某个地方吧。”她抬起头，眼睛试着看穿上方黑漆漆的空间，但是却没有成功。

En: Wait, what?
Zh: “等等，什么？”

En: I don't understand.
Zh: “我不明白。”

En: Rehm Prefect, my radiocomputer, she explains.
Zh: “雷姆行政官，我的无线电脑，”她解释到。

En: Rehm Prefect, that's your radiocomputer, right?
Zh: “雷姆行政官，那是你的无线电脑，对吧？”

En: Mhmh.
They are connected to my Rehm Prefect. She looks up. "Whatever you do, just please don't move them, okay? Thanks."
Zh: “它们跟我的雷姆行政官是相连的。”她抬起头。“不管你做什么，请不要乱动它们，好吗？谢了。”

En: How did you get your hands on it?
Zh: “你是怎么弄到的？”

En: I know a friend of a friend who used to freelance for the Coalition, she says nonchalantly, scratching her ear. "I was actually aiming for the military-grade Rehm Rational series, but couldn't find one."
Zh: “我一个朋友的朋友，曾经给联盟国做过兼职，”她淡淡地说着，抓抓了自己的耳朵。“其实我是想要一台军事级雷姆智者系列，但是找不到。”

En: No, actually it *is* kind of a big deal. You don't see Rehm Prefects in every police department for example.
Zh: 不，其实这*确实*算一件大事。比如说，你就没在每个警局都见到过雷姆行政官。

En: I'm working.
Zh: “我在工作。”

En: Working on what?
Zh: “什么工作？”

En: Could you... She closes her eyes. "Could you just... *shh* for a moment? Or get to the point -- I really need to focus on something."
Zh: “你能不能……”她闭上双眼。“你能不能……稍微*安静*一下？或者直接说出来……我真的需要集中精神。”

En: It's not *just* rudeness. It really is hard to concentrate on whatever she needs to do. And you're not helping.
Zh: 这不（仅仅）是无礼。不论她需要做什么，这样真的很难集中精神。而且你也帮不上忙。

En: Strange things may flourish in the dark...
Zh: 奇怪的东西可能会在黑暗中繁茂生长……

En: Only a faint criss-cross of rafters can be made out from the dark, most of the tower disappearing into the shade.
Zh: 在那片黑暗中，只能依稀辨别出一个十字形的椽木，塔楼的大部分都消失在了它的阴影中。

En: Nothing, I'll let you work. [Leave.]
Zh: “没什么，我不打扰你工作了。”[离开。]

En: Hey, are you the lead programmer of 'Wirrâl Untethered' by any chance?
Zh: “嘿，你不会正好是*自由维勒尔*的主程序员吧？”

En: Yes. She thinks to herself. "Or no, not anymore. That project is dead."
Zh: “是的。”她沉思。“也许不再是了。那个项目已经没戏了。”

En: I am Soona Luukanen-Kilde, the former lead programmer of Fortress Accident and RSA Radios. I have over 16 years of programming experience and I'm proficient in both Vox and Orbis languages...
Zh: “我是苏娜·卢凯伦-吉尔德，要塞事故和RSA无线电的前任主程序员。我有16年的编程经验，精通沃克斯和奥比斯语言……”

En: That still doesn't answer what she's doing in an abandoned church.
Zh: 这还是没有回答她为什么会在这个废弃教堂的问题。

En: What is it? The woman is still hunched over the keyboard, gently illuminated by the purring machine.
Zh: “什么事？”女人还缩在键盘前面，轰隆的机器在她身上洒下轻柔的光。

En: How's the project going? I see that your neighbours have moved in, but all I hear is anodic dance music.
Zh: “工程进行的怎么样了？我看见你的邻居搬进来了，不过我唯一能听到的只有阳极舞曲。”

En: Right, I'll let you work in peace now. [Leave.]
Zh: “好吧，我会让你安静工作的。”[离开。]

En: Alright, I talked to Egg Head, he plugged in the cable. You can now *unmute* your speakers.
Zh: “好吧，我跟爱凡客聊过了，他已经接通了电源。现在你的扬声器可以*解除静音*了。”

En: How do you feel about *anodic dance music*?
Zh: “你觉得*阳极舞曲*怎么样？”

En: Have you seen... *the crab-man*?
Zh: “你有没有见过……*蟹男*？”

En: Have you seen the man living up in the rafters?
Zh: “你有没有见过住在椽木上的男人？”

En: What? She snorts with disdain. "I'm not going anywhere. This is a public space and I am free to bend the circuit as I find necessary. Besides..."
Zh: “什么？”她轻蔑地哼了一声。“我哪里都不去。这是公共场所，我可以根据自己的需要随意篡改电路。再说……”

En: Oh, I see, the lieutenant says, raising a finger, "this is connected to the drug manufacturing thing."
Zh: “哦，我明白了，”警督一边说，一边竖起一只手指，“这跟制毒有关。”

En: It's really hard to be a *cop* with all these *laws*!
Zh: “有那么多些*法律*规定，做*警察*真的好难。”

En: It's the Wayfarer Act again, the lieutenant says, nodding. "You can't just throw citizens out of public spaces, at least not legally."
Zh: “又是旅人法案，”警督点头说到。“你不能把公民赶出公共空间，至少法律上不允许。”

En: And she's not. Disrupting any order, I mean.
Zh: “而且她没有。我是说，扰乱任何秩序。”

En: Evict her.
Zh: 驱逐她。

En: Pistol argument.
Zh: 手枪的争论。

En: Standard Wirrâl die.
Zh: 标准威勒尔骰子。

En: No.
Zh: “没有。”

En: But you know he's around?
Zh: “但是你知道他在附近？”

En: Yes.
Zh: “是的。”

En: The lead programmer sighs. "I can't believe they got you so easily. Go have another talk with those up-and-coming entrepreneurs, will you? Thanks."
Zh: 主程序员叹了口气。“我真是不敢相信，他们这么容易就说服你了。再去跟那些前途无量的创业家聊聊，好吗？谢谢。”

En: How do you feel about manufacturing large quantities of drugs?
Zh: “你对大规模制毒有什么看法？”

En: Sorry, but... who *are* you? What are you doing here?
Zh: “抱歉，但是……你*是*谁？你在这里做什么？”

En: All you hear is the accelerated heart rate in your ears, pounding at 100bpm. Beat by beat, your blood readies for conflict.
Zh: 你唯一能听到的，只有耳中心跳加速的声音，它正在以100心率的速度跳动着。心跳一拍接着一拍，你的血液已经为冲突准备就绪。

En: You're not really feeling it, but you should try to force it, anyway. Maybe she won't be able to tell?
Zh: 你并没有真的感觉到，不过你应该试着推一下。也许她看不出来呢？

En: What if you *didn't* have to leave? I talked to Andre, he wants to make it work.
Zh: “如果你*不用*离开呢？我跟安德烈谈过，他希望你们能和平共处。”

En: Those ravers won't be bothering you anymore -- I arrested them because they were planning a drug trade.
Zh: “那些派对狂再也不会打扰你了——我逮捕了他们，因为计划贩毒。”

En: You arrested them... She looks up, surprised. "Didn't think the cops in this place had it in them," she murmurs.
Zh: “你逮捕了他们……”她惊讶地抬起头。“没想到这个地方的警察居然还有这种胆量，”她低语着。

En: But then it stops. Totally and utterly -- as if there never was a sound. Only your ears still ring from the shock. Everybody is staring at Egg Head -- holding a dangling cable in his hand. A black three-pin connector.
Zh: 然后它停止了。完完全全，彻彻底底——就好像声音从来没有出现过。只有你的耳朵还在因为刚才的震颤嗡嗡作响。所有人都看向爱凡客的方向——他的手中握着一根缠绕电线。一个黑色的三销接头。

En: And then it stops. Totally and utterly -- as if there never was a sound. Only your ears still ring from the shock. Everybody is staring at Egg Head -- holding a dangling cable in his hand. A black three-pin connector.
Zh: 然后它停止了。完完全全，彻彻底底——就好像声音从来没有出现过。只有你的耳朵还在因为刚才的震颤嗡嗡作响。所有人都看向爱凡客的方向——他的手中握着一根缠绕电线。一个黑色的三销接头。

En: In the silence, a low hum starts creeping up your spine. It's a song inside you -- not in the speakers, not in the room. A great bass sigh in the basement of your mind. Slowly it builds, until the air around you starts to vibrate.
Zh: 一片寂静中，一阵低沉的嗡嗡声爬上你的脊椎。那是你心底的一首歌——不在扬声器里，不在这个房间。你的心底深处发出一声低沉的叹息声。慢慢变大，直到你周围的空气都开始震颤。

En: He must have seen you.
Zh: “他肯定看见你了。”

En: Okay, it's probably not a big deal then.
Zh: “好吧，那可能没什么大不了的。”

En: If you're not here to hire me, I don't really know how can I help you. She turns back to the terminal.
Zh: “如果你来这里不是为了雇佣我，我真的不知道能怎么帮你。”她转回到终端面前。

En: She doesn't seem surprised to be recognized, rather sad. Something passes over her face before she straightens her back.
Zh: 被认出来似乎并没有让她感到惊讶，而是有些悲伤。在她伸直腰之前，有什么东西从她的脸上一闪而过。

En: Yes, what is it? She doesn't look up from the keyboard.
Zh: “是的，怎么了？”她仍然低头看着键盘。

En: I didn't break anything, did I?
Zh: “我没弄坏什么吧？”

En: No, you just printed out my personal log and wasted some paper. It does not look like a big loss to her.
Zh: “没有，你只是打印了我的个人日志，浪费了些纸。”看来对她来说不算太大的损失。

En: Yes, what is it? She doesn't look up from the keyboard.
Zh: “是的，怎么了？”她仍然低头看着键盘。

En: Wait, what?
Zh: 等等，什么？

En: But does it have anything to do with necroplasmic life forms? 'Ghosts' in everyday parlance.
Zh: 但是这跟死亡等离子生命体有什么关系吗？就是日常用语中的‘幽灵’。

En: She's looking for a disruption in the *radio waves*. That's what her personal log said.
Zh: 她正在寻找*无线电波*中的干扰。她的个人日志里就是这么说的。

En: Is the hole connected to the data loss in your journal?
Zh: “这个洞跟你日志里提到的数据丢失有关联吗？”

En: Yes, that's what led me here... She stares at the burnished antenna on a nearby table. "But I suspect it might be something a bit more complicated than that."
Zh: “是的，我就是为此而来的……”她盯着附近桌子上那个光洁锃亮的天线。“但是我怀疑实际情况可能远比那个要复杂。”

En: That's all I wanted to know about the scary two-millimetre hole in the world... for now. (Conclude.)
Zh: “关于世界上那个可怕的2毫米的洞，我只想知道这么多了……暂时。”（总结。）

En: And that antenna is its... processing unit?
Zh: “那天线就是它的……处理单元？”

En: Yes. She sighs. "You really don't know anything about radiocomputers, do you?" She has stopped working now.
Zh: “是的。”她叹了口气。“你真的不了解无线电脑，是吧？”她停下手中的工作了。

En: Not really.
Zh: “不知道。”

En: I know *a little*.
Zh: “我知道*一些*。”

En: Alright, well... All radiocomputers perform operations up on air, so in order to gain more processing power you need to invest in a *good antenna*.
Zh: “那好吧……所有无线电脑都是通过广播执行操作的，所以为了获得更强大的处理能力，就必须投资一条*好天线*。”

En: I guess it is… She stops to think. "So far I've been quite satisfied with it. Martinaise is an unstable region with bad coverage and the operation has been surprisingly stable."
Zh: 她停下来想了想。“我猜是吧……目前为止我对它还算满意。马丁内斯是个不稳定的区域，覆盖范围很糟糕，不过运营倒是出奇地稳定。”

En: I don't really know much about *anything* in this world, to be honest.
Zh: “老实说，我真的不知道这个世界上的*任何*事情。”

En: And the AR-1? Is it a 'good antenna'?
Zh: “那AR-1呢？它是‘好天线’吗？”

En: But it's not the cheapest one on the market, so I wouldn't recommend it for your regular red tape operations. Fraser 1000 is a foolproof line for civilians.
Zh: “但它不是市面上最便宜的，所以我不会建议用于常规的程序修改。弗雷泽1000对于普通民众来说使用更简便。”

En: Anyway... She turns back to her terminal. "You should do some research before you decide to buy anything. Ask around, compare the prices. There are many milieus dedicated to that sort of thing."
Zh: “不管怎么说……”她转回到终端面前。“在决定买任何东西之前，你都应该做些研究。四处问问，比较一下价格。有很多电台都致力于这类事情。”

En: So why do you have it?
Zh: “那你为什么要买它呢？”

En: And you work for the government?
Zh: “你是为政府工作的吗？”

En: No, I don't.
Zh: “不，我不是。”

En: She inhales sharply, before answering in a single breath: "Because I needed something *good* for my investigation and Rehm Civic is widely agreed to be below all standards, so I had to upgrade."
Zh: 她猛地吸了一口气，然后一口气说了出来：“因为我需要一些*好东西*来进行调查，而且大家普遍认为雷姆思域低于所有标准，所以我必须升级。”

En: Besides, owning a Rehm Prefect isn't such a big deal anymore.
Zh: “再说了，搞一台雷姆行政官也不算什么大事情了。”

En: She pats her glowing machine. "Prefect is mainly based on the same technology as Rehm Civic, so it's kind of a rip-off, but it does have better compatibility with newer antenna models, so I won't complain."
Zh: 她摸了摸那台发光的机器。“行政官跟雷姆思域基本都是基于相同技术的，所以有点算是价格虚高，不过它确实能跟新版的天线模式更加兼容，所以我没什么好抱怨的。”

En: Did you *steal it*?
Zh: “你是*偷来*的吗？”

En: No! She looks up, disturbed. "No, of course not. I know a friend of a friend who used to freelance for the Coalition. I bought it from her."
Zh: “不是！”她抬起头，有些不安。“不，当然不是了。我认识的一个朋友的朋友曾经给联盟国做过兼职。我是从她那里买来的。”

En: I was actually aiming for the military-grade Rehm Rational series, but couldn't find one. She pats her machine: "Prefect is mainly based on the same technology as the Civic, but it does work better with newer antenna models, so I'm not complaining."
Zh: “其实我是想要一台军事级雷姆智者系列，但是找不到。”她摸了摸那台发光的机器。“行政官跟雷姆思域基本都是基于相同技术的，所以有点算是价格虚高，不过它确实能跟新版的天线模式更加兼容，所以我没什么好抱怨的。”

En: You really like those questions, don't you? There's a hint of amusement in her tired eyes.
Zh: “你真的很喜欢那些问题，不是吗？”她那双疲惫充血的眼睛里有一丝消遣的意味。

En: I'm a police officer, it's my job to ask questions.
Zh: “我是一个警官，我的工作就是问问题。”

En: You're occupying a public space. I need to know what you're doing here.
Zh: “你占据了一个公共空间。我需要知道你在这里做什么。”

En: They're bloodshot. She really hasn't been getting much sleep lately, has she?
Zh: 她的眼睛有些充血。她最近真的没怎么休息，不是吗？

En: There have been complaints from your neighbours.
Zh: “你的邻居有些抱怨。”

En: I need to make sure you're not hiding anyone here.
Zh: “我需要保证你没有在这里藏匿什么人。”

En: No, I'm not, it's just me and the... She looks up at the ceiling before murmuring: "...*the crab-man*. I'm just doing some research. That's all."
Zh: “不，我没有，只有我和那个……”她抬头望向天花板，然后低语到：“……*蟹男*。我只是在做些研究。仅此而已。”

En: I'm looking for the location of a two-millimetre hole in the world.
Zh: “我正在寻找世界上那个2毫米孔洞的位置。”

En: I am conducting scientific research here. You can't throw me out, she says, ready to stand her ground again.
Zh: “我在这里进行一项科学研究。你不能把我赶出去，”她说到，准备坚持自己的立场。

En: I am conducting scientific research here. You can't throw me out, she says, ready to stand her ground.
Zh: “我在这里进行一项科学研究。你不能把我赶出去，”她说到，准备坚持自己的立场。

En: What research?
Zh: “什么研究？”

En: I'm looking for the location of a two-millimetre hole in the world.
Zh: “我正在寻找世界上那个2毫米孔洞的位置。”

En: Does it have anything to do with *ghosts*?
Zh: “这跟*幽灵*有什么关系吗？”

En: That's a little above your pay grade at the moment.
Zh: 这就超过了你目前的薪金等级。

En: *Ghosts?* she repeats. "No, I don't think so, I don't believe in ghosts."
Zh: “*幽灵*？”她重复着。“不，我不这么觉得，我不相信幽灵的存在。”

En: What about other supra-natural entities?
Zh: “那其他超自然存在呢？”

En: I don't believe in them either.
Zh: “我也不相信。”

En: Why there?
Zh: “为什么呢？”

En: There's this place at the back of the church, a place where all audible vibrations seem to decease -- I've named it 'the swallow'. And the higher you go, the less you record.
Zh: “教堂后面有一个地方，在那里所有声音震动好像都会消亡——我把它叫做吞噬。而且地方越高，录到的声音越小。”

En: What not many know is: necroplasma *exists*.
Zh: “很多人不知道的是：死亡等离子是*存在*的。”

En: (Nod.) "That's probably wise. Not believing in them."
Zh: （点点头。）“这样可能比较明智吧，不相信它们的话。”

En: Great. What are we talking about? And *why*? She gets back to work -- a clear signal that this branch of the conversation is over.
Zh: “太棒了。我们在说什么来着？还有*为什么*？”她又回去工作了——这是个明显的信号：这部分对话已经结束。

En: Great. Her hands are a whirl on the keyboard. "Thanks."
Zh: “很好。”她的双手在键盘上飞速的旋转。“谢谢。”

En: Hey, about that two-millimetre hole again...
Zh: “喂，关于那个2毫米的孔洞……”

En: The swallow, you mean. She looks up, slightly irked. "What about it?"
Zh: “你是说，吞噬。”她抬起头，稍微有点厌倦。“怎么了？”

En: Now this house of worship is also going to be a drug lab... Always something fascinating happening in tents.
Zh: “现在这个教堂要变成一间制毒实验室了……帐篷里总是会发生一些让人神魂颠倒的事呢。”

En: Just to be completely clear: I am here to conduct a citizen's investigation into a radio anomaly and I want nothing to do with the local vices of Martinaise. Okay? Thanks.
Zh: “我要明确声明一点：我来这里是为了进行无线电异常的公民调查，而且我完全不想跟本地的恶习扯上任何关系，好吗？谢谢。”

En: Just to be completely clear: I am here to conduct a citizen's investigation into a radio anomaly and I want nothing to do with the local vices of Martinaise. Got it? Thanks. She turns back to work.
Zh: “我要彻底说明一点：我来这里是为了进行一次关于无线电异常的公民调查，而且我完全不想跟本地的恶习扯上任何关系。明白了吗？谢谢。”她又转回去工作了。

En: Convince her to cooperate with the ravers.
Zh: 说服她跟派对狂合作。

En: Heard Egg Head's speech.
Zh: 听爱凡客的演讲。

En: Research not going well.
Zh: 研究进行地不太顺利。

En: Discovered Fortress Accident.
Zh: 发现要塞事故。

En: Discussed FA with Dicemaker.
Zh: 跟骰子匠人讨论过FA。

En: Just tell her about Egg Head's defence speech.
Zh: 把爱凡客的辩护词告诉她。

En: Easy -- when her research is done, she can move out.
Zh: 放松点——等她的研究完成之后，再搬出去也不迟。

En: Listen, about that nightclub...
Zh: “听着，关于夜店的事……”

En: I already told you -- I'm not interested.
Zh: “我已经告诉过你了——我不感兴趣。”

En: Those kids really need a win. We all do. Give this club a chance -- give them a chance.
Zh: “那些孩子需要一场胜利。我们都需要。给夜店一个机会吧——给我一个机会。”

En: Listen, about your research... Maybe I can help with anything? Help you refill those water basins or something...
Zh: “听着，关于你的研究……也许我可以帮上忙？帮你给水盆加水，或者什么的……”

En: What? She looks up from her work, disoriented. "No, I don't really need any help with the project."
Zh: “什么？”她停下工作抬起头，有点迷惑。“不，我这个工程不需要任何帮助。”

En: But if I could help you finish the project then you wouldn't have to live in a church next to the boom-boom anymore -- just think about it.
Zh: “但是如果我能帮你完成这个工程，你就不用住在一间教堂里，隔壁天天动次打次响个不停——你考虑一下。”

En: Alright, she finally says, blinking twice. "Bring me the game's off-site copy from my old workspace if you really want to help. It's stored on a filament memory and I'm unable to go and fetch it myself."
Zh: “好吧，”她总算开了口，眨了两下眼睛。“如果你真想帮忙的话，那就去我之前的旧办公地点把游戏的场外拷贝拿过来。它存在一个纤维内存上，而且我自己没办法亲自去取。”

En: She thinks about it, a glassy look in her eyes. A gust of wind brings more snow in from the broken gallery. It touches her hair.
Zh: 她想了想，脸上的表情有些呆滞。一阵风夹杂着雪花吹进这个破败的走廊，碰触着她的头发。

En: What if I helped you *finish* your project? Then you could leave -- and they could come in.
Zh: “如果我帮你*完成*这个工程呢？然后你可以离开——然后他们可以搬进来。”

En: That's a bit biased, don't you think?
Zh: “这就有点片面了，你不觉得吗？”

En: No. She stares at you with droopy eyes. "She literally started praying for the higher powers when she first saw my Rehm Civic. I'm not making this up."
Zh: “不。”她用无神的双眼盯着你。“当她第一次看见我的雷姆思域时，她真的开始向至高力量祈祷了，就是字面上的意思。我不是瞎编的。”

En: Once I came in one morning only to find that my terminal was full of those strange... trinkets and amulets. *Wards*. It looked like some Semenine magic.
Zh: “有一天早上，我发现自己的终端上堆满了那些奇怪的……小玩意和护身符。*守护*。看起来像是某种塞美尼魔法。”

En: Don't worry, you can't miss the bear. She gives you a rueful smile. "It's hideous."
Zh: “别担心，你不能错过那只熊的。”她露出一个悲伤的笑容。“它实在是太惨不忍睹了。”

En: You might be more familiar with the name *Doomed Commercial Area*. You can get in through the bookshop on plaza. You just have to do some explaining to the bookstore lady.
Zh: “你可能对*诅咒商业区*这个名字更熟悉吧。你可以通过广场上的那家书店穿进去。只要能想办法跟书店女店主解释就行。”

En: Yeah, people in Martinaise don't really like to *get with the times*.
Zh: “是啊，马丁内斯人真的不太喜欢*与时俱进*。”

En: She literally started praying for the higher powers when she first saw my Rehm Civic. I'm not making this up.
Zh: “当她第一次看见我的雷姆思域时，她真的开始向至高力量祈祷了，就是字面上的意思。我不是瞎编的。”

En: Don't be too harsh. That's only because of their *socio-economic* situation.
Zh: “不要太苛刻。那只不过是因为他们的*社会经济*状况造成的。”

En: Yes, but she *literally* started praying for the higher powers when she first saw my Rehm Civic. I'm not making this up.
Zh: “是的，不过当她第一次看见我的雷姆思域时，她真的开始向至高力量祈祷了，就是*字面上*的意思。我不是瞎编的。”

En: Wait, a note from *whom*? She freezes. "Did it specify where they took the filament memory?"
Zh: 她僵住了。“等等，*谁*写的纸条？有没有说明他们把纤维内存带到哪里去了？”

En: It said the off-site copy had been taken to a nearby ice cream maker. The note was signed by someone named Sulisław.
Zh: “上面说场外拷贝被放到附近的冰淇淋制造机里了。纸条上的署名人是苏里罗。”

En: And the Heads -- I won't even get *into* the Heads, millions of them... She stops. "Go find that copy from that ice cream maker, will you? Thanks."
Zh: “还有头——我甚至都没*接入*那些头，有好几百万个……”她停了下来。“去冰淇淋制造机里把它找出来，好吗？谢了。”

En: Wait, what? She looks up, alarmed. "*Whose* dead body?"
Zh: “等等，什么？”她警觉地抬起头。“*谁*的尸体？”

En: It's the body of the hanged man.
Zh: “那个吊人的尸体。”

En: Don't worry, I put it there -- temporarily. It's all part of an official police investigation.
Zh: “别担心，我把它放在那里了——暂时而已。这些都是警察官方调查的一部分。”

En: *And what is it doing in the fridge?!*
Zh: “*那你把它放进冰箱里做什么*？！”

En: Just wanted to let you know.
Zh: “只是想告诉你。”

En: Yeah, that's what I said in the first place.
Zh: “是啊，我一开始也是这么说的。”

En: The investigation warranted it.
Zh: “调查批准的。”

En: Okay, she says, pressing fingers into her eyebrow ridge. "Very cool, thanks for keeping me in the loop."
Zh: “好吧，”她一边说着，一边用手指按压自己的眉头。“很好，谢谢你把情况告诉我。”

En: This is getting ridiculous. Can't you just defrost it? Or I don't know, I don't know about the ice cream maker... just figure something out.
Zh: “这太荒谬了。你就不能解冻吗？或者，我不知道，我也不懂那个冰淇淋制造机……想点办法啊。”

En: Thanks. She takes the filament, inspecting the metallic tape on its side. "Looks like it's the one."
Zh: “谢谢。”她接过纤维内存，查看了一下旁边的金属胶带。“看来就是它了。”

En: What's going to happen now?
Zh: “接下来会怎么样呢？”

En: Now I'm going to print it out to see what's left of it. She's already inserted the filament into the radiocomputer's core, ready to close the door.
Zh: “现在我要把它打印出来，看看里面还剩下什么。”她已经把纤维内存插入无线电脑核心，准备关上舱门。

En: She starts laughing, but stops almost immediately, her voice oddly humourless: "No, it's not dangerous, just depressing."
Zh: 她笑了起来，不过突然停了下来，声音正经地有些古怪：“不，它不危险，只是压抑。”

En: What are you even hoping to find?
Zh: “你到底指望发现些什么？”

En: How do you know?
Zh: “你怎么知道的？”

En: I have a theory, she says, as the filament clicks into place, "Lintel was able to divine the anomaly's location from this broken copy. I want to repeat their calculation, only this time with better equipment."
Zh: “我有一个理论，”她回复着，纤维内存正好也嵌入进去。“林内尔能够从这个坏掉的拷贝里预测出异常地点。我想重复它们的计算，只是这次会用更好的设备。”

En: Watch, she says and presses PRINT on the machine's keyboard, "what an intricate display of failure."
Zh: “看着，”她一边说，一边按下机器键盘上的打印按钮，“多么复杂的显示故障啊。”

En: Watch, she presses PRINT on the machine's keyboard, "what an intricate display of failure."
Zh: “看着，”她一边说，一边按下机器键盘上的打印按钮，“多么复杂的显示故障啊。”

En: What do you mean?
Zh: “你是什么意思？”

En: The paper gets soaked in ink again, its evil blackness shining in the light of the mainframe. It looks eerie.
Zh: 纸张再次被墨水浸透，那邪恶的黑暗在主机箱的灯光下闪闪发光。看起来很是怪异。

En: Watch, she presses PRINT on the machine's keyboard, "what an intricate display of failure."
Zh: “看着，”她一边说，一边按下机器键盘上的打印按钮，“多么复杂的显示故障啊。”

En: The paper starts filling out with ink, soaking it in a gleaming blackness. Not a single line of data stands out.
Zh: 纸张渐渐被墨水铺满，浸泡在一片闪亮的黑色中。没有哪一行数据是特别突出的。

En: The paper starts filling out with ink, soaking it in a gleaming darkness. Not a single line of data stands out.
Zh: 纸张渐渐被墨水铺满，浸泡在一片闪亮的黑色中。没有哪一行数据是特别突出的。

En: Alright, got your message.
Zh: “好吧，收到你的信息了。”

En: Nope, not interested in your degenerate money schemes.
Zh: “不，我对你堕落的赚钱计划不感兴趣。”

En: Listen, how about we offer you part of the profit?
Zh: “听着，如果我们给你一部分利润怎么样？”

En: Hey, what makes the world go round?
Zh: 喂，到底是什么让这个世界转动的？

En: No, that's a bad idea -- don't say anything.
Zh: 不，这是个坏主意——什么也不要说。

En: She stares at the terminal, completely oblivious to her surroundings.
Zh: 她盯着终端，完全忽略了周围的环境。

En: Good. She gets back to work.
Zh: “很好。”她又回头工作了。

En: Where? (Lean closer.)
Zh: “哪里？”（靠近一些。）

En: I can't see anything.
Zh: “我什么也没看见。”

En: Hold on. She's behind the keyboard now, typing in some numbers that only she understands. The terminal beeps, and the light inside starts pulsing like a glowing heart.
Zh: “等一下。”她站到键盘背后，输入着某些只有她自己能理解的数字。终端开始发出哔哔的响声，里面的光线像一颗发光的心脏跳动起来。

En: Can I do anything?
Zh: “我能做些什么吗？”

En: I'm still not sure it's a good idea -- tinkering with the unknown...
Zh: “我还是不确定这是不是个好主意——摆弄未知的东西……”

En: (Say nothing.)
Zh: （什么也不说。）

En: Shh... just give me a second, I'm almost... She clocks up her typing speed.
Zh: “嘘……等我几秒，我就快……”她加快了打字速度。

En: The lieutenant leans closer to whisper: "I've never witnessed a programmer work before..."
Zh: 警督靠近了一些，低语着：“我之前还从没见过程序员工作……”

En: Done! Soona jumps up from the keyboard like a spring. "I've got it, I've found the location of the anomaly!" There's joy in her voice as she bumps her fist into air.
Zh: “搞定！”她像弹簧一样从键盘上跳了起来。“我明白了，我发现异常的位置了！”她把拳头举向空中，声音里满是喜悦。

En: Just give me a second, she murmurs, "I'm almost..." She clocks up her typing speed.
Zh: “给我几秒钟，”她嘟囔着，“我就快……”她加快了打字速度。

En: You did it? You found the coordinates?!
Zh: “你做到了？你发现坐标了？！”

En: Oh my god, congratulations!
Zh: “天啦，恭喜你！”

En: So -- where is it? Where is your two-millimetre hole in the world? (Proceed.)
Zh: “所以——它在哪？世界上那个2毫米的孔洞在哪？”（继续。）

En: I found the coordinates! She lets out a celebratory laugh.
Zh: “我发现坐标了！”她发出欢庆的笑声。

En: This is embarrassing...
Zh: 这就尴尬了……

En: It's that white dot! In the same place I saw it before when I printed it out.
Zh: “就是那个白点！跟我之前把它打印出来的时候看到的那个地方一样！”

En: Thank you! she yells back with a grin.
Zh: “谢谢你！”她笑着大声回应着。

En: There. In the swallow. She points at the other end of the church where a group of water bowls forms a ritualistic arch.
Zh: “那里。”她指向教堂另一边，那里有一堆水盆，组成了一个很有仪式感的拱形。“就在吞噬里。”

En: Think you can help me again? She tilts her head, eyes sparkling.
Zh: “你还能再帮我个忙吗？”她歪着头，眼睛闪闪发光。

En: Sure.
Zh: “当然可以。”

En: Do I have a choice?
Zh: “我有的选吗？”

En: I need you to go move those water bowls for me, I need to double-check my calculations.
Zh: “我需要你帮我移动那些水盆，我要再复核一下自己的计算。”

En: Just walk over to the circle and follow my instructions: Move the third bowl two centimetres to the left and the fourth bowl five centimetres to the right. This should do the trick.
Zh: “只要走到那边的圆圈里，然后听从我的指示：把第三个水盆向左移动2厘米，第四个水盆向右移动5厘米。这样应该就可以了。”

En: Can you please just go and move the water bowls for me? Thanks.
Zh: “你能不能直接过去帮我移动水盆呢？谢谢。”

En: Great, everything should be aligned now... She stops, biting into her chapped lip.
Zh: “很好，现在一切都排列好了……”她停了下来，咬紧干裂的嘴唇。

En: Miss Know-It-All is hesitating.
Zh: 万事通小姐在犹豫。

En: What's wrong?
Zh: “出什么事了。”

En: Okay, let's get this party started!
Zh: “好吧，那就让派对开始吧！”

En: Yeah, she snaps out of the lull. "Nothing. Now the only thing left to do is to unmute the headphones."
Zh: “是啊，”她打破了平静，“没什么。现在唯一要做的就是解除耳机静音。”

En: Yeah, she snaps out of the lull. "Let's do it. Now the only thing left to do is to unmute the headphones."
Zh: “是啊，”她打破了平静，“没什么。现在唯一要做的就是解除耳机静音。”

En: If we've got the location right, we should then be able to hear whatever sound this anomaly makes.
Zh: “如果地点没错，那应该就能听到异常发出的声音了。”

En: I mean, what sound does the nothing make? How can you even listen to something that doesn't exist? She turns to face you, the mainframe throwing shadows on her chin.
Zh: “我是说，*无*会发出什么声音？你到底怎么才能听到不存在东西的声音呢？”她面对着你，主机箱在她的下巴上投下阴影。

En: What if silence is only what surrounds it, but the swallow itself is... She grows silent, her face very pale in the cold light of electricity.
Zh: “如果它周围只有寂静，但是吞噬本身……”她沉默了，脸庞在冰冷灯光的照射下显得十分苍白。

En: What?
Zh: “怎么了？”

En: You're overthinking this.
Zh: “你想多了。”

En: You're right, we should be cautious -- we don't know what we're dealing with here.
Zh: “你说得对，我们应该小心点——我们不知道自己面对的是什么。”

En: Maybe. She rubs her face. "Maybe I'm just tired."
Zh: “也许吧。”她揉了揉自己的脸。“也许我只是太累了。”

En: Why assume that something that has no presence, existence or qualities has to be terrifying?
Zh: “为什么要假设一个没有存在，实体或者质量的东西就一定是可怕的呢？”

En: Yeah... She breathes in. "You're right, let's do it." She puts on her oversized headphones, ready to press UNMUTE on the keyboard...
Zh: “是啊……”她吸了一口气。“你说得对，我们开始吧。”她戴上硕大的耳机，准备按下键盘上的解除静音按钮……

En: Hey, I don't think I want to do it anymore... seems too *dangerous*. (Slowly start to back off.)
Zh: “喂，我不想再这么做了……好像有点太过*危险*。”（开始慢慢退后。）

En: No... She breathes in, determined. "We *have* to do it." She puts on her oversized headphones, ready to press UNMUTE on the keyboard...
Zh: “不……”她深吸一口气，做下决定。“我们*必须*这么做。”她戴上硕大的耳机，准备按下键盘上的解除静音按钮……

En: Nothing happens as Soona Luukanen-Kilde presses unmute on her keyboard. Nothing but silence.
Zh: 苏娜·卢凯伦-吉尔德按下键盘上的解除静音按钮，但是什么也没发生。只有一片寂静。

En: Can you hear anything?
Zh: “你能听见什么吗？”

En: (Say nothing.)
Zh: (什么也不说。)

En: She doesn't answer, her eyes closed and brows knitted together in a state of deep focus, one hand cupping the headphone.
Zh: 她没有回答，双眼紧闭，眉头紧锁，高度集中，一只手捂住了耳机。

En: Well?
Zh: “怎么样？”

En: She doesn't talk, her eyes closed and brows knitted together in a state of deep focus, one hand cupping the headphone.
Zh: 她没有说话，双眼紧闭，眉头紧锁，高度集中，一只手捂住了耳机。

En: Damnit. She lets out a loud sigh, before tearing off her headphones; she's still avoiding your gaze.
Zh: “见鬼。”她大声地叹了一口气，然后扯下头上的耳机；她依然在回避你的目光。

En: Come on, did you hear anything?
Zh: “说啊，你听见什么了吗？”

En: Well, at least the world seems to be still alright. (Look around.)
Zh: “呃，至少这个世界好像还好。”（看看周围。）

En: Yeah, yeah, she says, disappointed. "Nothing happened, let's move on."
Zh: “是啊，是啊，”她失望地说到，“什么也没发生，我们继续吧。”

En: No, of course not, she says, clearly disappointed. "Nothing happened, let's move on."
Zh: “不，当然没有，”她说到，显然很是失望，“什么也没发生，我们继续吧。”

En: What do you mean 'nothing happened'? Did you find the swallow?
Zh: “你说‘什么也没发生’是什么意思？你发现吞噬了吗？”

En: No. She rests her face on her hands, massaging the forehead.
Zh: “没有。”她把脸埋进双手，按摩着前额。

En: You said that the research isn't going well. Why not?
Zh: “你说研究进行地不太顺利。为什么呢？”

En: Because it's just trial and error, trying to locate the swallow -- the exact point in space.
Zh: “因为这是尝试错误法，试着定位吞噬——空间中一个特定的点。”

En: And I don't have a... She stops mid-sentence. "You know what, it would be really helpful if you could just stop talking and let me work."
Zh: “而且我没有……”她中途停了下来。“你知道吗，如果你闭上嘴让我安心工作的话，那就真是再好不过了。”

En: Listen, about your research... You mentioned earlier that it's not going very well. Maybe I can help with something?
Zh: “听着，关于你的研究……你之前提过说进行地不太顺利。也许我能帮到你？”

En: We clear? She stares at you with pleading, furious eyes.
Zh: “说清楚了吗？”她用恳求而愤怒的眼神盯着你。

En: Ghosts, speak to me!
Zh: 幽灵，跟我说说话！

En: Everything disappears. You are draped in silence like a drowning man staring into his puny little headspace.
Zh: 一切都消失了。你像是一个溺水的人，凝视着顶部微不足道的小小空间，整个人被寂静包围。

En: Soona... (Take off the headphones.) "What if we just need a better sound system?"
Zh: “苏娜……”（取下耳机。）“如果我们只是需要一套更好的音响系统呢？”

En: A better sound system? she repeats. "Alright... But where would we get one?"
Zh: “更好的音响系统？”她重复着。“好吧……但是该去哪里找呢？”

En: Of course, the speedfreaks... They have a *fantastic* sound system. And you think they would help me?
Zh: “当然了，那些瘾君子……他们有一套*极好的*音响系统。所以你觉得他们会愿意帮我吗？”

En: What she means is that she's ready to turn a blind eye if the need arises.
Zh: 她的意思是说，如果有需要的话，她准备好睁一只眼闭一只眼了。

En: It's shaking the building's foundation. The floor twists...
Zh: 它在震动建筑的地基。地板开始扭曲……

En: No, I'm a master circuit-bender, I know *everything*.
Zh: “不，我是一个电路创意改装大师，我*什么*都知道。”

En: Then you *certainly* know that all radiocomputers perform operations on air, so in order to gain more processing power you need to invest in a good antenna.
Zh: “你*肯定*知道所有无线电脑都是通过广播执行操作的，所以为了获得更强大的处理能力，就必须投资一条好天线。”

En: You don't *feel* scary? What are you, some kind of *diva*?
Zh: 你没有*感觉*到害怕？你是什么，某种*女神*吗？

En: Fuck your things. Get out now.
Zh: “让你的东西见鬼去吧。马上出去。”

En: She turns off her computer and leaves.
Zh: 她关掉电脑，然后离开了。

En: The lieutenant sighs, but says nothing.
Zh: 警督叹了口气，但是什么也没说。

En: Is that *really* necessary, detective?
Zh: “*真的*有这个必要吗，警探？”

En: Cradled in her arms are a pair of glowing lungs, clearly visible from underneath her flowing dress. \n\nYou should kneel.
Zh: 她的双臂之间拥着一对闪闪发光的肺，从她那飘逸的长裙下正好看了个清楚。\n\n你应该跪下。

En: She acknowledges the passing of someone who is still alive...
Zh: 她承认仍然活着的某个人已经去世了……

En: You.
Zh: 那个人就是你。

En: The mother of humanism stands above you -- a precious and complex wax painting on a single pane of glass. A crack runs across the length of her body, her face oval and sad.
Zh: 人道主义之母站在你上方——那是印在一面玻璃上的一副珍贵而又复杂的蜡画。一道裂缝贯穿了她的整个身体，她的脸庞是椭圆形的，上面写满悲伤。

En: Look up.
Zh: 向上看。

En: The woman looks down at you kneeling. She towers among her followers: architects, laymen, courtiers. There is a sad smile on her lips and a glint in her green blue eye -- of what? Compassion? Remorse?
Zh: 女人俯视着下跪的你。她高高耸立在自己的信徒之中：建筑师，普通教徒，侍臣。她的嘴角露出一抹悲伤的笑容，蓝绿色的眼睛里闪烁着一道微光——它代表着什么？怜悯？懊悔？

En: The woman looks down at you standing there. She towers among her followers: architects, laymen, courtiers. There is a sad smile on her lips and a glint in her green-blue eye -- of what? Compassion? Remorse?
Zh: 女人俯视着站立的你。她高高耸立在自己的信徒之中：建筑师，普通教徒，侍臣。她的嘴角露出一抹悲伤的笑容，蓝绿色的眼睛里闪烁着一道微光——它代表着什么？怜悯？懊悔？

En: The woman looks down at you kneeling. She towers among her followers: architects, laymen, courtiers. There is a sad smile on her lips and a glint in her green-blue eye -- of what? Compassion? Remorse?
Zh: 女人俯视着下跪的你。她高高耸立在自己的信徒之中：建筑师，普通教徒，侍臣。她的嘴角露出一抹悲伤的笑容，蓝绿色的眼睛里闪烁着一道微光——它代表着什么？怜悯？懊悔？

En: No.
Zh: 不。

En: Close your eyes first.
Zh: 先闭上双眼。

En: The world is silent, but for the creaks and cracks of the massive wooden structure behind you. It covers you from the wind outside.
Zh: 世界一片寂静，只有你身后的巨大木制结构在嘎吱作响。它替你挡住了外面的寒风。

En: In the darkness, you sense her eyes on you, inspecting you with their multicoloured glass. As if you're a bug under a microscope.
Zh: 在黑暗之中，你感觉到她在注视着你，和那些五光十色的玻璃一起审视着你。就好像你是显微镜下的一只小虫。

En: Cold air seeps in through the crack in the glass, cooling the sweat on your forehead. Makes you shudder...
Zh: 冷风从玻璃上的裂缝里钻了进来，给你前额的汗水降了温。让你开始颤栗……

En: The ocean feels distant. Its ebb and flow blocked off by the centuries-old pinewood sarcophagus around you.
Zh: 海洋感觉很遥远。潮起潮落的声音被你周围数百年历史的松木石棺挡住了。

En: No, she doesn't care about you. She only cares about her shiny sovereign's orb and her silk robes and getting to the aerodrome on time -- to *leave*!
Zh: 不，她不在乎你。她只在乎那颗象征着至高权力的闪亮球体，她的丝绸长袍，还有准时赶到机场——为了*离去*！

En: This is Her Innocence Dolores Dei.
Zh: 这就是无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛。

En: Cold wind seeps in from the crack in the glass. Snowdrifts cover the floorboards at your feet. Above, you feel her multicoloured eyes on you. Inspecting you.
Zh: 冷风从玻璃上的裂缝里钻了进。雪花盖住了你脚下的地板。你感觉她那双五光十色的眼睛在上面注视着你，审视着你。

En: Look up.
Zh: 向上看。

En: No.
Zh: 不。

En: As if under a microscope.
Zh: 就好像在显微镜下一样。

En: No. I am not your bug.
Zh: 不。我不是你的小虫。

En: It's compassion.
Zh: 这是同情。

En: It's remorse.
Zh: 这是懊悔。

En: It's mourning.
Zh: 这是哀痛。

En: It's not possible to live.
Zh: 根本不可能活下去。

En: As that soft word passes through your mind, the lieutenant draws an x-shaped cross from shoulder to shoulder.
Zh: 那句温柔的话语掠过你的脑海，警督在两肩上划了一个X形的十字。

En: As that great and desperate thought passes through your mind, the lieutenant draws an x-shaped cross from shoulder to shoulder.
Zh: 那个伟大而又绝望的想法掠过你的脑海，警督在两肩上划了一个X形的十字。

En: As that bitter thought passes through your head, the lieutenant draws an x-shaped cross from shoulder to shoulder. With three fingers.
Zh: 那个苦涩的思绪掠过你的脑海，警督在两肩上划了一个X形的十字，用的三根手指。

En: That bitter thought passes through your mind as you stand in the apricot-coloured light of the window. The woman keeps smiling her distant smile above you, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中，脑海中掠过那道苦涩的思绪。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: That great and desperate thought passes through your mind as you stand in the apricot-coloured light of the window. Above you, the woman keeps smiling her distant smile, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中，任由那个伟大而又绝望的想法掠过你的脑海。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: That soft word passes through your mind as you stand in the apricot-coloured light of the window. The woman keeps smiling her distant smile, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中，脑海中掠过那句温柔的话语。女人脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: As that useless word passes through your mind, the lieutenant draws an x-shaped cross from shoulder to shoulder.
Zh: 那句无用的话语掠过你的脑海，警督在两肩上划了一个X形的十字。

En: That lonely word passes through your mind as you stand in the apricot-coloured light of the window. The woman keeps smiling her distant smile above you, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中，脑海中掠过那句孤寂的话语。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: As that terrible word passes through your mind, the lieutenant draws an x-shaped cross from shoulder to shoulder.
Zh: 那句可怕的话语掠过你的脑海，警督在两肩上划了一个X形的十字。

En: That terrible word rings through your mind as you stand in the apricot coloured-light of the window. The woman keeps smiling her distant smile above you, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中，那句可怕的话语在你脑海中响起。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: Do the same as you get up.
Zh: 起身时也做同样的事情。

En: Your fingertips touch your chest four times. Then you rise from your knees, into the apricot-coloured light of the window. Above you, the woman still smiles her distant smile, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你用指尖碰触胸口四次。然后站起身来，沐浴在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: Who is this, Kim?
Zh: “这是谁，金？”

En: This is Dolores Dei.
Zh: “这是德洛莉丝·黛。”

En: I'm not doing that cross either.
Zh: 我不会比划那个十字的。

En: Make the same gesture.
Zh: 学做相同的手势。

En: Good call. She left us all in the shits, don't give her a single thing. Just stand there in the apricot-coloured light of the window, secretly grinding your teeth. The woman smiles her distant smile, unmoved, struck in half by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 好主意。她把我们全都留在这个狗屁地方，什么都不要给她。只要站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中，背地里狠狠磨着牙。女人的脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容，无动于衷，被玻璃上的裂缝撕成了两半。

En: Your fingertips touch your chest four times. When it's done, you're still standing in the apricot-coloured light of the window. The woman smiles her distant smile, unmoved, struck in half by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你用指尖碰触胸口四次。做完之后，你仍然站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒之中。。女人的脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容，无动于衷，被玻璃上的裂缝撕成了两半。

En: Serves her right. She only cares about her sovereign's orb and her silk robes and getting to the aerodrome on time -- to *leave*.
Zh: 她活该。她只在乎那颗象征着至高权力的闪亮球体，她的丝绸长袍，还有准时赶到机场——为了*离去*！

En: Leave me alone, woman. [Leave.]
Zh: 离我远点，女人。[离开。]

En: There she still stands. In white, silver, and apricot, the young mother of humankind. A crack runs across her body. She is impossibly tall, oval-faced and sad -- a dark and radiant majesty.
Zh: 她依然站立在那里。白色、银色和杏色的彩陶器上，是那年轻的人道主义之母。一道裂缝从她身上穿过。她高大的难以置信，椭圆形的脸庞略显悲伤——带着一种黑暗而又光芒四射的威严。

En: The old woman in the village was right... He looks around. "This must be the Dolorian Church of Humanity in Martinaise -- or the Small Pinewood Church in some records..."
Zh: “村子里那个老妇人说得对……”他看了看周围。“这里一定就是马丁内斯的德洛莉丝人道主义教堂——又或者，在某些记录中，是一座小型的松木教堂……”

En: You knew of the place?
Zh: “你知道这个地方吗？”

En: It's a minor landmark, not easy to find. Most maps misplace it... He lowers his voice. "It was built not long after Revachol's founding, 300 or so years ago by first-generation settlers."
Zh: “它算是个小地标吧，不太容易找到。大部分地图都把位置搞错了……”他压低声音。“差不多300年前，第一批定居者在瑞瓦肖成立不久之后就把它建成了。”

En: What else do you know?
Zh: “你还知道些什么？”

En: There used to be seven stave churches on the coast. Les Sept Soeurs they call them -- The Seven Sisters. Only one remains. The rest were burnt in the Revolution, or used for building materials.
Zh: “海岸上曾经有7座木质教堂。他们称之为七姐妹。只有一座剩了下来。其他的都在大革命中烧毁了，或者拿去做了建筑材料。”

En: We should be respectful here, although the building appears to be deserted. I do not believe we'll find the instigator here. Something else, perhaps... He looks at the machinery lying around.
Zh: “虽然建筑好像已经废弃了，我们在这里还是应该恭敬一些。我不太相信能在这里找到教唆犯。也许会有些别的吧……”他看着地上的那些机器。

En: We should be respectful here, although the building appears to be deserted. We have already found the instigator -- I'm not sure what else we could want from here... He looks around.
Zh: “虽然建筑好像已经废弃了，我们在这里还是应该恭敬一些。我们已经找到教唆犯了——我不确定还能在这里找到些什么……”他四下看了看。

En: Yes. He looks around. "I wasn't sure before, but this must be the Dolorian Church of Humanity in Martinaise -- it's called the Small Pinewood Church in some records..."
Zh: “是的。”他四下看了看。“我之前还不确定，不过这肯定是马丁内斯的德洛莉丝人道主义教堂——在某些记录中，这里被称作小松木教堂……”

En: On the coast of an uninhabited archipelago, where only animals had roamed before. In the wild reeds.
Zh: 海岸上有一个无人居住的列岛，以前只有动物在那里漫步，就在那片野生的芦苇丛中。

En: There it is again -- a small pang of guilt. It's time to ask him what happened here.
Zh: 又来了——内疚引起的剧痛。是时候问问他这里发生过什么了。

En: A pang of guilt? The lieutenant is leaving something out.
Zh: 内疚引起的剧痛？警督在掩饰什么东西。

En: Respectful? Is the lieutenant a follower of Dolorianism?
Zh: 恭敬？警督也是德洛莉丝主义的追随者吗？

En: Do you know *why* it was abandoned?
Zh: “你知道这里*为什么*会废弃吗？”

En: Better to not intrude upon him.
Zh: 最好不要打扰他。

En: Cold air seeps in through the crack in the glass. The woman is keeping her colourful eye on both of you.
Zh: 冷风从玻璃上的裂缝里钻了进来。女人那双五彩斑斓的眼睛还停留在你们俩身上。

En: I have a theory, yes. There's a pause, then he continues: "There was a police raid a while back. I heard the place was shot to pieces."
Zh: “是的，我有一个理论。”他停顿了一下，然后继续说到：“不久之前，这里发生过一次警察突袭。我听说这个地方被打的粉碎。”

En: The old woman in the village was being tactful with us when she didn't mention it. She has more respect for the RCM than many around here.
Zh: “村子里那个老妇人挺机智的，没有在我们面前提这个。她比这里很多人都要尊敬RCM。”

En: You're not sure?
Zh: “你不确定？”

En: If I was here, I should find out what I was doing. (Proceed.)
Zh: “如果我来过这里，我应该搞明白自己做过什么。”（继续。）

En: Better to forget about it. (Proceed.)
Zh: “最好还是忘了吧。”（继续。）

En: Who conducted this raid?
Zh: “是谁指挥那次突袭的？”

En: Well... your station was involved, I hear. Although I can't be sure.
Zh: “呃……我听说，你们分局有牵涉其中。不过我不太确定。”

En: How come the lieutenant isn't sure? Is this confidential information?
Zh: 一名警督怎么会不确定呢？这是保密信息吗？

En: Three Precincts were involved in the raid and people say Precinct 41 was one of them.
Zh: “那次突袭有三个分局参加，大家说41分局也是其中之一。”

En: I don't remember being here.
Zh: “我不记得来过这里。”

En: I... (Look around.) "I guess I could have been here."
Zh: “我……”（看看四周。）“我猜我可能来过这里。”

En: I am pretty sure it was a clandestine operation. I don't know anything more about it. Why it was conducted, or who participated... I try not to pry into extra-district matters.
