Zh: “我很肯定那是一次秘密行动。更多的事我就不知道了。为什么要指挥那次行动，或者有谁参与其中……我尽量不去打听其他分局的事情。”

En: I'm sorry, I'm not saying you were... There's a pause. "It was a clandestine operation. I don't know anything about it. Why it was conducted or who participated. I try not to pry into extra-district matters."
Zh: “很抱歉，我不是说你……”他停顿了一下。“那是一次秘密行动。我知道的不多。为什么要指挥那次行动，或者有谁参与其中。我尽量不去打听其他分局的事情。”

En: Good luck. You will not get information on a confidential operation from your station secretary just by calling. If you really don't remember -- it might be better to keep this one forgotten.
Zh: “祝你好运。这种秘密行动的信息是不能给警局秘书打个电话就能拿到的。如果你真的不记得了——那可能还是忘记的好。”

En: I agree. It happened a while ago. It's unimportant to our business in Martinaise now.
Zh: “我同意。那件事已经过去有一段时间了。对我们现在在马丁内斯进行的调查并不重要。”

En: It happened a while ago. It's unimportant to our business in Martinaise now.
Zh: “那件事已经过去有一段时间了。对我们现在在马丁内斯进行的调查并不重要。”

En: Kim, are you a follower of Dolorianism?
Zh: “金，你是德洛莉丝主义的追随者吗？”

En: Yes. We all are. Her name, body, and rule are synonymous with humanism. The laws we enforce are Dolorian in origin.
Zh: “是的。我们全都是。她的名字，身体和规则就是人道主义的同义词。我们执行的法律就是起源于德洛莉丝的。”

En: It's not spiritual. It's constitutional. The Dolorian system does not demand faith -- only accordance.
Zh: “这不是宗教和精神上的。这是宪法上的。德洛莉丝体系不要求信仰——只有遵从。”

En: I didn't think you were spiritual.
Zh: “我觉得你不像有宗教信仰的人啊。”

En: Hmm... (Stroke your chin first.)
Zh: “嗯……”（先摸摸你的下巴。）

En: I don't like her. She looks like a *leaver*.
Zh: “我不喜欢她。她看起来像是个*退出者*。”

En: I have no idea what you mean by that. We've been standing here long enough. We should look around, or get out.
Zh: “我不知道你这话是什么意思。我们已经在这里站得够久了。我们应该四处看看，或者出去。”

En: The woman looks by in silence, smiling enigmatically.
Zh: 女人沉默地看着你，脸上带着让人捉摸不透的笑容。

En: It's Dolores Dei... he says in a hushed voice. "I believe we are in the Dolorian Church of Humanity in Martinaise. Or the Small Pinewood Church in some records."
Zh: “那是德洛莉丝·黛……”他的声音庄严而又肃穆。“我相信这里是马丁内斯的德洛莉丝人道主义教堂。又或者，在某些记录中是一座小松木教堂。”

En: It's Dolores Dei... he says in a hushed voice. "The old woman was right. This is the Dolorian Church of Humanity in Martinaise. Or the Small Pinewood Church in some records."
Zh: “那是德洛莉丝·黛……”他的声音庄严而又肃穆。“我相信这里是马丁内斯的德洛莉丝人道主义教堂。又或者，在某些记录中是一座小松木教堂。”

En: She was an almost preternaturally magnetic and intelligent individual. To her contemporaries she appeared out of time, a messenger from the future of the species. We *all* fell in love with her, head over heels. Even before she was declared an innocence her influence was tremendous.
Zh: 她是一个具有超凡魅力和聪明才智的人。对于她的同辈人来说，她像是超脱了那个时代，是来自未来人类的信使。我们*全都*爱上了她，为她神魂颠倒。甚至在她正式当选为无罪女王之前，她的影响力就已经大得惊人。

En: No. You *must* know.
Zh: 不。你*肯定*知道。

En: A motor carriage? A gunshot? Someone falling into it? Or maybe just hooligans looking for something to break.
Zh: 一辆汽車？一声枪响？有人掉进去了？或者也许只是一群想搞点破坏的小流氓。

En: Open your eyes.
Zh: 睁开双眼。

En: Tiago said the place was abandoned because of a police raid. Do you know anything about it?
Zh: “蒂亚戈说这个地方因为一次警察突袭而荒废了。你知道些什么吗？”

En: I do. There's a pause, then he continues: "I heard it was a particularly bad one. The place was shot to pieces."
Zh: “我知道。”他停顿了一下，然后继续说到：“我听说那次的情况特别糟糕。这个地方被打的粉碎。”

En: If this man was really here when it happened, then it's better for all of us that he doesn't remember what happened.
Zh: “如果事情发生的时候这个男人就在这里，那他不记得发生过什么其实对我们所有人来说都更好。”

En: In white, silver, and apricot faïence, the young mother of humanism stands above you. A crack runs across her body. She is impossibly tall, oval-faced and sad -- a dark and radiant majesty.
Zh: 白色、银色和杏色的彩陶器上，年轻的人道主义之母站在你上面。一道裂缝从她身上穿过。她高大的难以置信，椭圆形的脸庞略显悲伤——带着一种黑暗而又光芒四射的威严。

En: Reconstruct the cracked glass.
Zh: 重构碎裂的玻璃。

En: Saw missing shard with letters
Zh: 看见字母遗失的碎片。

En: Discovery of Insulinde.
Zh: 发现伊苏林迪。

En: Cursed Iilmaraan die.
Zh: 伊尔玛诅咒骰子。

En: A jigsaw of broken shards falls into place in front of you: a ghostly reconstruction of the stained glass window. Before it was shattered there was an older woman beneath the younger one -- and a text, a *leitmotiv* below them both.
Zh: 一堆碎裂的拼图碎片在你面前依序排列：彩色玻璃窗宛如幽灵一般在你面前还原。在它碎裂之前，有一个老妇人站在另一个年轻女性的下面——两人下面还有一排文字，一条*格言*。

En: The shards glimmer in the dark -- you see little pearls of light on the edges of the crack that splits the female figure. Something was written there... remains of broken letters line the emulsion. What it said, you do not know.
Zh: 碎片在黑暗中闪烁——你在撕裂女性雕像的裂缝边缘看到如小珍珠一般的光点。那里写着什么东西……残存的破碎字母排列在乳剂上。它说了什么，你不知道。

En: Turn away. [Leave.]
Zh: 转过脸。[离开。]

En: How did I know this is the *mother of humanism*?
Zh: 我怎么知道这是*人道主义之母*？

En: Basic Dolorianism Unlocked.
Zh: 基础德洛莉丝主义解锁。

En: Read "The Greatest Innocence"
Zh: 阅读“最伟大的无罪者”

En: Despite the damage you've done to yourself, the title appears lodged in your hippocampus. This is Her Innocence Dolores Dei -- the innocence of humanism, internationalism, and the welfare state -- perhaps the most famous human being ever to have lived.
Zh: 尽管你对自己造成了很大的伤害，这个标题好像还是停留在你的海马体里。这是无罪女王德洛莉丝·黛——人道主义、国际主义和福利国家的无罪者——她也许是有史以来最著名的人类。

En: It's a mystery. A mystery sprinkled with self-pity and regret. For some reason, unknown to your mind, looking at her delicate eyes makes you feel like you're ready for drowning...
Zh: 这是个谜。一个充满自怜和悔恨的谜。不知出于什么原因，仅仅是看着她那双柔美的双眼，就让你感觉自己准备好为她沉溺……

En: No amount of *Commodore Red* can wipe her sad smile from your brain-thing. It has survived the deluge and haunts you still. And will haunt you forever, as it haunts all men.
Zh: 多少瓶*海军准将红朗姆*也不能从你的大脑里抹去她那张悲伤的笑脸。它在酒精的洪水中幸存下来，依然缠绕在你脑海。而且还将永远缠绕着你，就像它缠绕着所有男人一样。

En: All you know is, this is the young mother of humanity -- and that you should go. Do something else! Escape her sad, worryworn look.
Zh: 你唯一知道的只有她是人道主义之母——而且你应该走了。做点别的事！逃离她那副悲伤而心力交瘁的神情。

En: Kneel.
Zh: 下跪。

En: Your knees touch the floor. The floorboards are hard and cold -- there you kneel among the snowdrifts, diffuse light falling on your hands from beyond the glass.
Zh: 你的膝盖碰触到地面。地板坚硬而冰冷——你的膝盖淹没在雪堆中间，上方玻璃的漫射光落在你的手上。

En: Do the same.
Zh: 做同样的事。

En: Your fingertips touch your chest four times as you stand in the apricot-coloured light of the window. Above you, the woman still smiles her distant smile, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 你站在窗户散发出的杏色光芒中，用指尖碰触胸口四次。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: As that soft word passes through your mind, you rise -- into the apricot-coloured light of the window. The woman keeps smiling her distant smile, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 那句温柔的话语掠过你的脑海，你站起身——沐浴在窗户散发出的杏色光芒中。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: As that lonely word passes through your mind, you rise -- into the apricot-coloured light of the window. The woman keeps smiling her distant smile above you, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 那句孤寂的话语掠过你的脑海，你站起身——沐浴在窗户散发出的杏色光芒中。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: With that terrible word ringing through your mind, you rise -- into the apricot coloured-light of the window. The woman keeps smiling her distant smile above you, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 那句可怕的话语掠过你的脑海，你站起身——沐浴在窗户散发出的杏色光芒中。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: As that great and desperate thought passes through your mind you rise. From your knees and into the apricot-coloured light of the window. Above you, the woman keeps smiling her distant smile, sundered by the crack in the glass.
Zh: 那个伟大而又绝望的想法掠过你的脑海，你站起身，沐浴在窗户散发出的杏色光芒中。女人站在上方，脸上一直挂着那副冷淡疏远的笑容——被玻璃上的裂缝切割开来。

En: Nothing, just the edge of the crack sparkles in the dark. There may have been a writing here but you cannot make out what it said.
Zh: 什么也没有，只有裂缝的边缘在黑暗中闪闪发光。这里可能有些文字，但你分辨不出上面说了些什么。

En: Who is this older woman?
Zh: 这个老妇人是谁？

En: The motto? What does it say?
Zh: 格言？说了什么？

En: Lieutenant, this used to say: 'After life, death -- after'...
Zh: “警督，这里曾经写着：生后的世界——死亡；死后……”

En: Step back.
Zh: 后退一步。

En: The escutcheon on her throne says: Irene the Navigator. She is depicted as an older woman wearing thick rimmed eye-glasses, holding a golden *reichsapfel* in one hand and a scepter in the other. This is the queen Her Innocence Dei advised -- above, she herself is whole.
Zh: 王座上的名牌写着：领航者艾琳。她被描绘成一个戴着厚有框眼镜的老妇人，一只手中握着金色的*盾形纹章*，另一只手握着一支节杖。这就是无罪者黛作为顾问服务过的女皇——在那之上是黛本人的全身像。

En: Small figures of wise men, common men, worshippers walk up the stairs to stand at her feet. Secret servicemen -- Therriers -- stand in a row guarding her. It must have taken years to produce this work in all its dizzying detail.
Zh: 智者，普通人，还有崇拜者的小雕像走上台阶，站在她的脚旁。秘密军人——梗犬--站成一排守卫着她。这件作品的细节让人目不暇接，生产出来一定要花上好几年时间。

En: Below both women, in luminous blackletter: APRÈS LA VIE - MORT; APRÈS LA MORT - LA VIE DE NOUVEAU.
Zh: 两个女人下面用发光的黑体字写着：生后的世界——死亡；死后的世界——又是新生。

En: And then along the left side: APRÈS LE MONDE - LE GRIS; APRÈS LE GRIS - LE MONDE DE NOUVEAU.
Zh: 然后沿着左侧看：世界之后——灰域；灰域之后——又是新世界。

En: AFTER LIFE -- DEATH; AFTER DEATH -- LIFE AGAIN. AFTER THE WORLD -- THE PALE; AFTER THE PALE -- THE WORLD AGAIN. This is the great leitmotiv of humanism, a summary of the effect of the discovery of this isola -- the Insulindian -- on human thinking. A tremendous sea change akin to finding life after death...
Zh: 生后的世界——死亡；死后的世界——又是新生。世界之后——灰域；灰域之后——又是新世界。这是人道主义的伟大格言，发现这个洲——伊苏林迪——对人类思维方式产生的影响的总结。一个巨大的转变，类似于死后重生……

En: The mother of humanism towers above you -- a wax painting on a cracked pane of glass. Nothing has changed in her expression.
Zh: 人道主义之母矗立在你面前——一副碎玻璃上的蜡画。她的表情没有丝毫变化。

En: ...death, life again, he nods. "After the world, the pale; after the pale -- the world again."
Zh: “……死后的世界——又是新生命，”他点点头。“世界之后——灰域；灰域之后——又是新世界。”

En: Could these words be the password that unlocks the filament memory?
Zh: 这些话会不会是解锁纤维内存的密码？

En: This exaltation is common in Dolorian sacralism. In the early years it was even incorporated as the RCM's slogan. No more, however.
Zh: “这种提炼在德洛莉丝的神圣主义中很常见。早些年甚至还被编入RCM的口号了。不过现在已经没有了。”

En: Why?
Zh: “为什么？”

En: What is the RCM motto now?
Zh: “那RCM现在的口号是什么？”

En: 'Justice, Union, Prudence, and Force'.
Zh: “正义，联合，审慎和魄力。”

En: It was deemed *subservient* to use a strongly Moralintern-related motto. We're already suspected of bootlicking. The sentence was also seen as... too feminine. It was a macho thing.
Zh: “使用跟国际道德伦理委员会强相关的口号被认为是一种*奉承*。大家本来已经怀疑我们在拍马屁了。而且，这句话还显得有些……太过女性化。这是个很大男子主义的事情。”

En: Cool.
Zh: “挺酷的。”

En: Not very feminine.
Zh: “不是很女性化啊。”

En: I like the other one better.
Zh: “我比较喜欢另外那个。”

En: So do I.
Zh: “我也是。”

En: Not at all.
Zh: “一点也不。”

En: Ice cool, the lieutenant nods.
Zh: “酷的都能结冰了，”警督点点头。

En: I like this. Puts the fear of god back in the f****ts.
Zh: “我喜欢这个。把对神明的恐惧都放回尿水三千。”

En: You are brutal and *dumb*. He says, his voice ice cold.
Zh: “你很粗鲁，而且很*愚蠢*。”他的声音冷的跟寒冰一般。

En: There is silence in the church. He wants there to be.
Zh: 教堂里一片寂静。他希望如此。

En: Wait, what exactly is an innocence? I've read a book but it was fuzzy...
Zh: 等等，无罪到底是什么意思啊？我读过一本书，不过还是很模糊……

En: Wait, what exactly is an innocence? I've heard of the system...
Zh: 等等，无罪者到底是什么意思啊？我听说过那个系统……

En: Wait, what exactly is an *innocence*?
Zh: 等等，*无罪*到底是什么意思啊？

En: Okay. When did she rule?
Zh: 好吧。她是什么时候在位统治的？

En: The highest category of historic individual -- an embodiment of the World Spirit.
Zh: 历史个体的最高范畴——世界精神的化身。

En: A ruler?
Zh: 一个统治者？

En: More. An innocence is elected to office by the Founding Party, a precedent that has taken place a mere six times in the entirety of History. The legal system of the Reál Belt is built to accommodate innocentic rule, should it coincide with our time.
Zh: 不止。无罪者被立国党推选为公职，这个先例在整个人类历史上仅仅发生过六次。雷亚尔货币法律体系的建立就是为了容纳无罪者的统治，来适应我们的时代。

En: A tyrant?
Zh: 一个暴君？

En: In a way. An innocence is elected to office by the Founding Party, a precedent that has taken place a mere six times in the entirety of History. The legal system of the Reál Belt is built from the ground up to accommodate innocentic rule, should it coincide with our time.
Zh: 有点。无罪者被立国党推选为公职，这个先例在整个人类历史上仅仅发生过六次。雷亚尔货币法律体系的建立就是为了容纳无罪者的统治，来适应我们的时代。

En: An innocence is infallible. The decisions made by one are not decisions. They are inevitabilities -- what would have happened anyway, only accelerated, packed into decades instead of centuries. An Innocence is a continuous, compressed event, a sacred human being. It is an honour and a glory to live when one is in office.
Zh: 无罪者是绝对不会犯错的人。无罪者做出的决策不是决策。它们是必然——无论如何都会发生，只会加速促进，压缩成几十年的时间，而不是几个世纪。无罪者是一种连续而精简的结果，是一个神圣的人类。大家都说生活在无罪者执政的时代是一种荣誉和骄傲。

En: Is one in office now?
Zh: 现在还有无罪者在位吗？

En: No. We are alone.
Zh: 没了，我们只有自个儿了。

En: Three hundred years ago, in the wake of the discovery of this isola -- the Insulindian -- by explorers from the continent of Mundi. She is, among other things, the innocence of interisolary travel and the connected world.
Zh: 三百年之前，随之而来的就是来自蒙迪大陆的探险家发现这个洲——伊苏林迪。除此之外，她还是跨洲旅行和互联世界的无罪女王。

En: What else do I know about her?
Zh: 关于她的事，我还知道些什么？

En: Many things. You know she was a woman of the court, the wife of an influential *marchese*, and eventually the principal advisor to Irene La Navigateur, Queen of Suresne (modern day Sur-la-Clef). Also, that she was gorgeous beyond beauty.
Zh: 很多事。你知道她是个女法官，一位有权有势*侯爵*的妻子，最后还成为了苏雷恩女皇（现在的苏拉菲）——领航者艾琳的首席顾问。而且，她还是个美的不可方物的女人。

En: Terribly. Women of the court were expected to play both contract bridge and chess sufficiently well to prove an interesting challenge to a man -- a similar grasp in matters of philosophy, theology, and science was encouraged. She was, by all means, a kept woman...
Zh: 聪明地吓人。大家都期望女法官拥有很高的棋艺和桥牌水平，这样对男人来说才是一种有趣的挑战——同时还鼓励在哲学、神学和科学上多方位发展。她一定是个情妇……

En: She made the most of her position in the Antedolorian court -- a court visited by the most prominent thinkers and artists of the day. In secret, she was becoming the era's pre-eminent philosopher of the state. A scalpel, a piercing gaze...
Zh: 她充分利用了自己在前德洛莉丝时代法院的地位——当时最杰出的大思想家和艺术家都会拜访那里。私下里，她变成了那个时代里国家最杰出的哲学家。一把手术刀，一道犀利的目光。

En: Draped in ancient sadness. Are you sure you want to remember this bit of historic trivia? Standing under her long slender form like this, dwarfed...
Zh: 被古老的悲伤包围。你确定想要记起过去的那些琐事？站在她如此修长的身形之下，你就像一个侏儒……

En: Yes -- big bummer. Boring history. Gotta keep it light, man, keep it moving. Get fucked up instead.
Zh: 是的——非常不愉快的经历。无聊的历史。轻松点吧，伙计，不要停下来。还是把一切搞得乱七八糟吧。

En: Right. Better not to go poking any further.
Zh: 没错。最好不要再深入刺探了。

En: Okay, what else? Was she smart?
Zh: 好吧，还有什么？她很聪明吗？

En: Where is this coming from?
Zh: 这是哪里来的？

En: The past. It's a *silo* of sadness. Fermenting. You should keep away.
Zh: 过去。它是一个悲伤堆积成山的*筒仓*。正在发酵。你应该离远一点。

En: Fuck this pain, bratan. It's unhealthy.
Zh: 让这种痛苦玩蛋去吧，兄弟。这样不健康。

En: Exactly. Back out of this corner of your mind by choosing the bottom-most option: "To hell with this."
Zh: 没错。跳出自己思维的角落，选择最下面的选项：“下地狱去吧。”

En: To hell with this.
Zh: 下地狱去吧。

En: But... she was not hell. She is the opposite of that, the wind of paradise, stripping away the covers from the hearts of men...
Zh: 但是……她不是地狱。她正好相反，她是天堂的风，掀开了男人心房的盖子……

En: There you stand, below her once more, with your hands on your side. Momentarily at a loss as to what to do with yourself.
Zh: 你又一次站到了她脚下，双手垂放在两侧，一时竟不知道如何是好。

En: It was on her advice that Irene La Navigateur sponsored a number of voyages into the pale. A costly, often tragic endeavour, ultimately vindicated by the discovery of the *New New World*, the piece of reality you're standing on...
Zh: 正是在她的建议下，领航者艾琳资助了好几次深入灰域的航行。这些代价高昂、经常会成为悲剧的努力，最终因为*新新世界*的发现而被正名，那就是此刻我们脚下所处的现实世界。

En: I want more.
Zh: 我想多了解一些。

En: As did we all. The lands of Mesque and the Occident and even far-away Supramundi -- altogether twenty one of the forty Mondial nations of the time -- immediately accepted innocentic rule. Even before her crowning.
Zh: 我们都想。梅斯克和奥西登，甚至还有远方的苏帕穆迪——那个时候，全世界40个国家中的21个——立即接受了无罪的统治。那时的她甚至还没有被加冕。

En: In a city called Advesperascit, in Vesper-Messina, her homeland. The name of the city means 'Evening comes,' but it happened on a winter's morning with the canals frozen and slush falling out of the sky.
Zh: 她的家乡在威斯珀·墨西拿一个名叫艾弗斯派瑞特的城市。那个城市名字还有“夜幕降临”的意思，但是它发生在一个冬日清晨，运河结冰了，泥浆从天而降。

En: Wow indeed. When her innocence was declared -- and the queen she had advised for years fell on her knees before her -- she was so overcome with emotion that her *lungs* started *glowing* in her chest...
Zh: 确实很惊人。她当选为无罪女王的时候——她作为顾问服务了好几年的女皇跪倒在她面前——她的情绪是如此的激动，连她的*肺*都开始在胸中*发光*了。

En: How come?
Zh: 怎么会？

En: That is why the lungs are the symbol of love for the cultures of the Reál Belt.
Zh: 所以肺才会成为雷亚尔体系文化里爱的象征。

En: Oh yes... She looked like humanity's young mother, a perfect mother. Insultingly beautiful. It was as if her face and shoulders and hands were covered in a soft down of under-feathers. You know this well -- very well.
Zh: 没错……她看起来很像年轻的人道主义之母，一个完美的母亲。无比的美丽。就好像她的脸庞，双肩还有双手都覆盖在一层柔软的羽绒之下。你知道的很清楚——非常清楚。

En: One of the men in this secret service killed her -- 22 years later. A young man who had come to suspect that Dolores Dei was not entirely human, but something else.
Zh: 特务机关里的一个男人杀了她——在22年后。一个年轻人，他怀疑德洛莉丝·黛并不完全是一个人类，而是别的存在。

En: Wow.
Zh: 哇哦。

En: I don't care.
Zh: 我不在乎。

En: Okay. When her innocence was declared -- and the queen she had advised for years fell on her knees before her -- she was so overcome with emotion that her *lungs* started *glowing* in her chest...
Zh: 好吧。她当选为无罪女王的时候——她作为顾问服务了好几年的女皇跪倒在她面前——她的情绪是如此的激动，连她的*肺*都开始在胸中*发光*了。

En: Her crowning?
Zh: 加冕？

En: She was crowned? Like Sola?
Zh: 她被加冕了？就像索拉一样吗？

En: She must have been beautiful...
Zh: 她一定很美……

En: I don't care how she looked. I don't care. It doesn't hurt me.
Zh: 我不知道她长什么样。我不在乎。这伤不到我。

En: Oh, she was insultingly beautiful. She was humanity's young mother, a perfect mother. It was as if her face and shoulders and hands were covered in a soft down of under-feathers. You know this well -- very well.
Zh: 哦，她美的不可方物。她是人类年轻的母亲，一个完美的母亲。就好像她的脸庞、双肩还有双手都覆盖在一层柔软的羽绒之下。你知道的很清楚——非常清楚。

En: Yes. In a city called Advesperascit, in Vesper-Messina, her homeland. The name of the city means "Evening comes," but it happened on a winter's morning with the canals frozen and slush falling out of the sky.
Zh: 是的。她的家乡在威斯珀·墨西拿一个名叫艾弗斯派瑞特的城市。那个城市名字还有“夜幕降临”的意思，但是它发生在一个冬日去清晨，运河结冰了，泥浆从天而降。

En: She was dressed in a white and pearl dress on an emptied-out plaza, with the crowd far away. Already her *Therriers* -- the secret servicemen of the innocence -- were worried about an assassination attempt.
Zh: 她穿着一条缀以珍珠的白裙，站在一个被清空的广场，人群在遥远的地方张望。她的*梗犬*——无罪的秘密军人——担心有人会企图行刺。

En: Midwinter snow was beating the cobblestones around her. A small attaché of officials stood by as her Therriers placed a white gold wreath on her head. The crowning was mostly witnessed by secret servicemen.
Zh: 隆冬的雪花飘落在她身边的鹅卵石上。一小群官员站在旁边，她的梗犬把一个白金色的花冠戴在她的头上。观赏加冕仪式的大多都是秘密军人。

En: Then what?
Zh: 然后呢？

En: What?
Zh: 什么？

En: Something that had walked in our midst, watching us stumble for hundreds, if not thousands of years, until it decided to interfere -- interfere in the course of our history. 'We were supposed to come up with this ourselves!' the man was reported to have screamed at the innocence...
Zh: 有什么东西走进我们当中，看着我们不断跌倒，不说千年，至少也有几百年的时间，直到它决定干预其中——干预我们的历史进程。“我们本该自己想出来的！”根据报道，男人这样朝着无罪女王大喊道……

En: This *inhuman* quality was witnessed by many others as well -- glowing lungs and all. It is commonly attributed to mass hysteria and religious psychology.
Zh: 这种*非人类*的特质同样被其他很多人看到了——发光的肺部，还有那一切。这通常被归因于集体歇斯底里和宗教心理学。

En: Dolores Dei was shot in the chest with a fowling piece, eight times. The man, thought to be insane, said he once touched her and her body had been unnaturally warm, like a furnace -- and that sometimes while on duty he observed her forgetting to breathe for over ten minutes...
Zh: 德洛莉丝·黛被一支猎枪击中胸口，整整八次。那个男人，大家口中的疯子，说他曾经碰过她，说她的身体温暖的有些不自然，就像火炉——而且他有时值班的时候，会看见她有10多分钟都没有呼吸……

En: Was there something... *terrifying* about her?
Zh: 那她有什么……*吓人*的地方吗？

En: *Terrifying* is a term too emotionally charged for your semantic memory. Or what remains of it, but...
Zh: 对于你的语义记忆来说，*吓人*这个词有些太过情绪化。或者是你仅剩的语义记忆，不过……

En: Suggesting those who fight against it are not part of humanity.
Zh: 暗示那些反对计划的人并不是人类的一份子。

En: Terrifying. It's a simple word. She was *bad* for humanity and you shouldn't have started thinking about her!
Zh: 吓人。多么简单的一个词。她对人类*有害*，所以你不应该再想她了！

En: Although she is often considered to be the greatest human being to ever live, there *was* something ominous about Dolores Dei -- constantly surrounded by her Therriers...
Zh: 虽然她经常被认为是人类有史以来最伟大的人物，德洛莉丝·黛身上*确实*有些不祥的东西——总是被她的卫兵包围……

En: She adored chess, yes -- but also military war games. Dolores Dei often holds a tiny tin soldier between her index finger and thumb -- in icons such as this. She was also blonde, the blondest woman you have ever seen, with green eyes the colour of the Pisantic *mare interregnum*...
Zh: 她喜爱下棋，没错——还喜欢军事战争游戏。德洛莉丝·黛经常把一个小锡兵夹在自己的食指和拇指之间——就是类似这样的雕像。她还是个金发女郎，那种金色是你见过的最极致的存在，而且还有一双绿色的眼睛，就像*阴暗过渡区*皮桑缇克海的颜色……

En: She was the most socially secluded and least self-aware of all the innocences. Some modern thinkers would consider her a war criminal for the campaigns she waged against the Mesque state. And then there were the resettlement programs...
Zh: 在所有无罪统治者中，她是社会交际最孤僻、最缺乏自我意识的一个。有些现代思想家会把她当成是一个战犯，因为她发起了对抗梅斯克国家的活动。而且还有之后的重新安置计划……

En: The Mesque state tried to detach itself from innocentic rule. Parts of the world were experiencing whiplash from accelerating into secularism. Her mandatory education programs and mass resettlement of upstream Magritte were problematic as well. Dissenters were suppressed by a military force she called “The Army of Humanity"...
Zh: 梅斯克国家试图脱离无罪的统治。世界上某些部分由于加速走向世俗主义而经历着鞭挞。她的强制教育计划以及大规模的马格里特上游重新安置计划也引起不少疑问。反对者被她称为“人道主义大军”的一支军事力量镇压了。

En: Little is known of her marchese husband. It's as if he vanished from history after completing his role -- which was to introduce Dolores Dei to court. In conclusion, yes, there *is* something lonely, paranoid, and even terrifying that people seldom mention, but *feel* when they think of her...
Zh: 很少有人知道她的那位侯爵丈夫。他好像在完成自己的角色任务之后就消失了——这就把德洛莉丝·黛推向了法庭。总之，人们鲜少会提到她，但是想到她的时候，会*感觉*到一丝孤独，偏执，甚至是恐怖……

En: This subtle terror is part of her iconography.
Zh: 这种微妙的恐怖是她造像的一部分。

En: You're right. Time to heed the warning.
Zh: 你说得对。是时候注意警告了。

En: No -- was there something *bad* about her, I want to know...
Zh: 不——我想知道，她有什么*不好*的地方吗……

En: You already do. Although she is often considered to be the greatest human being to ever live, there *was* something ominous about Dolores Dei -- constantly surrounded by her Therriers...
Zh: 你已经想了。虽然她经常被认为是人类有史以来最伟大的人物，德洛莉丝·黛身上*确实*有些不祥的东西——总是被她的卫兵包围……

En: Good. Now back out of this corner of your mind by choosing the bottom option: "To hell with this." You've been standing here long enough.
Zh: 很好。现在跳出自己思维的角落，选择最下面的选项：“下地狱去吧。”你已经在这里站的够久了。

En: What happened?
Zh: 发生什么事了？

En: Lieutenant-yefreitor, you've stood there for over five minutes... The lieutenant's calm voice echoes in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “荣誉警督，你站在那里已经超过5分钟了……”警督平静的声音在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: Officer, you've stood there for over five minutes... The lieutenant's calm voice echoes in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “警官，你站在那里已经超过5分钟了……”警督平静的声音在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: What are you thinking of, if I may ask?
Zh: “如果可以的话，我能问问你在想什么吗？”

En: What are you thinking -- you okay? He seems a tad worried.
Zh: “你在想什么——你还好吗？”他好像有点担心。

En: Hey man, you've stood there for like... half an hour! The hedgehog-haired man yells from across the coldness of the church.
Zh: “喂，老兄，你已经站了差不多……半小时了！”一个刺猬头男子的穿过教堂寒冷的空气传了过来。

En: She's somehow connected to the case.
Zh: “她和这个案子有点关系。”

En: Why did we tolerate this bourgeois woman that long before we shot her?
Zh: “在枪毙这个资产阶级女人之前，我们为什么要折磨她那么久？”

En: Glowing lungs... That's fucked up...
Zh: “发光的肺……都是哪来的鬼话……”

En: Nothing. Just looking around.
Zh: “没什么。只是随便看看。”

En: She's not human.
Zh: “她不是人类。”

En: She's been dead for three hundred years. I am almost a thousand percent certain she isn't connected to the case... He takes his glasses off to clean them.
Zh: “她已经死了300年。我几乎可以100%肯定她跟这个案子没有关系……”他取下眼镜擦拭着。

En: Yell: "War criminal!"
Zh: 大喊：“战犯！”

En: She's beautiful...
Zh: “她很美……”

En: None of this is, in fact. This church. The coast... This isn't a good place to get lost in.
Zh: “其实全都没有关系。这座教堂。这个海岸……这可不是个迷路的好地方。”

En: An imperceptible tinge of fear. The lieutenant is afraid of this place.
Zh: 一股难以觉察的恐惧气味。警督害怕这个地方。

En: That's mega cool, man, you coming up with theories in here like that. Right on.
Zh: “真是太酷了，老兄，你居然能在这里提出一个理论。太给力了！”

En: That's mega cool, man, you coming in here and questioning religious symbolism like that. Right on.
Zh: “真是太酷了，老兄，你就这样走进来，然后像那样质疑宗教象征主义。实在是太给力了！”

En: Yes. Glowing lungs are quite unusual. After that one time, they have not been reported to glow. He takes his glasses off to clean them.
Zh: “是的。发光的肺是很不寻常。那次之后，就再也没有类似的报道了。”他取下眼镜擦拭着。

En: You know... this church. The coast... we shouldn't linger. Finish what you came here to do and let's move on. This isn't a good place to get lost in.
Zh: “你知道的……这座教堂。这片海岸……我们不应该到处闲晃。把你要做的事做完，然后我们继续前进。这可不是个迷路的好地方。”

En: An imperceptible tinge of fear. The lieutenant is afraid of the church. He does not know why, but he is.
Zh: 一股难以觉察的恐惧气味。警督害怕这个地方。他不知道为什么，但他就是害怕。

En: That's mega cool, man, you coming up with wild left-wing stuff in here like that. Right on!
Zh: “真是太酷了，老兄，你能在这里想出这些疯狂的左翼言论。实在是太给力了！”

En: She introduced us to the idea of public education, retirement, and modern diplomacy. He takes his glasses off to clean them.
Zh: “她向我们引入了公共教育，退休和现代外交的理念。”他取下眼镜擦拭着。

En: Either way... this church, we shouldn't linger here. This isn't a good place to get lost in.
Zh: “不管怎么说……这座教堂，我们不应该到处闲晃。这可不是个迷路的好地方。”

En: That's not left wing, Andre. I don't think we should have that spooker adorning the club... Another voice from the back of the church...
Zh: “那不是左翼，安德烈。我觉得我们不应该用那种吓人的东西装饰俱乐部……”教堂后面又传来一个声音……

En: This church. The coast in general... we shouldn't linger here. This isn't a good place to get lost in. We should conclude our business and move on.
Zh: “这座教堂。这整片海岸……我们不应该到处闲晃。这可不是迷路的好地方。我们最好赶紧办完事，然后继续前进。”

En: Right on. A voice from the back of the church. "She was inhuman. You've got a critical mind, cop-man. Yo, Andre, we should board that window up. No one wants that spooky shit when they're dancing."
Zh: “你说得对，”教堂后面响起一个声音。“她不是人类。你的想法很有批判性，警察。喂，安德烈，我们应该把窗户封起来。他们跳舞的时候可不想看到那些吓人的鬼玩意。”

En: Okay... He takes his glasses off to clean them. Then, after a while, he says:
Zh: “好吧……”他取下眼镜擦拭着。然后，过了一会儿，他说到：

En: That's mega cool, man, you coming in and accusing her of not being human like that. Right on, man!
Zh: “真是太酷了，老兄，你就这样走进来，然后指控她不像人类什么的。实在是太给力了，老兄！”

En: We should board that window up, Andre. A voice from the back of the church. "People don't want to see that woman when they're dancing. It's unhealthy."
Zh: “我们应该把窗户封起来，安德烈，”教堂后面传来一个声音。“大家跳舞的时候可不想看到那个女人。不利于身心健康。”

En: This church. The coast in general... we shouldn't linger here. This isn't a good place to get lost in.
Zh: “这座教堂。这整片海岸……我们不应该到处闲晃。这可不是迷路的好地方。”

En: Cool, man! Yeah! Look around as much as you wanna, this is *your* place too!
Zh: “没问题，老兄！你想怎么看就怎么看吧，这也是*你的*地方！”

En: Okay. It's just that you've been looking at that window for quite long now. And it's cold in here... He takes off his glasses to clean them.
Zh: “好吧。只是你已经盯着那面窗户看了好久。而且这里很冷……”他取下眼镜擦拭着。

En: I told you, Andre. A voice from the back of the church. "People don't want to see that woman when they're dancing. It's unhealthy. And we're *not* putting disco lights on it. That would only make it worse."
Zh: “我告诉过你，安德烈，”教堂后面传来一个声音。“大家跳舞的时候可不想看到那个女人。不利于身心健康。而且我们*不会*把迪斯科灯光打到那上面去的。那样只会变得更糟糕。”

En: Cool, man! Yeah, we should light her up with disco lights. People are gonna lose their shit when they see a club for anodic music with this kind of... sacred shit.
Zh: “没问题，老兄！我们应该用迪斯科灯光把她点亮。他们在阳极舞曲夜店看到这种……什么鬼圣人的时候，一定会连屎都吓出来的。”

En: That she is. A great sacral piece. I wonder what we're doing here, however... He takes his glasses off to clean them.
Zh: “的确如此。这是一件伟大的圣礼作品。不过，我在想我们来这里做什么……”他取下眼镜擦拭着。

En: In this church I mean? The coast in general... we shouldn't linger. This isn't a good place to get lost in.
Zh: “我是说，在这座教堂里？还有这片海岸……我们不应该到处闲晃。这可不是迷路的好地方。”

En: Yeah, glowing lungs *are* fucked up. A voice from the back of the church. "We should board that window up. Bad sines, you know?"
Zh: “是啊，发光的肺*是*他妈在鬼扯。”教堂后面传来一个声音。“我们应该把窗户封起来。你知道吗，这是严重的罪行？”

En: Right on. A voice from the back of the church. "She was inhuman. You've got a critical mind cop-man -- furthermore, Andre, we should board that window up. No one wants that spooky shit when they're dancing."
Zh: “你说得对，”教堂后面响起一个声音。“她不是人类。你的想法很有批判性，警察。喂，安德烈，我们应该把窗户封起来。没人想在跳舞的时候看到那些吓人的鬼玩意。”

En: Either way -- this church, the coast in general... we shouldn't linger here. This isn't a good place to get lost in. We should conclude our business and move on.
Zh: “不管怎么说……这座教堂，这片海岸……我们不应该到处闲晃。这可不是迷路的好地方。我们最好赶紧办完事，然后继续前进。”

En: Uhm... Andre doesn't know what to say. "It's cold man, you've been looking at that window for like forever now, man. You should dance, get some warmth!"
Zh: “嗯……”安德烈不知道该说什么。“老兄，这里挺冷的，你盯着那个窗户看得不知道有多久了。你应该跳跳舞，让自己暖和起来！”

En: I don't know about that... He takes his glasses off to clean them. "It was a different time. A different war."
Zh: “那我就不知道了……”他取下眼镜擦拭着。“那是个不同的时代。一场不同的战争。”

En: We should board that old spooker up, Andre. A voice from the back of the church. "It'll only disturb the people while they're trying to dance, unshackled by the failure of humanism."
Zh: “我们应该把那个老幽灵封起来，安德烈。”教堂后面传来一个声音。“这种东西只会打扰大家跳舞，他们只想摆脱人道主义失败的束缚。”

En: How about we *don't* do that, Noid? Another voice. "She looks cool."
Zh: 又一个声音响起：“那我们*不要*这么做怎么样，诺伊德。她看起来挺酷的。”

En: Okey-dokey.
Zh: “好咯。”

En: Okey-doe-doe, he whispers in the silence of the great building.
Zh: “好咯，”他在这栋沉默的巨大建筑里轻声低语。

En: A touch of absurdity there, nice!
Zh: 有一点荒谬的意味，真棒！

En: A very, very skilled thespian would detect a touch of drama when he says he doesn't know why it was conducted...
Zh: 当他说出不知道是谁指挥的时候，只有非常、非常成熟的悲剧演员才能觉察到里面那一点点戏剧性的成分……

En: You're leaving something out.
Zh: “你在掩饰什么。”

En: I assure you, I'm not. He looks up at the stained glass window.
Zh: “我向你保证，并没有。”他抬起头看着彩色玻璃窗。

En: Stonewalling. Good technique.
Zh: 防守挡击。好手法。

En: What shattered this mosaic?
Zh: 是什么打碎了这个地砖？

En: Unknown.
Zh: 未知。

En: Something during the raid the lieutenant mentioned? Or just hooligans looking for something to break.
Zh: 大概是警督提到的突袭时候的事？或者只是些小流氓想随意砸破些什么。

En: Your heart knows. But it does not want to say, not yet. Let these things be unknown for now.
Zh: 你心里知道。但是它不想说，暂时还不行。暂时还是让这些事保持未知状态吧。

En: Looks like I can't give this figurine to her.
Zh: 看来我不能把这个小雕像给她。

En: Why?
Zh: 为什么？

En: Because she's a stained glass window.
Zh: 因为她是一面彩色玻璃。

En: That does seem to be a problem... maybe you meant something else?
Zh: 确实好像是个问题……也许你是说别的东西？

En: Like what? Is the task still on?
Zh: 比如什么？任务还在吗？

En: I don't know. What are we thinking of? Part of your mind has gone on to other things already...
Zh: 我不知道。我们在想什么？你的一部分思绪已经转到其他事情上了……

En: Only a strange little sadness remains.
Zh: 只剩下一点奇怪的悲伤。

En: Okay.
Zh: 好吧。

En: We should be respectful here, although the building appears to be deserted. I do not believe we'll find anything connected to the lynching here. Something else, perhaps... He looks at the machinery lying around.
Zh: “虽然建筑好像已经废弃了，我们在这里还是应该恭敬一些。我不认为我们会在这里找到和私刑相关的东西。也许是别的什么……”他看着地上的那些机器。

En: She was crowned two years after the first expedition returned, setting in motion what is widely considered the greatest era in history -- the Dolorian Era.
Zh: 第一支远征队返回两年后，她被加冕成王，从那时便开启了被普遍认为是历史上最伟大的时代——德洛莉丝时代。

En: Bystanders reported golden filaments lighting the already sunlit chamber around her, clearly visible beneath her dress.
Zh: 旁观者报告说她那被日光照亮的胸膛上出现了金丝样的光芒，在她的裙子下面清晰可见。

En: Bystanders reported golden filaments lighting the already sunlit chamber around her, clearly visible beneath her dress. I repeat: *Her lungs started glowing*. Do you care now?
Zh: 旁观者报告说她那已经被日光照亮的胸膛上出现了金丝样的光芒，在她的裙子下面清晰可见。我重复一遍：*她的肺部开始发光*。现在你在乎了吗？

En: Uhm.... oops?
Zh: 呃……哎呀？

En: That accent is Villalobos -- a peninsula in Mesque *and* a district in Jamrock. There's a sizable contingent of Villalobos-speaking mesques in Revachol.
Zh: 那个口音来自维拉洛博斯——那是梅斯克的一个半岛，*而且*还是加姆洛克的一个行政区。瑞瓦肖有相当数量说维拉洛博斯语的梅斯克人。

En: Fuck you, crab-man, you don't tell us what to do. Tell him.
Zh: 去他妈的，蟹男，我们做什么不用你来教。告诉他。

En: This Mother of Silence sounds like a serious player. You might want to be careful, until you find out what you're dealing with.
Zh: 这个沉默之母听起来像是个很有分量的角色。你也许应该小心些，直到你发现自己面对的到底是什么。

En: All right. Let's consider the context and meaning here.
Zh: 好吧。我们考虑一下这里的语境和含义。

En: His adoration is beyond sexual. This cavity is something that no human form has.
Zh: 他的崇拜超越了性。这个空洞是任何人类形体所没有的东西。

En: That sounds a *bit* like substitution behaviour, no? You know a thing or two about that...
Zh: 听起来有*一点点*像替代行为，不是吗？对这个你算是了解一点点……

En: There are drugs darker than alcohol circling your system.
Zh: 有比酒精还要黑暗的毒品在你体内循环。

En: It looks like he truly doesn't remember. There's a vagueness to his sense of time.
Zh: 看起来他是真心不记得了。他的时间观念很模糊。

En: All right. Let's consider the context and meaning here.
Zh: 好吧。我们考虑一下这里的语境和含义。

En: Though he used to. A long time ago.
Zh: 不过他曾经有过。很久以前的事。

En: Viejita is... grandma?
Zh: 老婆子，是说……老奶奶吗？

En: No need to overdo it. He won't mind sharing it with you.
Zh: 没必要这么夸张。他不介意跟你一起分享。

En: So you've got nothing else to tell me? How she looks, what she does, who *is* she?
Zh: “所以你没什么要告诉我的吗？她什么样，她是做什么的，她*是*谁？”

En: I'm afraid not, ese. You just have to wait until she comes back or... He shrugs.
Zh: “恐怕不能。你只能等她回来，或者……”他耸耸肩。

En: Or search through her radiocomputer.
Zh: 或者翻查她的无线电脑。

En: Something like that, he responds, his voice suddenly flat.
Zh: “差不多吧，”他回应着，声音突然变平了。

En: Not really, or at least I don't remember much of it anymore. The Mother's Love has done its job... That's what's so great about the Mother -- it lets you forget about everything.
Zh: “不算吧，或者至少我不怎么记得了。母亲的爱发挥了作用……这就是母亲的伟大之处——能让你忘记一切。”

En: Hmh... does it mean he witnessed the police raid?
Zh: 嗯……那是不是说他目击警方突袭了？

En: Honestly, I don't get it. All these figures look half-finished.
Zh: “老实说，我不明白。这些东西看起来都只完成了一半。”

En: I see what you've done there. It's as if these figures are trying to become one with the church -- or perhaps with the Mother herself.
Zh: “我看见你在那里做了什么。就好像这些东西想要跟教堂融为一体——或者也许，是母亲本身。”

En: Honestly, I don't get it. All these figures look half-finished.
Zh: “老实说，我不明白。这些东西看起来都只完成了一半。”

En: I see what you've done there. It's as if these figures are trying to become one with the church -- or perhaps with the Mother herself.
Zh: “我看见你在那里做了什么。就好像这些东西想要跟教堂融为一体——或者也许，是母亲本身。”

En: (Rub your chin.) "You've got some nice... curves going there."
Zh: （抚摸自己的下巴。）“你有些……很漂亮的曲线。”

En: (Squint your eyes.) "These seem a tad... derivative."
Zh: （斜着眼睛。）“这些看起来有一点像是……衍生物。”

En: You're promoting the objectification of women with your reactionary depiction of female bodies.
Zh: “你这是在推动用女性身体的保守描述物化女性。”

En: *Sssurveys* are a good way to fish for personal information, especially in the name of public sssafety...
Zh: *调查*是一种搜寻个人信息的好方法，特别是以公共安全为名义的时候……

En: Just tell me what's the password. (Step closer.)
Zh: “赶紧把密码告诉我吧。”（靠近一些。）

En: Yes, it's for a first-degree murder investigation in Martinaise.
Zh: “是的，是为了调查马丁内斯发生的一起一级凶杀案。”

En: Honestly I just want to break into a radiocomputer, see what's on it.
Zh: “说真的，我只想入侵这台无线电脑，看看里面有什么。”

En: The lieutenant raises his eyebrow, but doesn't say anything.
Zh: 警督扬起眉头，但是什么也没说。

En: Hey, it's your neighbours who came up with this name, not me.
Zh: “喂，想出这个名字的是你的邻居，不是我。”

En: Never known myself to be a *crab*. But if that's the name you've got for me, I won't stop you from using it.
Zh: “从来不知道我自己是只*螃蟹*。不过如果这是你帮我取的名字，我不会阻止你这么叫的。”

En: I don't do names either. Names are *out*. I don't care what mine is.
Zh: “我也没有名字。名字太*过时*了。我不在乎自己的名字是什么。”

En: My name's Harry. (Extend your hand for a greeting.)
Zh: “我叫哈里。”（伸出手向他问候。）

En: That's just the thing, homes -- none of that *matters*... He ignores your hand, his limbs a mere shadow below the ceiling.
Zh: “那不过是个东西，兄弟——这些都不*重要*……”他忽略了你的手，天花板下他的身影只不过是一团阴影。

En: My name's Harrier Du Bois, and my place in the world is lieutenant double-yefreitor.
Zh: “我的名字叫哈里尔·杜博阿，我在这个世界的位置是一名双重荣誉警督。”

En: That's just the thing, homes -- none of that *matters*... He sounds melancholy saying this, his limbs a mere shadow below the ceiling.
Zh: “那不过是个东西，兄弟——这些都不*重要*……”他说这句话的时候，声音有些悲伤，天花板下他的身影只不过是一团阴影。

En: Wait, so there *is* another person living in the church -- and it's a *viejita*?
Zh: “等等，所以*还有*其他人住在这座教堂——而且还是个*老婆子*？”

En: No, I just call her 'viejita' because of her clothes, she's actually quite young... He scratches his head. "Or maybe not *that* young... Age is just one of the many masks we wear."
Zh: “不是，我叫她‘老婆子’是因为她的衣服，本人其实挺年轻的……”他挠了挠头。“或者也许没*那么*年轻……我们戴着很多面具，年龄不过是其中之一。”

En: Wait, what if it's Ruby?
Zh: 等等，如果是露比呢？

En: Did it ever seem to you like she was hiding here from something? The lieutenant seems to be thinking the same. He takes out his little notebook.
Zh: 警督好像跟你想的一样。他掏出自己的小笔记本：“在你看来，她是不是因为什么事藏在这里的？”

En: You mean like a fugitive? He glances at the abandoned radiocomputer on the other side of the nave, pulsing with light. Then he shakes his head.
Zh: “你是说一个逃犯吗？”他瞥了一眼中殿另一侧那台废弃的无线电脑，它正闪着光脉动着。然后他摇摇头。

En: I see. The lieutenant seems contented with that answer. "And where is she now?"
Zh: “我明白了。”警督好像对答案很满意。“那她现在在哪里？”

En: I told you, homes -- I don't know.
Zh: “我告诉过你了，兄弟——我不知道。”

En: Right, thanks. I'll see if I can find her some other way...
Zh: “好吧，谢谢。我看看能不能用别的方法找到她……”

En: You just have to wait until she comes back or... He shrugs.
Zh: “你只能等她回来，或者……”他耸耸肩。

En: No, man, quite the opposite -- I don't think she cares much about authority or, anything else for that matter. Maybe only about her machines.
Zh: “不，兄弟，正好相反——我不觉得她很在乎当局，或者其他任何相关的事情。也许她只在乎她的机器。”

En: Did it seem like she was hiding in here? Or running from something?
Zh: “她看起来像是藏在这里吗？或者想逃离什么的东西？”

En: You mean like a fugitive? He glances at the abandoned radiocomputer on the other side of the nave, pulsing with light. Then he shakes his head.
Zh: “你是说一个逃犯吗？”他瞥了一眼中殿另一侧那台废弃的无线电脑，它正闪着光脉动着。然后他摇摇头。

En: No, man, quite the opposite -- I don't think she cares much about authority or, anything else for that matter. Maybe only about her machines.
Zh: “不，兄弟，正好相反——我不觉得她很在乎当局，或者其他任何相关的事情。也许她只在乎她的机器。”

En: And you don't know where she is?
Zh: “你不知道她在哪里？”

En: That's what I said, homes.
Zh: “我已经这么说过了，兄弟。”

En: Hey, not judging -- I've got drug problems myself.
Zh: “呃，不是评判什么——我自己也有成瘾的问题。”

En: I can see, and it saddens me. But devotion can save you -- it saved me.
Zh: “我能看出来，这让我很难过。不过奉献能拯救你——它拯救了我。”

En: (Let's keep moving on.) "What were you before you became a crab-man?"
Zh: （我们继续吧。）“你成为蟹男之前是做什么的？”

En: (Nod.) "Singing is good. I'm a bit of a singer myself."
Zh: （点头。）“唱歌很好啊。我自己就可以算是个歌手吧。”

En: (Nod.) "Singing is good. We should all sing a bit more."
Zh: （点头。）“唱歌很好啊。我们都应该多唱点歌。”

En: That was... an interesting conversation.
Zh: “这还真是一次……有趣的对话。”

En: However, I'm still not sure how it's relevant to our investigation.
Zh: “不过，我还是不确定这跟我们的调查有什么关系。”

En: However, I'm still not sure whether we'll find our suspect here.
Zh: “不过，我还是不确定能不能在这里找到嫌犯。”

En: Are these yours? (Show him the scarf and shoes you found lying around.)
Zh: “这些是你的吗？”（把你在附近找到的围巾和鞋子拿给他看。）

En: I think they were, a long time ago... He looks at the red clothing items you're wearing. "I had to *shed* them like skins, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have them. I don't need them anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着你身上的红色衣物。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: I think they were, a long time ago... He looks at the red clothing items. "I had to *shed* them like skins, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have them. I don't need them anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着红色的衣物。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: I think they were, a long time ago... He looks at the red clothing items. "I had to *shed* them like skins, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have them. I don't need them anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着红色的衣物。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: I think they were, a long time ago... He looks at the red clothing items in your hand. "I had to *shed* them like skins, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have them. I don't need them anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着你手中的红色衣物。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: They'd only stop him from climbing.
Zh: 它们只会阻拦他攀爬。

En: They look pretty dapper actually.
Zh: 其实吧，它们看起来挺整洁的。

En: Are these your shoes lying around here? (Show him the red brogues you found.)
Zh: “这是你的鞋子吗？”（把你找到的红色布洛克鞋拿给他看。）

En: Is this your scarf lying around here? (Show him the red scarf you found.)
Zh: “这是你的围巾吗？”（把你找到的红围巾拿给他看。）

En: These shoes look pretty dapper actually.
Zh: 其实吧，这双鞋看起来挺整洁的。

En: They'd only stop him from climbing.
Zh: 它们只会阻拦他攀爬。

En: I think they were, a long time ago... He looks at the red brogues you're wearing. "I had to *shed* them like skins, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have them. I don't need them anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着你脚上的红布洛克鞋。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: I think they were, a long time ago... He looks at the red brogues in your hand. "I had to *shed* them like skins, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have them. I don't need them anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着你手中的红布洛克鞋。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: The scarf looks pretty dapper actually.
Zh: 其实吧，这条围巾看起来挺整洁的。

En: It'd only stop him from climbing.
Zh: 它只会阻拦他攀爬。

En: I think it was, a long time ago... He looks at the red winter scarf around your neck. "I had to *shed* it like a skin, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have it. I don't need it anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着你脖子上的红色冬日围巾。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: I think it was, a long time ago... He looks at the red winter scarf in your hand. "I had to *shed* it like a skin, to get closer to the centre of the silence. You can have it. I don't need it anymore."
Zh: “我想是吧，很久以前是……”他看着你手中的红色冬日围巾。“我必须把它们像皮肤一样*剥离*下来，这样才能更接近沉默中心。你可以收下它们。我已经用不着了。”

En: Hey, you already practically sacrificed a limb when you got shot in the tribunal... it's nothing major!
Zh: 喂，你在宣判中中枪的时候，几乎已经牺牲一条腿了……这没什么大不了的！

En: Don't trust me -- trust the Mother. I'm only the messenger, homes. His voice echoes in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “不要相信我——相信母亲。我只是她的信使，兄弟。”他的声音在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: Say nothing. Be quiet for now.
Zh: 什么也不说。暂时保持安静。

En: The man leans forward a little, fixing you with a steady, unreadable gaze, then speaks...
Zh: 他向前靠近了一点点，以一种沉着、难以辨认的目光看着你，然后开了口……

En: *Estás crudo*, wey. I see deep inside you. Your body and spirit are suffering greatly from *overindulgment* and you don't even know it.
Zh: “*你是原始的*。我能看到你的内心深处。你的身体和精神因为*过度放纵*而遭受着极大的苦难，而你自己甚至都不知道。”

En: Oh, I'm very in touch with my suffering.
Zh: “哦，我跟我的痛苦经常接触。”

En: How do you know what I'm feeling?
Zh: “你怎么知道我的感受？”

En: Great. More patronising. So original.
Zh: “太棒了。又是个自认为高人一等的家伙。还真是新奇呢。”

En: I know it's hard to admit that you've got a problem. I was like you once -- couldn't take an honest look into my own heart and see I was in pain.
Zh: “我知道你很难承认自己有问题。我曾经也像你一样——不敢诚实地看看自己的内心，看到我正在遭受痛苦。”

En: Not all of it. I was like you once. You don't know all the havoc *el vino* is wreaking on your mind and your spirit. Necesitas parar, homie.
Zh: “并不是全部吧。我曾经跟你一样。你不知道*酒精*是如何肆意破坏你的思想和精神的。必须停下来，兄弟。”

En: I was like you once -- just dragging my feet to the next bottle... Shit was dark, homes.
Zh: “我曾经也像你一样——拖着我的双脚去往下一个酒瓶的方向……家这种东西，太黑暗了。”

En: I know it's hard to admit that you have a problem... I was like you once -- couldn't take an honest look at the damage *el vino* was doing to my mind and my spirit.
Zh: “我知道你很难承认自己有问题……我曾经也像你一样——不敢诚实地看一眼*酒精*对我的思想和精神造成的伤害。”

En: Hold your peace.
Zh: 冷静一点。

En: I'm not scared. I just don't understand why the female sexual organs have to be associated with weakness.
Zh: “我没有担惊受怕。我只是不理解为什么每次一提到虚弱就要用女性相关的词汇来形容。”

En: You've really got a case of frayed nerves. *Pussy* in this usage comes from *pusillanimous*. Everybody knows that.
Zh: “你还真是神经受损啊。这里*阴柔*的用法是来自于*优柔寡断*这个词。所有人都知道这一点。”

En: Here you can receive the Mother's love, and, when you're ready, she will take your hand and lift you out of the despair at the bottom of the bottle.
Zh: “在这里你能得到母亲的爱，还有，你准备好之后，她会牵起你的手，把你从深渊底部的绝望中拉出来。”

En: The lieutenant whispers to you, quietly. "This man is obviously a habitual narcotics user. Do we really need to question him?"
Zh: 警督小声地对你说着。“这人明显是个瘾君子。我们真有必要询问他吗？”

En: I'm put off by this religious stuff, he thinks -- and maybe the ceiling-climbing, too. It's all very hard to square with the lieutenant's own view of reality.
Zh: 我对宗教之类的东西不感冒，他想到——也许还有天花板攀爬技巧。这一切都很难符合警督对现实的看法。

En: He's a born preacher. Enjoy the rhetorical flourishes, but remain vigilant -- don't let him get too close. He's crazy.
Zh: 他天生就是个传教士。享受他花里胡哨的言辞和夸耀，但要保持警惕——别让他靠的太近。他是个疯子。

En: Chill out, man. Here you can receive the Mother's love, and, when you're ready, she will take your hand and lift you out of the despair at the bottom of the bottle.
Zh: “冷静点，伙计。在这里你能得到母亲的爱，还有，你准备好之后，她会牵起你的手，把你从瓶子底部的绝望中拉出来。”

En: Wait, what qualifies as *too* close?
Zh: 等等，什么才叫*太近*？

En: I think I'm willing to try crazy. Anything to stop this suffering.
Zh: 我想我愿望尝试疯狂的感觉。只要能让这种折磨停下来。

En: Giving away all of your possessions,  sacrificing a limb to appease an angry god, trying to live on air and light alone as though you were some kind of plant...
Zh: 放弃你的全部财产，献祭一只手足去副平息神明的怒意，尝试像某些植物一样，只依靠光和空气生活……

En: Vigilance is practically my middle name. I won't let anyone talk me into anything unreasonable.
Zh: 警惕这个词早已写入我的名字。我不会让任何人说服我做任何不合理的事情。

En: That's right. You've got to watch your own back.
Zh: 没错。你一定要小心点。

En: Seems it's already too late. Have fun on the kooky train.
Zh: 好像有点太迟了。祝你在乖僻的列车上享受愉快的旅途。

En: Are you human? You weren't moving like a human.
Zh: “你是人类吗？你的动作不太像人类。”

En: The sinewy figure lingers on the wooden beams, blending into the shadows.
Zh: 木梁上停着一个健美的身影，整个人都融入在阴影里。

En: How can you not know that when you both live here?
Zh: “你们俩都住在这里，你怎么会不知道？”

En: Don't really follow her comings and goings. Just see her typing on her computer now and then. We've got different interests.
Zh: “我又没有真的跟着她来来去去。只是偶尔会看见她在电脑上打字。我们的兴趣爱好不同。”

En: Look at these crazies. What is this, a fucking intervention?
Zh: 看看这些疯狂的东西。这是什么，他妈的调停吗？

En: For some reason I feel like you have a point there.
Zh: “出于某种原因，我觉得你说的有点道理。”

En: You know, alcohol is central to my identity. If I wouldn't drink I just wouldn't be *me*.
Zh: “你知道的，酒精是我身份的核心。如果不喝酒的话，我就不是*我*了。”

En: This is stupid. I don't even know what this is.
Zh: “真是太愚蠢了。我甚至都不知道这是什么。”

En: You talk funny.
Zh: “你说话很有意思。”

En: I was in a gang, wey. But my memories of that time are fading... Most of them are already gone.
Zh: “我曾经加入过一个帮派。不过那段记忆已经褪色……大部分已经不在了。”

En: I'm doing a survey of passwords and passcodes, identifying regional trends. In the interests of public safety, of course.
Zh: “我正在做一个密码和口令的调查，来确认地区趋势。当然了，是为了维护公共安全。”

En: What'd you think of that? Makes me almost pity *la nihilista pequeña* when I hear it.
Zh: “你觉得怎么样？我听到的时候，几乎都有点可怜*小虚无主义者*了呢。”

En: Don't sweat it, vato. The password is 'AFTER LIFE DEATH.'
Zh: “别担心，朋友。密码是‘生后的世界是死亡。’。”

En: That is true. But what comes after death?
Zh: 那倒是真的。但是死后会怎么样呢？

En: One day, you may be able to scale this tower. But it won't be today. Neither your sinews nor your spirit could handle this ascent.
Zh: 有一天，你也许能爬上这座塔楼，只是不在今天。不管是你的肌肉，还是你的精神，都承受不了这种高度。

En: Sorry, but you just don't have it in you right now.
Zh: 抱歉，但是现在的你真的没有这种能力。

En: Nope, not happening.
Zh: 没有，什么也没发生。

En: Okay then, thanks. [Leave.]
Zh: “那好吧，谢谢。”[离开。]

En: Have you by any chance heard the *viejita* say the password to her radiocomputer?
Zh: “你有没有碰巧听见*老婆子*对着她的无线电脑念密码呢？”

En: Too many times, ese. You need it for something?
Zh: “太多次了。你要它有什么用吗？”

En: I think we're done here, ese. The figure crawls off into the darkness above.
Zh: “我想我们到此为止吧。”那个人影爬进上方的黑暗中。

En: Hey, who's there?
Zh: “喂，谁在那里？”

En: This is the police. Show yourself!
Zh: “我是警察。快出来！”

En: Habitual alcohol use has made you into a scared little pussy, homes. But don't worry, everything's gonna be alright -- you've come to the right place.
Zh: “喝酒的习惯把你变成了一个担惊受怕的阴柔小基佬。但是别担心，一切都会好起来的——你来对地方了。”

En: Yeah, I guess I have a bit of a problem, and it's been getting out of hand lately, but...
Zh: “是啊，我猜我是有点问题，最近有些失控，不过……”

En: I'm going to ask you again: are you the crab-man?
Zh: “我再问你一次：你是蟹男吗？”

En: You must be the crab-man.
Zh: “你一定就是那个蟹男。”

En: What are you doing here?
Zh: “你在这里做什么？”

En: Do you know where the other spooker is? (Point at the strange machines around you.)
Zh: “你知道其他幽灵在哪里吗？”（指向周围的奇怪机器。）

En: You've been here a long time. Do you know why the church was abandoned?
Zh: “你在这里待了很长一段时间。你知道为什么教堂会废弃吗？”

En: Other spooker? Oh, esa viejita muy estudiosa! He laughs. "Dunno, homes."
Zh: “其他幽灵？哦，那个非常勤奋的老婆子！”他笑了。“不知道，家里吧。”

En: Oh hey, wey. There's coffee in the back... Oh, wait, I meant the Mother's love.
Zh: “哦，是的。后面有咖啡……哦，等等，我是说母亲的爱。”

En: Oh, hell no! [Run away.]
Zh: “该死，我才不要！”[跑开。]

En: You've come back to face your cacodaemons. Good. You ain't gonna outrun them if you don't drop the bottle.
Zh: “你回来面对自己心中的恶鬼了。很好。如果不放下酒瓶的话，你是超越不了它们的。”

En: Why do you keep talking about alcohol?
Zh: “你为什么老是说喝酒的问题？”

En: This is tiresome. [ Leave.]
Zh: “这真的太让人厌倦了。”[离开。]

En: The grave's comin' for your little 'identity' sooner than you think, homes. He shakes his head sadly. "You sure you're ready? The Mother could set you free from all that shit." His words echo in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “坟墓很快会来找你的那个小‘身份’，比你想象的要早，兄弟。”他悲伤的摇摇头。“你确定自己准备好了？母亲会将你从这一切苦难中拯救出来。”他的话语在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: It don't matter who *I* am, wey. I'm just bringin' you the message of the Mother's love. She don't want you to hang yourself on your own stubbornness. His words echo in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “*我*是谁不重要。我只是为你传达母亲的爱意。她不想让你因为自己的顽固自取灭亡。”他的话语在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: Don't trust me -- trust the Mother. I'm only the messenger, homes. His voice echoes in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “不要相信我——相信母亲。我只是她的信使，兄弟。”他的声音在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: You know what this is. It's truth, homes. Join me, become one with it -- aflame with the Mother's love. His voice echoes in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “你知道是什么。这就是真相，兄弟。加入我，成为其中之一——与母亲的爱一同燃烧。”他的声音在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: That's all well and good, but we need to talk about the unlicensed occupation of ecclesiastic property. I'm in a rush.
Zh: “你说的都挺好的，不过我们要讨论一下非法占用教会财产的问题。我赶时间。”

En: That's all well and good, but we need to talk about the unlicensed occupation of ecclesiastic property.
Zh: “你说的都挺好的，不过我们要讨论一下非法占用教会财产的问题。”

En: The only thing we need to talk about is how the Mother can really help you, homes. His voice echoes in the cold air of the church.
Zh: “我们唯一需要讨论的只有母亲能怎么帮到你，兄弟。”他的声音在教堂冰冷的空气中回荡。

En: Wait, what does that even mean?
Zh: 等等，这到底是什么意思啊？

En: This is the Church of the Mother of Silence. You are welcome here. He sways gently on the beams, waiting for you to take it all in.
Zh: “这是沉默之母的教堂。欢迎你来到这里。”他在横梁上轻轻摇摆，等着你接受这一切。

En: Haven't you ever met a Mesque before, *cabron*? Surprising number of us around Revachol...
Zh: 他轻声笑了。“你之前从没遇到过梅斯克人吗，*公羊*？瑞瓦肖周边的人数不可少呢……”

En: So you're another immigrant, come to take up space and steal jobs...
Zh: “所以你又是一个移民，来这里占据我们的空间，偷走我们的工作……”

En: Space? Jobs? He laughs. "Do you see anyone taking swings at me for my place on the rafters?..."
Zh: “空间？工作？”他笑了。“你见过有人因为我在椽木上的位置而攻击我吗？……”

En: ...your place among your fellows, your place in the world. I ain't got no use for such a place anymore.
Zh: “……是你在同伴之间的位置，你在这个世界的位置。我已经不再需要这种地方了。”

En: My name sets me apart from my fellows. How often do you meet a Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau?
Zh: 我的名字让我显得很特别。你多久会遇见一个叫拉斐尔·安普罗修斯·库斯托的人？”

En: Maybe that's why I feel so lonely -- without a name, I have no place.
Zh: “也许这就是我感到如此孤独的原因——没有名字的我，没有立足之地。”

En: That's not quite right, but you're getting somewhere. He smiles enigmatically. "Any one of us could have been anything else..."
Zh: 他神秘地笑了。“其实不太对，不过你确实有些进展。我们任何一个人可能曾经都是别的什么人……”

En: We are all one, who sing the Mother's glory.
Zh: “我们都是一体的，歌颂着母亲的荣耀。”

En: The right place for what?
Zh: “什么对的地方？”

En: What are you doing here?
Zh: “你在这里做什么？”

En: Hold on, stop it right there. "Pussy"? What's with this?
Zh: 等一下，先停一下。“阴柔”？这是怎么回事？

En: I'm here to receive the Mother's love. You can, too, homes. And, when you're ready, she will take your hand and lift you out of the despair at the bottom of the bottle.
Zh: “我来这里是为了得到母亲的爱。你可以的，我的兄弟。你准备好之后，她会牵起你的手，把你从瓶子底部的绝望中拉出来。”

En: I'm a police man. I need to talk to you about police things. I don't know anything about 'alcohol use.'
Zh: “我是个警察。我需要跟你讨论一下警察的事情。我不知道你说的‘喝酒’是什么意思。”

En: Oh yeah, sure. His eyes remain fixed on yours. "You don't know anything about alcohol use. You've got it *all under control*, wey. I can smell the control all the way over here."
Zh: “哦，当然可以。”他的目光没有从你身上移开。“你不知道喝酒是什么意思。一切*都在你的掌控之中*。我在这里都能闻到控制的味道。”

En: Oh yeah, sure. His eyes remain fixed on yours. "You don't know anything about alcohol use. You've got it *all under control*, wey. I saw you hide that control behind your back."
Zh: “哦，当然可以。”他的目光没有从你身上移开。“你不知道喝酒是什么意思。一切*都在你的掌控之中*。我看见你把那份控制藏在身后。”

En: I'm a police man. I need to talk to you about police things. I don't know anything about 'alcohol use.' (Hide the bottle behind your back.)
Zh: “我是个警察。我需要跟你讨论一下警察的事情。我不知道你说的‘喝酒’是什么意思。”（把酒瓶子藏在背后。）

En: I was like you once -- just dragging my feet to the next bottle... Shit was dark, homes.
Zh: “我曾经也像你一样——拖着我的双脚去往下一个酒瓶的方向……家这种东西，太黑暗了。”

En: I know it's hard to admit that you have a problem... I was like you once -- couldn't take an honest look at the damage *el vino* was doing to my mind and my spirit.
Zh: “我知道你很难承认自己有问题……我曾经也像你一样——不敢诚实地看一眼*酒精*对我的思想和精神造成的伤害。”

En: This conversation is making me uncomfortable. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “这样的对话让我感觉不太舒服。我们说点别的吧。”

En: Okay, fine, I'm struggling. But you don't need to lecture me -- I know what I need to do.
Zh: “好吧，没错，我是在挣扎。不过不需要你教训我——我知道自己该做什么。”

En: Not all of it. I was like you once. You don't know all the havoc *el vino* is wreaking on your mind and your spirit. Necesitas parar, homie.
Zh: “并不是全部吧。我曾经跟你一样。你不知道*酒精*是如何肆意破坏你的思想和精神的。必须停下来，兄弟。”

En: I know it's hard to admit that you've got a problem. I was like you once -- couldn't take an honest look into my own heart and see I was in pain.
Zh: “我知道你很难承认自己有问题……我曾经也像你一样——不敢诚实地看一眼*酒精*对我的思想和精神造成的伤害。”

En: I'm doing just fine, thank you!
Zh: “我挺好的，谢谢你！”

En: You know, actually, since we're here -- you may want to pay attention to what the ceiling climber is saying.
Zh: “你知道的，其实吧，既然我们现在人在这里——你可能需要注意一下天花板攀爬者在说些什么。”

En: You know, he may be strange, but you might want to pay attention to what he's saying.
Zh: 你知道的，他也许是有点奇怪，不过你应该注意他在说什么。

En: If the lieutenant agrees, then maybe, just maybe -- you should pay attention?
Zh: 如果警督同意，那么也许，只是也许——你应该注意些？

En: Look at these crazies. What is this shit?
Zh: 看看这些疯狂的东西。这是什么狗屁？

En: Coffee in the back? Something familiar about that. Coffee and stale cookies...
Zh: “后面有咖啡？”听起来好像有点熟悉。咖啡和腐败的曲奇……

En: What was that about coffee? I feel like I've heard it somewhere before...
Zh: “咖啡怎么了？我觉得之前好像在什么地方听过……”

En: Look, man. I'm at liberty to talk about the sacred blaze of the Mother's glorious heart. But not about the coffee.
Zh: “你看，伙计。我可以随时跟你讨论慈悲母亲的神圣光辉。但是咖啡不行。”

En: Is that... a man? A man made of the same stuff as the carpentry of the building. He is studying you intently.
Zh: 那是……一个人吗？一个像是跟建筑木器拥有相同材质的人。他正一心一意地打量着你。

En: The shadow is... a man? But a man made of the same stuff as the carpentry of the building. He is studying you intently.
Zh: 那个阴影是……一个人？不过像是一个跟建筑木器拥有相同材质的人。他正一心一意地端详着你。

En: Is that... a man? Looks more like part of the carpentry of the building came alive and is now studying you intently.
Zh: 那是……一个人吗？看起来更像是建筑木器的一部分活了过来，正在一心一意地端详你。

En: The crab-man!
Zh: 蟹男！

En: Uhm... are you the crab-man?
Zh: “呃……你是蟹男吗？”

En: Hey and what was that about *the bottle* again? You haven't even drank that much lately! Lay off it already. Sheesh.
Zh: 喂，那个*瓶子*是怎么回事来着？你最近根本都没怎么喝酒！已经戒掉了。难以置信。

En: I'm actually here on behalf of some young people looking to establish a night club...
Zh: “其实我是代表一群年轻人来的，他们想要建一家夜店……”

En: I'm actually here on behalf of some young people looking to establish a night club, and after that I need to get to this island, so could we just...
Zh: “其实我是代表一群年轻人来的，他们想要建一家夜店，在那之后我需要前往这座小岛，这样我们就能……”

En: This conversation is making me uncomfortable. Let's talk about something else.
Zh: “这样的对话让我感觉不太舒服。我们说点别的吧。”

En: You don't know me. You don't know what it's like -- there's a shadow pursuing me!
Zh: “你不知道我。你根本不懂我的感受——有阴影在追赶我！”

En: You don't know me! You don't know what it's like! There's something... something white pursuing me! White and very sad...
Zh: “你不知道我。你根本不懂我的感受！有什么东西……什么白色的东西在追赶我！很白，很悲伤……”

En: You don't know me. You don't know what I've been through!
Zh: “你不知道我。你不知道我都经历过什么！”

En: (Adjust your tie.) "Who do you think you are? Some crazy guy under the roof? What is this shit?"
Zh: （调整一下你的领带。）“你以为你是谁？住在屋顶下面的疯子吗？这都是什么鬼？”

En: Who do you think you are? Some crazy guy under the roof? What is this shit?
Zh: “你以为你是谁？住在屋顶下面的疯子吗？这都是什么鬼？”

En: 'Tis not an act, my liege -- saving, perchance, he hath deceived his very self. This man is a zealot.
Zh: 这不是在演戏，大人——考虑一下，或许，他连自己都欺骗了。这男人是一个狂热信徒。

En: Whatever it is, he's quite confident about it -- just look how gracefully he sways.
Zh: 不管是什么，他都非常自信——你看看他摆动地多优雅。

En: You have no idea what the fuck he's talking about. Is he just trying to throw you off your game?
Zh: 你不知道他到底在说什么鬼东西。他是想让你表现失常吗？

En: Don't think I've met one yet, homes. He studies you silently for a moment.
Zh: 他默默地研究了你一会儿。“我觉得之前应该没见过，兄弟。”

En: Don't worry, man, you got a place -- right here. He sounds happy saying this, his limbs a mere shadow below the ceiling.
Zh: “别担心，兄弟，你有的——就在这里。”他听起来很开心，四肢在天花板下面不过是一团阴影。

En: This Mother of Silence -- you mean *her*? (Point to the window.)
Zh: “这位沉默之母——是一个*女性*吗？”（指向窗户。）

En: Who's this Mother of Silence you keep talking about?
Zh: “你一直说的这个沉默之母是谁？”

En: No, no, no, there's a *new* god in town. And she can't be painted or sculpted, because she has no limbs or even a face. She is the end.
Zh: “不不不，兄弟，她是城里*新兴*的神明。而且她无法被描绘或者雕刻出来，因为她没有四肢，甚至没有脸庞。她是终结。”

En: Oh, that's no simple question, ese. She is one who can't be painted or sculpted...
Zh: “哦，这可不是个简单的问题。她是无法被描绘，也无法被雕刻出来的……”

En: To be fair, it's really more like a spider.
Zh: “说句公道话，其实真的更像蜘蛛。”

En: He considers this for a moment. "I always thought of myself more like a *flame*. Flickering along the rafters and beams." He pauses. "It may be that I gotta work on my technique."
Zh: 他考虑了一会。“我一直觉得自己更像是一团*火焰*。在椽木和横梁之间忽隐忽现。”他停了一会儿。“也许我应该磨练一下自己的技术。”

En: He chuckles. "I am, at least in part, homes... until the Mother's love burns away the crude distinctions of the body."
Zh: 他轻笑到。“我是，至少部分是，兄弟……直到母亲的爱烧尽身体的那粗糙的特质。”

En: Wait, did you also carve all those sculptures? (Point at the nearest pillar.)
Zh: “等等，这些雕塑也都是你做的吗？”（指着最近处的柱子。）

En: That's not the only technique he's working on. Look at those carved sculptures. And is that a satchel of tools over there?
Zh: 他磨练的可不仅仅是这门技术。那边是个小工具包吗？

En: So you used to be in a gang, but you don't really remember it? Sounds convenient.
Zh: “所以说你曾经加入过帮派，但是却不记得了？这借口很方便啊。”

En: I lost my memory, too. And it haunts me.
Zh: “我也失忆了。而且它困扰着我。”

En: I lost my memory, too, but I like it. It's like I get to create a whole new me, start again from scratch.
Zh: “我也失忆了，不过我喜欢这样。就好像我可以塑造一个全新的我，从零开始。”

En: (Shake your head.) "So many people losing their memory... A certain portent of *doom*..."
Zh: （摇摇头。）“太多人失忆了……这是*末日*的某种前兆……”

En: Convenience has got nothing to do with what goes on up there... He gazes up at the ceiling.
Zh: “方便跟上面发生的事一点关系都没有……”他抬头望向天花板。

En: There's a profound longing in that gaze. Like he wants to be one with whatever is up there, shrouded in darkness.
Zh: 他凝望的目光中有一种深厚的渴望。感觉他很想跟上面那片黑暗中某种不知名的东西在一起。

En: No, man. You gotta let that shit go. He looks at you as if he wants to pat you on the back. "Then the Mother's light touch will fill you with rapture..."
Zh: 他看着你，就像是想拍拍你的后背一样。“不，伙计。你应该放下那个鬼玩意。然后母亲的光辉会触碰你，让你满心欢喜……”

En: That's not really the point, ese. He frowns. "You gotta give yourself over to service... Service of the Mother, that is..."
Zh: 他皱起眉头。“其实这并不是重点。你应该全身心地奉献……奉献给母亲……”

En: Sure am. Whittling wood used to be something I just did to busy my hands...
Zh: “当然是了。削木头曾经是我让双手忙起来的方式……”

En: ...now I use those same hands in service of something greater than my own restlessness.
Zh: “……现在我把相同的这双手，奉献给比自己的不安更加伟大的东西。”

En: Naw, ese, it's not like that. Best thing that could happen to you -- losing your memory...
Zh: “不，不是这样的。能在你身上发生的最好的事——就是失去记忆……”

En: Do you remember your name, sir?
Zh: “你还记得你的名字吗，先生？”

En: The lieutenant is not particularly interested in this information. He's just trying to assert some control over the conversation.
Zh: 警督对这条信息不是特别感兴趣。他只是试着对这次谈话施加一些控制。

En: Tiago is my name. But those syllables don't mean much to me these days. A name isn't just your identity, but also, so to speak...
Zh: “我的名字叫蒂亚戈。不过最近这个音节对我来说已经没有多大意义了。名字不仅仅是你的身份，而且可以说……”

En: Tiago's my name, by the way, though the syllables don't mean much to me. A name isn't just your identity, but also, so to speak...
Zh: “我的名字叫蒂亚戈，顺便说一句，这个音节对我来说已经没有多大意义了。名字不仅仅是你的身份，而且可以说……”

En: The lieutenant raises his eyebrows and coughs.
Zh: 警督抬起眉头，咳嗽几声。

En: That was him suppressing a chuckle.
Zh: 他是在忍笑。

En: A body is only crude so long as it's unformed, son. That's where your training comes in.
Zh: 只有未成形的身体才是粗糙的，小子。这时候训练就派上用场了。

En: Sorry, you just weren't moving like a human.
Zh: “抱歉，你的动作实在不像人类。”

En: What does he mean 'in part'?
Zh: 他说‘部分’是什么意思？

En: If you're not a crab, then what are you?
Zh: “如果你不是一只螃蟹，那是什么？”

En: I think having a body is great! I don't want to burn away any of its distinctions!
Zh: “我觉得身体的存在是一件很棒的事情！我不想烧尽它的任何特质！”

En: That's because you're soaked through and through with *al gul*, bro. Keeps you heavy and grounded. Then fools you into thinking that's a *good* thing.
Zh: “那是因为你被*阿尔古*浸透了全身。让你变得沉重，无法离开地面。然后欺骗你这是一件*好事*。”

En: If you was to surrender to the Mother -- you know, get a taste of *saeraffic* existence -- you'd never wanna go back.
Zh: “如果你想向母亲诚服——你知道的，品尝一下那*纯洁而美丽*的存在——你永远也不会想回头的。”

En: And it's great! Such diversity is a boon to the economy.
Zh: “而且它很棒！这种多样性是利好经济的。”

En: What's 'Mesque'?
Zh: “‘梅斯克’是什么？”

En: You sound like you're not from around here.
Zh: “听起来你不是本地人。”

En: I don't know, I think he may be onto something.
Zh: 我不知道，我觉得他说的也许有点道理。

En: I don't want to argue with him. He's kind of scary.
Zh: 我不想跟他讨论这个。他有点吓人。

En: What's next? A raw food diet? You're a disappointment.
Zh: 接下来是什么？只吃生食来减肥？你真是让人失望。

En: I'm going to ask you again: Are you the crab-man?
Zh: “我再问你一次：你是蟹男吗？”

En: Where did you come from?
Zh: “你从哪里来的？”

En: Did you just climb down from the church tower?
Zh: “你是刚从教堂塔楼爬下来的吗？”

En: Up there, homes. He smiles strangely and turns his head up to look into the darkness of the church tower.
Zh: 他怪异地笑了笑，然后抬头深望教堂塔楼的那片黑暗。“从上面，兄弟。”

En: Sure did, homes.
Zh: “当然了，兄弟。”

En: You know, there are some speedfreaks around here who call you 'the crab-man'.
Zh: “你知道吗，附近有些瘾君子把你叫做蟹男。”

En: *Un Cangrejo*! He laughs. "Never thought of myself as a crab. More like... a flame flickering among the rafters and beams..." He pauses. "It may be that I gotta work on my technique."
Zh: “*一只螃蟹*！”他笑了。“我从没觉得自己像一只螃蟹，而更像是……一团在椽木和横梁之间闪烁的火焰。”他停了一会儿。“也许我应该磨练一下自己的技术。”

En: That's not the only technique he's working on. Look at those carved sculptures. And is that a satchel of tools over there?
Zh: 他磨练的可不仅仅是这门技术。看看那些雕塑。还有，那边是个小工具包吗？

En: Wait, did you carve those sculptures? (Point at the nearest pillar.)
Zh: “等等，这些雕塑也都是你做的吗？”（指着最近处的柱子。）

En: Sure am. Whittling wood used to be something I just did to busy my hands...
Zh: “当然是了。削木头曾经是我让双手忙起来的方式……”

En: ...now I use those same hands in service of something greater than my own restlessness.
Zh: “……现在我把相同的这双手，奉献给比自己的不安更加伟大的东西。”

En: Just thought you should know.
Zh: “我只是觉得你应该知道。”

En: It don't bother me none. Crab's no worse than a man, if you think about it.
Zh: “我一点也不在乎。如果你仔细想想的话，螃蟹并不比人类差。”

En: (Rub your chin.) "You've got some nice... curves going there."
Zh: （抚摸自己的下巴。）“你雕出了……不少美妙的曲线啊”

En: (Squint your eyes.) "These seem a tad... derivative."
Zh: （斜着眼睛。）“这些雕刻看上去好像……只是一些仿品。”

En: You're promoting the objectification of women with your reactionary depiction of female bodies.
Zh: “你这是在用反动的方式描绘女性的躯体并以来物化女性。”

En: There must be something illegal about living in a church ceiling. I don't know what yet, but there must be.
Zh: “住在教堂的天花板上肯定有些非法的成分。虽然我还不知道是什么，但是肯定有。”

En: Oh, man, I'd love to see that statute. He pauses to think. "I'm sure I'd be 'trespassing' if the church was in use -- but it's not, hombre."
Zh: “哦，兄弟，我很看看那道法令。”他停下来想了一会儿。“如果教堂正常使用的话，我确实自己是犯了‘非法入侵’罪——不过它没有，兄弟。”

En: Okay, I have other questions.
Zh: “好吧，我还有其他问题。”

En: Is this what you were doing reps for? So you could slink from an argument about the importance of cultivating the body?
Zh: 这就是你重复练习的原因？这样你就可以在争论雕塑身体的重要性的时候逃走？

En: What is this? Is this what you've been training for? So you could wuss out when push comes to shove?
Zh: 这是干什么？你接受训练就是为了干这个的？就为了在紧要关头的时候畏首畏尾？

En: I've done my share of illegal shit. Used to be in a gang, the whole deal. But even memories of that time are fading... Most of them are already gone.
Zh: “我已经干过非法勾当了。曾经加入过帮派，来真的那种。不过就连那个时候的记忆都在逐渐褪色……大部分已经没有了。”

En: Well, at least, you're human. That's a relief.
Zh: “呃，至少你是个人类。那我就放心多了。”

En: Glad you're feeling more comfortable. He grins. "First-timers are always nervous."
Zh: 他咧嘴一笑。“很高兴你感觉舒服多了。第一次总是会比较紧张的。”

En: Yeah! My *body* is my church!
Zh: 是啊！我的*身体*就是我的教堂！

En: Tell him, son! Show him you're not going to be bent out of shape by his *philosophies*.
Zh: 告诉他，小子！展示给他看，你是不会被他那套*哲学*搞得大发雷霆的。

En: He laughs. "Not sure I'm contributing to the economy none, homes."
Zh: 他笑了。“不确定我对经济有没有贡献，兄弟。”

En: You know, I'm not so sure myself anymore. He thinks for a moment. "Don't really matter, though."
Zh: 他想了一会儿。“你知道吗，我自己都不那么确定了。不过也没什么要紧的。”

En: It's all just for the Mother, man. No need to overthink it.
Zh: “这全都是为了母亲，兄弟。没必要想太多。”

En: It's all just for the Mother, man. No need to overthink it.
Zh: “这全都是为了母亲，兄弟。没必要想太多。”

En: I thought you'd be offended. Go and scare them or something.
Zh: “我还以为你会生气呢。跑去吓吓他们什么的。”

En: I can't leave my post here. He smiles. "Besides, it don't bother me none. Crab's no worse than a man, if you think about it."
Zh: “我不会离开这里的岗位。”他笑了。“再说，我已经不会为这种事烦扰了。如果仔细想想的话，螃蟹并不比人类差。”

En: She is a cavity, in the dark, beyond sense. She saved me... but I couldn't describe her to you. No one can, homes, and no one ever will.
Zh: “她是一个黑暗中的空洞，超越感知之外。她拯救了我……但我无法向你描述她。没人可以，兄弟，永远也不可以。”

En: Some ravers want to turn this place into a nightclub.
Zh: “有些派对狂想把这个地方变成一家夜店。”

En: The ones in the tent outside, right? I seen 'em. Guessing they're the ones who call me a crab? Probably scared of me...
Zh: “外面帐篷里的那些人，是吗？我看见过他们。我猜就是他们叫我螃蟹的吧？可能有点怕我……”

En: The ones in the tent outside, right? I seen 'em. Think they're scared of me.
Zh: “外面帐篷里的那些人，是吗？我看见过他们。我觉得他们可能有点怕我……”

En: Wait, do they have *reason* to be scared?
Zh: “等等，他们有*理由*害怕吗？”

En: So, what do you think? About the nightclub, that is.
Zh: “所以，你怎么觉得？夜店的事。”

En: Turning a church into a nightclub -- I have to say it's a great idea. You should support it.
Zh: “把教堂变成一家夜店——我必须说这是一个很棒的主意。你应该支持。”

En: I trust you won't get in the way of some enterprising youth? Their business looks promising.
Zh: “我相信你应该不会妨碍那些有事业心的年轻人吧？他们的生意看起来挺有前途的。”

En: Naw, man. They look pretty funny. And I don't harm no one anymore anyway.
Zh: “没有，兄弟。他们看起来挺有意思的。而且我再也不会伤害任何人了。”

En: Why not? They wouldn't bother me none. I'm usually way up there, imbibing. Ain't no music on Earth that can reach where I go.
Zh: “为什么不呢？他们不会打扰到我的。我通常会待在上面，吸收养分。世界上没有任何音乐能够到达我前往的地方。”

En: Absolutely, man. Wouldn't bother me none to have 'em spin music in here. I'm usually way up there, imbibing.
Zh: “绝对的，兄弟。在这里稍微放点音乐是不会打扰到我的。我经常都待在上面，吸收养分。”

En: Wouldn't bother me none to have 'em spin music in here. I'm usually way up there, imbibing.
Zh: “在这里稍微放点音乐是不会打扰到我的。我经常都待在上面，吸收养分。”

En: Might even be nice to have some company...
Zh: “有人陪说不定还挺好的呢……”

En: He said that in spite of himself. He's more attached to the human than he'd like to think.
Zh: 他不由自主地说出了这句话。他比自己想象中更依恋人类。

En: This is a special place. There's a perforation in the world up there. A way out, into nothingness. He nods toward the ceiling. "This church was built around it, for purposes of veneration."
Zh: “这是个特别的地方。上面有一个世界的穿孔。一个出口，深入虚无。”他朝着天花板点点头。“这座教堂就是围绕着它建起来的，以示崇拜。”

En: I circle it, nurtured by the silence bestowed by the Mother. One of these days, I'll be pure enough to go drink from it directly.
Zh: “我围绕着它，被沉默之母赐予的礼物滋养着。总有一天，我会变得足够纯净，就能直接从中饮取了。”

En: You sure you didn't just switch one drug for another?
Zh: “难道这不是仅仅换了一种毒品吗？”

En: Police raid a while back, he responds, his voice suddenly flat.
Zh: “警察不久之前突袭过，”他回应着，声音突然变得单调。

En: You've been here for a long time. Did you see the police raid that took place here?
Zh: “你在这里待过很长时间了。你见过这里发生的警察突袭吗？”

En: It's not like that at all, man. It's just faith and joyful service.
Zh: “不是这样的，兄弟。这只是信仰和愉悦的服务。”

En: Faith is a kind of drug.
Zh: “信仰是一种毒品。”

En: Let's agree to disagree. (Proceed.)
Zh: “那就让我们求同存异吧。”（继续。）

En: When, then?
Zh: 那是什么时候？

En: I'm a man in his prime. I should be able to do anything. This isn't fair.
Zh: 我是个正处于全盛时期的男人。我应该无所不能。这不公平。

En: I was being insensitive. Sorry. Let's move on. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我有点麻木不仁了。抱歉，我们继续吧。”（继续。）

En: I've heard that before, wey. He shakes his head. "And I know I can't convince you on the spot. But think -- when's the last time you woke up from *silent communion* with a hangover, regretting what you did last night?"
Zh: 他摇摇头。“我之前听说过，是的，我知道自己无法当场说服你。不过想一想——你上次带着宿醉从*无声交流*中醒来，为前晚发生的事后悔不已是什么时候？”

En: No worries, man. I know this shit takes time.
Zh: “别担心，兄弟。我知道这种事需要时间的。”

En: Right. I had other questions...
Zh: “好吧。我还有其他问题……”

En: When your mind is clear of the neurotoxins you've been injecting it with for years -- and your body regains some nimbleness and swiftness.
Zh: 当你的大脑终于从这些年来注射过的神经毒素中清醒过来的时候，你的身体重新恢复了一些敏捷和速度。

En: Too gleeful, those words -- he is lying. Not to you -- to his very own self.
Zh: 那些词语，过于兴奋——他在撒谎。不是对您——而是对他自己。

En: You're not doing *that* bad. But, yeah, there's some distance to go.
Zh: 你也没有*那么*糟糕吧。不过，没错，确实还有一段距离。

En: Yeah, you were getting a tad too ambitious there.
Zh: 是啊，你有点太野心勃勃了。

En: Seriously what were you even thinking? You haven't been putting the work in to pull off a stunt like climbing to great heights like a cat.
Zh: 说真的，你到底在想什么啊？想做出像猫一样爬得那么高的特技，也没见你真的付出过什么努力啊。

En: And your mind's pretty clear, all things considered. In fact, it's too clear for you to be buying into this climbing vision quest.
Zh: 就各方面而言，你的大脑非常清醒。事实上，对你来说有些太过清醒，不太能接受这次的攀爬追梦。

En: But, can't deny it -- your mind's pretty clear, all things considered. In fact, it's too clear for you to be buying into this climbing vision quest.
Zh: 但是，不能否认——就各方面而言，你的大脑很清醒。事实上，对你来说有些太过清醒，不太能接受这次的攀爬追梦。

En: I don't care about the physical stuff -- I'm in it for the spiritual experience.
Zh: 我不在乎身体什么的——我是为了这种精神体验。

En: (Look at your bottle.)  But it makes me feel more powerful...
Zh: （看着你的酒瓶。）但是，它让我感觉更强大了……

En: I feel like I am failing as a man. I am ashamed.
Zh: 我觉得自己是个失败的男人。我很羞愧。

En: Some people will find that attitude admirable... but it's not going to help you scale these beams.
Zh: 有人会觉得这种态度很令人钦佩……不过这样也没办法帮你爬上那些横梁。

En: Oh well, didn't really care about climbing up some stupid beams anyway.
Zh: 好吧，反正我不是真在乎爬上那个愚蠢的横梁什么的。

En: Yeah, sure. There are other things we could do. But, man, it would have been cool.
Zh: 是啊，没错。我们还有其他事可以做。但是，朋友，这样本来应该挺酷的。

En: You might be that numerically, but you've really done a number on yourself too.
Zh: 用数字表示也许是的，不过你真的给自己造成了不少伤害。

En: That's just whiny bullshit. Get it together.
Zh: 那只不过是在他妈的乱发牢骚。沉着一点。

En: There still may be time to recuperate. Just grit your teeth.
Zh: 也许还有时间可以恢复。咬紧牙关吧。

En: When your mind is clear of the neurotoxins you've been injecting it with for years, and your body regains some nimbleness and swiftness, you might achieve such a feat.
Zh: 当你的大脑终于从这些年来注射过的神经毒素中清醒过来的时候，你的身体重新恢复了一些敏捷和速度，也许你能完成这样的壮举。

En: What were you even trying to do, man? I believe you can overcome your problems eventually, if you purge yourself of your wilfulness and seek the Mother's guidance. But *this*, right now, would be some suicidal shit.
Zh: “你到底打算干什么，兄弟？如果你能消除自己的倔强，寻求母亲的指引，我相信你最终能解决自己的问题。不过现在，*这么*做无异于自杀。”

En: Hey, man. I see it in you -- you are prepared to surrender yourself to the fire of revelation. But you're still a bit shaky spiritually. He looks you over. "You're also just, physically, shaking."
Zh: “喂，兄弟。我在你身上看到了——你已经准备好诚服于真相的火焰。但在精神上，你还是有些动摇。”他看着你。“而且，在身体上，还是有些颤抖。”

En: What will happen once you drink from this 'perforation'?
Zh: “你直接从这个‘穿孔’饮取之后，会发生什么呢？”

En: I will be incinerated, but not destroyed -- finally at one with the state of the world before reality began.
Zh: “我会被烧成灰烬，但这并不是毁灭——最终我会与现实诞生之前的世界融为一体。”

En: Is this what you meant by 'saeraffic existence'?
Zh: “你说的‘纯洁的存在’，就是这个意思吗？”

En: I still don't understand what you're doing in the church.
Zh: “我还是不明白你在教堂做什么。”

En: He said something about "saeraffic existence" before. Maybe this is what he was talking about?
Zh: 他之前提到过‘纯洁的存在’。也许他说的就是这个？

En: Right on, homes. You're catching on. Yeah, you see, I'm sort of a saeraff to the Mother... I sing her glory.
Zh: “你说得对，兄弟。你开始有些懂了。是的，你可以看见，我有点像母亲的祭司……我为她的光辉而歌唱。”

En: I'm a saeraff, homes. I sing the Mother's glory.
Zh: “我是一个祭司，兄弟。我为母亲的光辉而歌唱。”

En: It doesn't really make sense for you to sing if she's the Mother of *Silence*.
Zh: “如果她是*沉默*之母的话，你的歌唱真没什么意义。”

En: You should sing for me, the Superstar Cop. (Point your thumbs at yourself.)
Zh: “你应该为我，明星警探歌唱。”（用大拇指指向自己。）

En: You should sing about the coming apocalypse, warn the unseeing.
Zh: “你应该歌唱即将降临的大灾难，警告那些视而不见的人。”

En: Can you sing for me? Sing for me something.
Zh: “你能为我歌唱吗？给我唱点什么吧。”

En: I ain't from no *marietti*, if that's what you're thinking. And the song I sing is silent as the Mother.
Zh: “我不是*流浪乐队*的，如果你想的是这个的话。而且我吟唱的歌曲如母亲一样沉默。”

En: He laughs. "I don't mean *literal* singing, homes. This is the Mother of Silence we're talking about. It's the singing of a burning heart..."
Zh: 他笑了。“我的意思不是*字面意义*上的歌唱，兄弟。我们说的可是沉默之母。这是一颗燃烧的心脏在歌唱……”

En: Wait, he says he didn't remember anything about being Mesque -- and now he remembers this?
Zh: 等等，他说不记得自己是梅斯克的事——结果现在又想起这个了？

En: He lost his cool there for a moment. Seems you hit some nerve.
Zh: 他一时失去了冷静。看来你说到点子上了。

En: Marietti is a Mesque style of music and dance commonly seen at all manner of festivities -- especially weddings. It's delightfully quaint, owing to its peasant origins.
Zh: “流浪乐队”是一种梅斯克风格的音乐，常见于各种各样的庆典——特别是婚礼。因为起源于农民，让它有种欢快自由的古怪感。

En: I thought you said you didn't remember what it meant to be Mesque.
Zh: “我以为你说不记得做梅斯克人意味着什么。”

En: Accept this contradiction. The man's obviously deranged.
Zh: 接受这个矛盾。这个男人明显有些精神错乱。

En: Things come back... in flashes. He thinks for a moment. "When something reminds me... Sometimes they even seem important again. *Really* important. But then, thanks to the Mother, they're gone again."
Zh: 他想了一会儿。“回想起那些事情……都发生在一刹那间。当某些事情提醒我的时候……有时候，它们甚至变得好像很重要。*真的*很重要。不过之后，多亏了母亲，它们又再度离去了。”

En: ...you may be thinking, 'But fire crackles!' No, homes, that's the material that's burning. The flames themselves are without sound.
Zh: “……你也许会想，‘但是火花会噼啪作响啊！’不，兄弟，燃烧的是物质。火焰本身是没有声音的。”

En: But I was talking about the singing of a burning heart... You may be thinking, 'But fire crackles!' No, homes, that's the material that's burning. The flames themselves are without sound.
Zh: “但我说的是一颗燃烧心脏的歌唱……你也许会想，‘但是火花会噼啪作响啊！’不，兄弟，燃烧的是物质。火焰本身是没有声音的。”

En: Did you suffer some singing tragedy?
Zh: “你是遭受了什么歌唱悲剧吗？”

En: He sighs. "Things come back... in flashes. When something reminds me... Sometimes they even seem important again. *Really* important. But then, thanks to the Mother, they're gone again."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“回想起那些事情……都发生在一刹那间。当某些事情提醒我的时候……有时候，它们甚至变得好像很重要。*真的*很重要。不过之后，多亏了母亲，它们又再度离去了。”

En: That seems to upset you. But you said you didn't really remember your past.
Zh: “看起来好像让你有些不安。不过你说你真的不记得过去的事了。”

En: I was rude. I'm sorry.
Zh: “是我太无礼了。抱歉。”

En: He sighs. "It don't matter none, homes. Not anymore."
Zh: 他叹了口气。“没关系的，兄弟。不会再有关系了。”

En: Maybe I did. He sighs. "It don't matter no more."
Zh: “也许我有过。”他叹了口气。“不过已经不重要了。”

En: I recently tore it up on the stage with my singing.
Zh: “我最近在舞台上唱了歌昏天暗地。”

En: I recently suffered a singing tragedy myself.
Zh: “我自己最近遭受了一次歌唱悲剧。”

En: Don't rub it in.
Zh: 别再提这件事了。

En: You don't need to share your shame.
Zh: 你不需要分享自己的耻辱。

En: What you're suffering from is *el vino*-- it's left you alone with your selfishness. That's your tragedy, wey.
Zh: “你遭受的是*酒精*——它把你和你的自私自利单独留在一起。这才是你的悲剧，没错。”

En: Good for you, man. But you must know that nothing you achieve's gonna make you happy and loved like you think you wanna be -- comes with being a slave to *el vino*.
Zh: “对你有好处，兄弟。但你必须知道，你获得的一切成就都无法让你高兴，也无法让你得到那种想要被爱的感觉——如果是作为酒精的奴隶的话。”

En: If you could submit to the mother -- sing her praises with your burning heart -- you could be free from selfish desire.
Zh: “如果你可以诚服于母亲——用自己燃烧的心脏歌唱她的赞美诗——你就能从私欲中解脱出来。”

En: I think love might have been my drug of choice. And I think I'm still hungover from it.
Zh: “我想爱情可能是我选择的毒药。而且我还在为此而难过。”

En: She took you for a good spin, huh? He looks at you gravely. "Don't worry, bro, that love is but a drop compared to the ocean of the Mother's love..."
Zh: 他严肃地看着你。“她真的把你耍得团团转，是吗？别担心，兄弟，如果母亲的爱是一片汪洋，那你的这份爱不过是浩瀚中的一滴。”

En: I guess you have a point. (Proceed.)
Zh: “我猜你说的有道理。”（继续。）

En: ...the Mother will eat all of you, and never spit you out.
Zh: “……母亲会吃掉你的全部，永远不会把你吐出来。”

En: I know it'll take time. Don't sweat it.
Zh: “我知道需要花点时间。别担心。”

En: Keep the flame in your soul alive, though. One day you'll make it up here, if you put in the work.
Zh: “不过，要保证你的灵魂之火不要熄灭。如果你付出努力，总有一天你会与自己和解的。”

En: Did you witness it?
Zh: “你亲眼见到的吗？”

En: Could it be that he just doesn't want to discuss a police raid with another police officer?
Zh: 难道他不想跟警察讨论警察突袭的事？

En: Did you see what happened?
Zh: “你看见发生什么了吗？”

En: How did you even find this place -- this church?
Zh: “你到底是怎么找到这个地方的——这座教堂？”

En: Hard to say. I think I did some construction work here, back when I still had material worries. Up there, I realized what the true purpose of the church was...
Zh: “很难说。在我还有物质烦恼的时候，我好像是在这里做过一些建筑工作。在那里，我意识到教堂存在的真正目的是什么……”

En: Been spending a lot of time here ever since. The past is nothing to me now, wey. It didn't belong to me.
Zh: “从那之后，我就尽可能地多花时间待在这里。现在过去对我来说毫无意义。它不属于我。”

En: Hey, are you a boiadeiro?
Zh: “喂，你是牛仔吗？”

En: A boiadeiro? No, homes. I don't go in for that kinda thing. It's all about surrendering to the Mother now.
Zh: “牛仔？不是的，兄弟。我不参与那种事情。现在一切都是为了诚服于母亲。”

En: What were you before you became a crab-man and a woodcarver?\n
Zh: “你成为蟹男和木雕家之前是干什么的？”

En: What were you before you became a crab-man?
Zh: “你成为蟹男之前是干什么的？”

En: To be fair, it's really more like a spider. But everyone calls you the crab-man.
Zh: “说实在的，真的更像蜘蛛一些。不过大家都叫你蟹男。”

En: Time to run back! Or maybe walk, this *is* a sacred place after all...
Zh: 该跑回去了！或者也许可以用走的，毕竟这*是*个神圣的地方……

En: *And* your leg still hurts from the tribunal.
Zh: *而且*你那条在宣判中受伤的腿还疼着呢。

En: It's awfully silent again, as if someone turned off the entire world outside those walls. Water inside the bowls stands still.
Zh: 可怕的沉默再次降临，就好像有人把墙壁外面的整个世界都关掉了。水盆里的水没有一丝涟漪。

En: Move the fourth bowl from the left five centimetres to the right.
Zh: 把第4个水盆从左向右移动5厘米。

En: [Leave.]
Zh: [离开。]

En: Move the third bowl from the left two centimetres away.
Zh: 把第3个水盆向左移动2厘米。

En: It moves like a ghost without creating a single trace of sound.
Zh: 它像幽灵一样移动着，但却没有发出一丝声音。

En: Some water spills out of the bowl, wetting the floor.
Zh: 有些水从盆里洒了出来，打湿了地板。

En: Oh boy, this is going to be good.
Zh: 天啦，这个应该超级好玩的。

En: The lead programmer sends you an encouraging thumbs up from across the hall.
Zh: 主程序员在大厅对面朝你竖起拇指，以示鼓励。

En: This task is an insult to your mental skills.
Zh: 这个任务是对你心智技能的一种侮辱。

En: Measurements have been marked down around the bowls, each chalk-drawn line representing a centimetre on the floor.
Zh: 水盆的周围已经标好了尺寸，地板上每一条粉笔线代表一厘米。
